tech_0053_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.technology_status_summary_zh: 氨生產過程中產生的二氧化碳，可透過不同比例與方式進行捕捉。此配置中，製氫過程的濃縮流與稀釋流皆會被捕捉，捕捉率高（超過90%的製程排放），濃縮流的捕捉通常採用物理吸收法等方式進行。
tech_0053_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 將二氧化碳捕捉技術應用於氨生產可帶來減排機會，物理吸收法已商業應用且成本通常較低；化學吸收法則可捕捉更高比例的廠區排放，包括來自較小型製程單元的排放。
tech_0053_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.technology_status_summary_en: CO2 produced during the ammonia production process may be captured at different rates and by different means. In this configuration, both concentrated and diluted streams from the hydrogen production process are captured, resulting in high capture rates (> 90% of process emissions). Capture on concentrated streams is typically carried out by physical absorption (using liquid solvents like Selexol or Rectisol to absorb CO2 from high-pressure gas streams without a chemical reaction), physical adsorption (capturing CO2 on the surface of the adsorbents, with desorption achieved through pressure swing adsorption, vacuum swing adsorption, or a hybrid configuration called vacuum pressure swing adsorption), or cryogenic capture (refrigeration-based system of separating CO2). Capture on more diluted streams typically requires other forms of capture including chemical absorption (CO2 reacting with a chemical solvent such as amines, and is subsequently released at 120°C - 150°C during solvent regeneration), or using auto-thermal reforming technology.
tech_0053_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.market_dynamics_summary_en: Applying CO2 capture technologies to ammonia production offers an opportunity to reduce emissions, with physical absorption already commercially applied and generally less costly. Chemical absorption could enable capture of a higher proportion of plant emissions, including from smaller process units, though its deployment may be less crucial where physical absorption is already used. Physical adsorption and cryogenic capture are currently less technically suited or less mature for ammonia production.
tech_0053_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.description: CO2 produced during the ammonia production process may be captured at different rates and by different means. In this configuration, both concentrated and diluted streams from the hydrogen production process are captured, resulting in high capture rates (> 90% of process emissions). Capture on concentrated streams is typically carried out by physical absorption (using liquid solvents like Selexol or Rectisol to absorb CO2 from high-pressure gas streams without a chemical reaction), physical adsorption (capturing CO2 on the surface of the adsorbents, with desorption achieved through pressure swing adsorption, vacuum swing adsorption, or a hybrid configuration called vacuum pressure swing adsorption), or cryogenic capture (refrigeration-based system of separating CO2). Capture on more diluted streams typically requires other forms of capture including chemical absorption (CO2 reacting with a chemical solvent such as amines, and is subsequently released at 120°C - 150°C during solvent regeneration), or using auto-thermal reforming technology.
tech_0053_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.NZErationale: Applying CO2 capture technologies to ammonia production offers an opportunity to reduce emissions, with physical absorption already commercially applied and generally less costly. Chemical absorption could enable capture of a higher proportion of plant emissions, including from smaller process units, though its deployment may be less crucial where physical absorption is already used. Physical adsorption and cryogenic capture are currently less technically suited or less mature for ammonia production.

initiatives.description: May 2022 Nutrien is evaluating its Geismar, Louisiana, ammonia facilities as a potential site for significant new ammonia production to serve growing demand in agriculture, industrial and emerging energy markets. Nutrien is progressing the proposed project to the front-end engineering design phase, with a final investment decision expected to follow in 2023 and, if approved, full production by 2027. The new plant is expected to have annual production capacity of 1.2 million metric tons of clean ammonia, with approximately 1.8 million metric tons per year of CO2 to be captured

In 2025, a final investment decision was reached on the Blue Point Number One ammonia complex, in the United States, a new auto-thermal reforming plant with CCUS targing the production of 1.4 Mt per year of low-emissions ammonia, and the capture of 2.3 Mt CO2 per year.

In 2023, a final investment decision was reached by Woodside energy to build a new auto-thermal reforming plant with CCUS for the 1.1 Mt per year Beaumont New Ammonia plant in Texas, United States. The plant targets the capture and sequestration of 0.5 Mt CO2 per year as early of 2026.

tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.technology_name: Haber-bosch process using hydrogen produced with partial CCUS
tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.technology_name_zh: 搭配部分CCUS製氫的哈伯-博施製程
tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.technology_status_summary_zh: 氨生產過程中產生的二氧化碳，可透過不同比例與方式進行捕捉。此配置僅捕捉製氫過程中的濃縮排放，屬於部分捕捉，已廣泛商業應用於以SMR為基礎的製氫，捕捉濃縮二氧化碳作為利用，通常用於尿素生產或透過強化採油封存。
tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 將二氧化碳捕捉技術應用於氨生產可帶來減排機會，物理吸收法已商業應用且成本通常較低；化學吸收法則可捕捉更高比例的廠區排放，包括來自較小型製程單元的排放。
tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.technology_status_summary_en: CO2 produced during ammonia production may be captured at different rates and by different means. In this configuration, only concentrated emissions from the hydrogen production process are captured, resulting in partial capture. This process is widely deployed commercially in SMR-based hydrogen production, capturing concentrated CO2 for utilisation, often for urea or storing captured CO2 via enhanced oil recovery (TRL 9C). Capture on concentrated streams is typically carried out by physical absorption (using liquid solvents like Selexol or Rectisol to absorb CO2 from high-pressure gas streams without a chemical reaction), or physical adsorption (capturing CO2 on the surface of the adsorbents, with desorption achieved through pressure swing adsorption, vacuum swing adsorption, or a hybrid configuration called vacuum pressure swing adsorption).
tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.market_dynamics_summary_en: Applying CO2 capture technologies to ammonia production offers an opportunity to reduce emissions, with physical absorption already commercially applied and generally less costly. Chemical absorption could enable capture of a higher proportion of plant emissions, including from smaller process units, though its deployment may be less crucial where physical absorption is already used. Physical adsorption and cryogenic capture are currently less technically suited or less mature for ammonia production.
tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.description: CO2 produced during ammonia production may be captured at different rates and by different means. In this configuration, only concentrated emissions from the hydrogen production process are captured, resulting in partial capture. This process is widely deployed commercially in SMR-based hydrogen production, capturing concentrated CO2 for utilisation, often for urea or storing captured CO2 via enhanced oil recovery (TRL 9C). Capture on concentrated streams is typically carried out by physical absorption (using liquid solvents like Selexol or Rectisol to absorb CO2 from high-pressure gas streams without a chemical reaction), or physical adsorption (capturing CO2 on the surface of the adsorbents, with desorption achieved through pressure swing adsorption, vacuum swing adsorption, or a hybrid configuration called vacuum pressure swing adsorption).
tech_0054_haber-bosch-process-using-hydr.NZErationale: Applying CO2 capture technologies to ammonia production offers an opportunity to reduce emissions, with physical absorption already commercially applied and generally less costly. Chemical absorption could enable capture of a higher proportion of plant emissions, including from smaller process units, though its deployment may be less crucial where physical absorption is already used. Physical adsorption and cryogenic capture are currently less technically suited or less mature for ammonia production.

initiatives.description: Nutrien Redwater ammonia plant has been sending excess CO2 for enhanced oil recovery through the Alberta Carbon Trunkline since 2020. In 2023, they expanded the CO2 capture and export capacity to 300 kt per year.
Coffeyville Resources Nitrogen Fertilizers has been operational since 2013 and captures 1 Mt CO2 per year.

Samsung E&A has started construction on the Wabash low-carbon project in the US, targeting completion for 2029. The project is designed to produce 500 thousand tonnes of low-carbon ammonia per year, capturing 1.67 million tonnes of CO2

tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.technology_name: Lignin-based (biomass) benzene, toluene and xylenes production
tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.technology_name_zh: 木質素基（生質）苯、甲苯與二甲苯生產
tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.technology_status_summary_zh: BTX芳香烴可透過多種不同路徑由木質素生產，包括去氧木質素裂解、觸媒轉化、由糖類生產（透過狄爾斯-阿爾德反應），或水解加薄膜過濾/滲透氣化製程。
tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此生產路徑可避免芳香烴生產直接使用化石燃料。然而永續生質原料的供應量可能限制此製程路徑的潛力上限，且製程資本與能源密集度高，使用生質原料使其成本高於CCS等競爭方案。
tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.technology_status_summary_en: BTX aromatics can be produced from lignin via several different routes, including cracking of de-oxygenated lignin, catalytic conversion, production from sugars (by the Diels-Alder reaction), or a hydrolysis plus nanofiltration or pervaporation process.
tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route would avoid the direct use of fossil fuels in aromatics production. However, availability of sustainable biomass may put an upper limit on the potential for this process route.  It is also capital and energy intensive, and the use of biomass - a high-cost commodity - makes it more expensive than competing options (such as CCS).
tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.description: BTX aromatics can be produced from lignin via several different routes, including cracking of de-oxygenated lignin, catalytic conversion, production from sugars (by the Diels-Alder reaction), or a hydrolysis plus nanofiltration or pervaporation process.
tech_0055_lignin-based-biomass-benzene-t.NZErationale: This production route would avoid the direct use of fossil fuels in aromatics production. However, availability of sustainable biomass may put an upper limit on the potential for this process route.  It is also capital and energy intensive, and the use of biomass - a high-cost commodity - makes it more expensive than competing options (such as CCS).

initiatives.description: Since 2018, the Dutch company BioBTX has proven at the pilot scale the feasibility of its technology, called Integrated Cascading Catalytic Pyrolysis (ICCP), to form aromatics from non-food biomass.

tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.technology_name: Methanol-based benzene, toluene and xylenes production
tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.technology_name_zh: 甲醇基苯、甲苯與二甲苯生產
tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.technology_status_summary_zh: 苯、甲苯與二甲苯（BTX）等芳香烴可透過觸媒轉化製程由甲醇生產。
tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若以低排放甲醇為基礎，此生產路徑可為傳統石腦油蒸汽裂解製程開闢新的替代化石原料途徑。然而每噸芳香烴需要大量甲醇，成本較高，且煉油廠與蒸汽裂解仍能提供相當數量的芳香烴。
tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.technology_status_summary_en: Aromatics such as benzene, toluene and xylenes (BTX) can be produced from methanol via a catalytic conversion process.
tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.market_dynamics_summary_en: If building on low-emission methanol, this process route would open a new avenue to displace fossil feedstock for aromatics production in conventional naphtha steam crackers. However, it requires a large amount of methanol per tonne of aromatics, which makes it expensive, and quantities of aromatics are still available from refineries and steam crackers so there is not a pressing need for an alternative source.
tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.description: Aromatics such as benzene, toluene and xylenes (BTX) can be produced from methanol via a catalytic conversion process.
tech_0056_methanol-based-benzene-toluene.NZErationale: If building on low-emission methanol, this process route would open a new avenue to displace fossil feedstock for aromatics production in conventional naphtha steam crackers. However, it requires a large amount of methanol per tonne of aromatics, which makes it expensive, and quantities of aromatics are still available from refineries and steam crackers so there is not a pressing need for an alternative source.

initiatives.description: Three pilot plants were developed in 2013, and commercial scale demonstration projects are under development. Actors involved include Mobil, Sinopec Engineering Group, Zhejiang University, and Tschinghua University, with a particular focus on this technology from China.
In 2017 a methanol to aromatics pilot plant was developed by ICC and CAS at a scaler of 100 t/year, and was operated for over 500 hours.

tech_0057_ethylene-production-using-ferm.technology_status_summary_zh: 乙烯可透過脫水製程由生質乙醇生產，生質乙醇可由含糖生質原料（如甘蔗）經發酵，或由澱粉類生質原料（如玉米）經水解再發酵（第一代生質燃料生產技術）製得。
tech_0057_ethylene-production-using-ferm.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此生產路徑可避免現行以化石原料為基礎、廣泛應用的蒸汽裂解製程直接使用化石燃料生產乙烯，目前已商業應用，且採用成本較低、加工熱耗較低的生質原料。
tech_0057_ethylene-production-using-ferm.technology_status_summary_en: Ethylene (C2H4) can be produced from bio-ethanol (C2H6O) via dehydration processes. The bio-ethanol could be produced from sugary biomass (e.g. sugarcane) by fermentation or from starchy biomass (e.g. corn) by hydrolysis followed by fermentation (first generation biofuel production technologies).
tech_0057_ethylene-production-using-ferm.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route would avoid the direct use of fossil fuels in ethylene production from the widely established fossil-feedstock based steam crackers. It is already used commercially today, and makes use of lower cost biomass that is less thermally intensive to process compared to biomass that would be required to produce other chemicals like ammonia and methanol. However, this technology may face difficulty in competing with alternatives, given limited availability of sustainable biomass and competition with other end uses.
tech_0057_ethylene-production-using-ferm.description: Ethylene (C2H4) can be produced from bio-ethanol (C2H6O) via dehydration processes. The bio-ethanol could be produced from sugary biomass (e.g. sugarcane) by fermentation or from starchy biomass (e.g. corn) by hydrolysis followed by fermentation (first generation biofuel production technologies).
tech_0057_ethylene-production-using-ferm.NZErationale: This production route would avoid the direct use of fossil fuels in ethylene production from the widely established fossil-feedstock based steam crackers. It is already used commercially today, and makes use of lower cost biomass that is less thermally intensive to process compared to biomass that would be required to produce other chemicals like ammonia and methanol. However, this technology may face difficulty in competing with alternatives, given limited availability of sustainable biomass and competition with other end uses.

initiatives.description: Several commercial plants are currently in operation in multiple countries, two of the largest being the Braskem plant (0.2 Mt/yr) which opened in Brazil in 2010 and produces ethylene from sugarcane based feedstocks, and the India Glycols plant (0.175 Mt/yr) in India which opened in 1989 and uses molasses as a feedstock.

In 2014, Axens, Total and IFP Energies Nouvelles announced for commercial sale a technology for ethylene production through dehydration of bioethanol under the technology brand name Atol™ to produce of polymer grade bio-ethylene. However, most of the capacity under construction is directed to non-polymer ethylene derivatives, such as ethylene oxide, which could later be used for producing polymers. The technology can use either first or second-generation bioethanol.
BP's Hummingbird technology for conversion of ethanol to ethylene was commercially released in 2013.
Applications so far are using bioethanol produced from fermentation (first generation), as far as can be deduced.

tech_0058_ethylene-production-using-lign.technology_name_zh: 木質纖維素氣化法生產乙烯
tech_0058_ethylene-production-using-lign.technology_status_summary_zh: 乙烯可透過脫水製程由生質乙醇生產，生質乙醇可由木質纖維素生質原料（如木本作物、農業殘餘物）經氣化產生合成氣，再經發酵或觸媒轉化製成乙醇。
tech_0058_ethylene-production-using-lign.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此生產路徑可避免傳統以化石原料為基礎的蒸汽裂解製程直接使用化石燃料生產乙烯。將木質纖維素生質原料轉化為生質乙醇的技術仍需進一步發展並降低成本，才能成為可行選項，但潛力可觀。
tech_0058_ethylene-production-using-lign.technology_status_summary_en: Ethylene (C2H4) can be produced from bio-ethanol (C2H6O) via dehydration processes. The bio-ethanol could be produced from lignocellulosic biomass (e.g. woody crops, agricultural residues) through gasification to produce a syngas and subsequent conversion into ethanol by fermentation or catalytic conversion.
tech_0058_ethylene-production-using-lign.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route would avoid the direct use of fossil fuels in ethylene production from the widely established fossil-feedstock based steam crackers. Further development of technologies and cost reductions for converting lignocellulosic biomass to bioethanol would be needed to make this route an option for bioethylene production, but would have the advantage of more readily available biomass feedstock. This technology may face difficulty in competing with alternatives, given limited availability of sustainable biomass and competition with other end uses.
tech_0058_ethylene-production-using-lign.description: Ethylene (C2H4) can be produced from bio-ethanol (C2H6O) via dehydration processes. The bio-ethanol could be produced from lignocellulosic biomass (e.g. woody crops, agricultural residues) through gasification to produce a syngas and subsequent conversion into ethanol by fermentation or catalytic conversion.
tech_0058_ethylene-production-using-lign.NZErationale: This production route would avoid the direct use of fossil fuels in ethylene production from the widely established fossil-feedstock based steam crackers. Further development of technologies and cost reductions for converting lignocellulosic biomass to bioethanol would be needed to make this route an option for bioethylene production, but would have the advantage of more readily available biomass feedstock. This technology may face difficulty in competing with alternatives, given limited availability of sustainable biomass and competition with other end uses.

initiatives.description: The process of converting bioethanol to ethylene would be the same for bioethanol produced from fermentation (first generation bioethanol) or lignocellulosic gasification (second generation bioethanol), and thus this aspect of the technology is already commercially available. However, bioethanol produced through lignocellulosic gasification is currently less advanced and more costly, and as such has not yet been linked to bioethylene production.

tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name: High value chemicals production using chemical absorption
tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name_zh: 化學吸收法生產高值化學品
tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_zh: 二氧化碳化學吸收是常見的製程操作，原理是二氧化碳與化學溶劑（如胺類）反應，二氧化碳通常在攝氏120至150度釋出，溶劑再生後可繼續使用。
tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CCS在以合理成本降低高值化學品生產排放方面具有良好潛力，是高值化學品減排的主要競爭技術之一。
tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_en: Chemical absorption of CO2 is a common process operation based on the reaction between CO2 and a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at temperatures typically in the range 120 °C - 150 °C and the solvent is regenerated for further operation.
tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_en: CCS has good potential to reduce emissions from high value chemicals (HVCs) production at a reasonable cost. It is a key competitor for emission reductions for HVCs.
tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.description: Chemical absorption of CO2 is a common process operation based on the reaction between CO2 and a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at temperatures typically in the range 120 °C - 150 °C and the solvent is regenerated for further operation.
tech_0059_high-value-chemicals-productio.NZErationale: CCS has good potential to reduce emissions from high value chemicals (HVCs) production at a reasonable cost. It is a key competitor for emission reductions for HVCs.

tech_0060_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name: High value chemicals production using gas fermentation-to-ethanol-to-ethylene
tech_0060_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name_zh: 氣體發酵轉乙醇再轉乙烯法生產高值化學品
tech_0060_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程從乙烯蒸汽裂解裝置捕捉二氧化碳，將其轉化為乙醇，再轉化為乙烯。
tech_0060_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_en: In this process, CO2 is captured from ethylene steam craker and converted to ethanol, and then converted into ethylene.
tech_0060_high-value-chemicals-productio.description: In this process, CO2 is captured from ethylene steam craker and converted to ethanol, and then converted into ethylene.

initiatives.description: In 2023, LanzaTech partnered with Technip Energies to combine its biorecycling carbon capture and utilisation technology with Technip's Hummingbird ethanol-to-ethylene technology. They plan to apply this CO2-to-ethylene technology at an ethylene steam cracker facility in the US as part of the SECURE project.
The SECURE project, a collaboration between Technip Energies and LanzaTech, received USD 200 million from US DOE and the authorisation to initiate the phase 1 of the project.

tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name: High value chemicals production using naphtha catalytic cracking
tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name_zh: 石腦油觸媒裂解法生產高值化學品
tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_zh: 流體觸媒裂解（FCC）是丙烯的第二大來源，基本上是煉油廠生產汽油的副產品。雖然FCC裝置通常以重質碳氫化合物為進料，但改用石腦油作為進料可提高製程產率，並增進烯烴組成的控制能力。
tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術有助於提升化學品生產效率，但無法達到接近零排放。
tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_en: Fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) is the second largest source of propylene, which is essentially a by-product of refinery gasoline production. While the usual feed to an FCC unit is heavy hydrocarbons, the use of naphtha as feedstock improves the yield of the process and increases the capability to control the composition of olefins.
tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can help improve the efficiency of chemicals production. However, it does not lead to near-zero emissions.
tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.description: Fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) is the second largest source of propylene, which is essentially a by-product of refinery gasoline production. While the usual feed to an FCC unit is heavy hydrocarbons, the use of naphtha as feedstock improves the yield of the process and increases the capability to control the composition of olefins.
tech_0061_high-value-chemicals-productio.NZErationale: This technology can help improve the efficiency of chemicals production. However, it does not lead to near-zero emissions.

initiatives.description: SK Energy, in partnership with KBR, has developed a process called Advanced Catalytic Olefins® (ACO). A first commercial plant (40 kt/yr) is in operation at KBR’s plant in Ulshan, Korea.

tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name: High value chemicals production using physical absorption
tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name_zh: 物理吸收法生產高值化學品
tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_zh: 物理吸收法利用液態溶劑，在不發生化學反應的情況下，從二氧化碳分壓高的煙道氣中吸收二氧化碳，常見的物理溶劑包括Selexol與Rectisol。
tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_zh: CCS在以合理成本降低高值化學品生產排放方面具有良好潛力，是高值化學品減排的主要競爭技術之一。
tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_en: Physical absorption uses a liquid solvent to absorb CO2 from flue gases that have high CO2 partial pressures, without a chemical reaction occurring. Common physical solvents include Selexol (dimethyl ethers of polyethylene glycol) and Rectisol (methanol).
tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_en: CCS has good potential to reduce emissions from high value chemicals (HVCs) production at a reasonable cost. It is a key competitor for emission reductions for HVCs.
tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.description: Physical absorption uses a liquid solvent to absorb CO2 from flue gases that have high CO2 partial pressures, without a chemical reaction occurring. Common physical solvents include Selexol (dimethyl ethers of polyethylene glycol) and Rectisol (methanol).
tech_0062_high-value-chemicals-productio.NZErationale: CCS has good potential to reduce emissions from high value chemicals (HVCs) production at a reasonable cost. It is a key competitor for emission reductions for HVCs.

tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name: High value chemicals production using steam cracker electrification
tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_name_zh: 蒸汽裂解電氣化法生產高值化學品
tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_zh: 蒸汽裂解是將長鏈碳氫化合物裂解為較簡單化合物的製程，例如將石腦油裂解為烯烴與芳香烴以供後續加工。由於高溫需求，蒸汽裂解目前以化石燃料燃燒供熱，但目前正在探索以電力運轉蒸汽裂解裝置。
tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術的可行性部分取決於電價，而在低碳世界中電力需求可能大幅上升。不過此選項目前正受到大型生產商積極投入研發。
tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.technology_status_summary_en: Steam cracking is a process in which long-chain hydrocarbons are broken into simpler ones, for example splitting naphtha into olefins and aromatics for further processing. Due to high temperature requirements, steam crackers are currently fossil fuel-fired. However, exploration is underway to run steam crackers on electricity.
tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.market_dynamics_summary_en: The viability of this technology is partly tied to the price of electricity, which will likely be in high demand in a low carbon world. However, this option is being pursued heavily by large scale producers.
tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.description: Steam cracking is a process in which long-chain hydrocarbons are broken into simpler ones, for example splitting naphtha into olefins and aromatics for further processing. Due to high temperature requirements, steam crackers are currently fossil fuel-fired. However, exploration is underway to run steam crackers on electricity.
tech_0063_high-value-chemicals-productio.NZErationale: The viability of this technology is partly tied to the price of electricity, which will likely be in high demand in a low carbon world. However, this option is being pursued heavily by large scale producers.

initiatives.description: VoltaChem is also working on the Electric Cracking option to make the chemicals production more sustainable, in the framework of the Power-2-Heat technologies. The project is looking at conceptual design of an experimental facility.
Dow and Shell announced in mid 2020 they will work together to develop an electric cracking technology, starting with lab and pilot operations to prove the technology.

Finnish startup Coolbrook, in partnership with Cambridge University, is developing Roto Dynamic Reactor technology, which runs on electricity, to power a steam cracker. They are building a pilot plant in Geleen, the Netherlands, with 400 kg/h of capacity, aiming to start up in the first half of Testing began in late 2022, with a broader commercial launch planned for 2025. This achieved positive results in 2024, with successful naptha cracking

The first demonstration plant for electrically heated steam cracking furnaces started up in 2024. This 6 MW demonstrator was developed by a consortium of BASF, SABIC and Linde (Cracker of the Future) and is installed at a BASF site in Germany. It has two furnaces, both using direct electric heating and testing slightly different heating elements. It will be testing two different electric heating approaches and will produce 4 t/h of hydrocarbon.

tech_0064_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_name: Recycling via solvent dissolution for PP
tech_0064_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_status_summary_zh: 利用溶劑分離出聚合物，再以液態進行純化（類似淨化飲用水的方式）。此過程並非將聚合物解聚，而是透過純化去除塑膠廢料中的顏色、氣味與污染物，以萃取出原生品質的聚合物。
tech_0064_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低對原生生產的需求，並減少材料被降級回收成低價值產品的情形。然而仍須克服行為障礙以提高塑膠回收率，且二次生產不太可能完全取代原生生產的需求。
tech_0064_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_status_summary_en: Solvent is used to separate out the polymer, which is then purified in a liquid state (similar to purifying drinking water). The polymer is not depolymerised (broken into its component monomers), but rather purification removes colour, odour and contaminants from plastic waste in order to extract a virgin-quality polymer.
tech_0064_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0064_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.description: Solvent is used to separate out the polymer, which is then purified in a liquid state (similar to purifying drinking water). The polymer is not depolymerised (broken into its component monomers), but rather purification removes colour, odour and contaminants from plastic waste in order to extract a virgin-quality polymer.
tech_0064_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: PureCycle has developed a process to recycle polypropylene (PP) plastic into virgin-quality plastic. Its first commercial-scale plant is under construction in Ohio, United States and was scheduled to be online towards the end of 2022. However, after a number of delays this has been pushed back into 2023.

tech_0065_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.technology_name: Recycling via chemical depolymerisation for PET
tech_0065_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.technology_status_summary_zh: 此化學回收技術利用化學藥劑將聚合物分解為單體，不同技術所需的熱量與壓力各異，但整體而言所需熱量遠低於熱裂解。
tech_0065_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低對原生生產的需求，並減少材料被降級回收成低價值產品的情形。然而仍須克服行為障礙以提高塑膠回收率，且二次生產不太可能完全取代原生生產的需求。
tech_0065_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.technology_status_summary_en: This chemical recycling technology uses chemicals to break down polymers into monomers. Techniques vary in the amount of heat and pressure used, but generally considerably less heat is used compared to pyrolysis.
tech_0065_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0065_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.description: This chemical recycling technology uses chemicals to break down polymers into monomers. Techniques vary in the amount of heat and pressure used, but generally considerably less heat is used compared to pyrolysis.
tech_0065_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: CARBIOS is constructing a PET biorecycling plant due for commission in 2027. It will use enzymatic depolymerisation of PET waste to generate monomers and aims a capacity of 50 kt of PET per year, including low recyclable ones like coloured bottles, food trays and textiles.

tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.technology_name: Recycling via chemical depolymerisation for polystyrene
tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.technology_name_zh: 化學解聚回收法（聚苯乙烯）
tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.technology_status_summary_zh: 此化學回收技術利用化學藥劑將聚合物分解為單體，不同技術所需的熱量與壓力各異，但整體而言所需熱量遠低於熱裂解。
tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低對原生生產的需求，並減少材料被降級回收成低價值產品的情形。然而仍須克服行為障礙以提高塑膠回收率，且二次生產不太可能完全取代原生生產的需求。
tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.technology_status_summary_en: This chemical recycling technology uses chemicals to break down polymers into monomers. Techniques vary in the amount of heat and pressure used, but in general, considerably less heat is used compared to pyrolysis.
tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.description: This chemical recycling technology uses chemicals to break down polymers into monomers. Techniques vary in the amount of heat and pressure used, but in general, considerably less heat is used compared to pyrolysis.
tech_0066_recycling-via-chemical-depolym.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: Agilyx Corporation and INEOS Styrolution have developed a recycling process based on depolymerization of polystyrene waste, which is now commercially available. The first plant opened in 2018 in Oregon in the United States. Several other plants are at different stages of development.
Another similar plant of 100 tons /day scale is being developed in Illinois, US.

tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程利用超臨界狀態的水裂解廢棄塑膠內的碳鍵，將聚合物分解為較短鏈的碳氫化合物。
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低對原生生產的需求，並減少材料被降級回收成低價值產品的情形。然而仍須克服行為障礙以提高塑膠回收率，且二次生產不太可能完全取代原生生產的需求。
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.technology_status_summary_en: This process uses water under supercritical conditions to crack carbon bonds within end-of-life plastic, thus breaking down polymers into shorter chain hydrocarbons.
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.description: This process uses water under supercritical conditions to crack carbon bonds within end-of-life plastic, thus breaking down polymers into shorter chain hydrocarbons.
tech_0067_recycling-via-hydrothermal-upg.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: Licella's Hydrothermal Reactor (Cat-HTR) technology has been proven at its large pilot plant in Somersby, Australia, going through 3 scale ups since a small pilot in 2020.. It converts mixed plastics waste into synthetic oil.
The UK-based company Mura is aiming to establish over 2 million tons capacity with Licella's Cat-HTR technology by 2030.

tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.technology_status_summary_zh: 熱裂解是在無氧狀態下於高溫分解材料的過程，可用於將混合塑膠廢料轉化為液態碳氫化合物，供石化業再利用。
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低對原生生產的需求，並減少材料被降級回收成低價值產品的情形。然而仍須克服行為障礙以提高塑膠回收率，且二次生產不太可能完全取代原生生產的需求。
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.technology_status_summary_en: Pyrolysis is the breakdown of material at high temperatures in the absence of oxygen. Pyrolysis can be used to convert mixed plastic waste into liquid hydrocarbons, which can be used again by the petrochemical industry.
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.description: Pyrolysis is the breakdown of material at high temperatures in the absence of oxygen. Pyrolysis can be used to convert mixed plastic waste into liquid hydrocarbons, which can be used again by the petrochemical industry.
tech_0068_pyrolysis-recycling.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: Versalis, a chemical company owned by Italy oil company Eni, launched in early 2020 the Hoop project, in which it planned to build a pilot scale plant (6 kt/yr) in Mantova using a pyrolysis technology developed by engineering company Servizi di Ricerche e Sviluppo. However, limited progress seems to have been made.

The Finish refining company Neste announced in 2019 partnerships with Germany waste management company Remondis and Belgian recycler Ravago in order to develop thermochemical recycling technologies. The project aims to process over 1Mt of waste plastics per year by 2030 onwards. In 2023, a range of companies including Neste applied pyrolysis in production of PEX pipes.

The Thermal Anaerobic Conversion (TAC) technology, which was developed by UK-based company Plastic Energy and converts end-of-life plastics to oil (called Tacoil), is in use at two commercial-scale plants in Spain (operational since 2014 and 2017). Plans are underway to develop another plant in Netherlands in cooperation with Saudi Arabian company SABIC, as well as a plant in Malaysia in partnership with Petronas.

Norwegian company Quantafuel has developed a technology that combines pyrolysis with catalysis to convert mixed plastic wastes to synthetic fuels. Its first commercial plant started operating in late 2019 in Denmark and will process 18 kt of plastic waste a year. The company has backing from companies such as major chemical producer BASF.

Dutch company Fuenix Ecogy Group has developed a pyrolysis-based recycling technology. In mid 2019, it entered into a partnership with the chemical company Dow to supply its pyrolysis oil feedstock to Dow's production facility in Terneuzen Netherlands.
OVM started their ReOil plant in 2025. It has the capacity to recycle 16 000 t of hard-to-recycle plastic per year.

tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_name: Recycling via solvent dissolution for PET
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_status_summary_zh: 利用溶劑分離出聚合物，再以液態進行純化（類似淨化飲用水的方式）。此過程並非將聚合物解聚，而是透過純化去除塑膠廢料中的顏色、氣味與污染物，以萃取出原生品質的聚合物。
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低對原生生產的需求，並減少材料被降級回收成低價值產品的情形。然而仍須克服行為障礙以提高塑膠回收率，且二次生產不太可能完全取代原生生產的需求。
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.technology_status_summary_en: Solvent is used to separate out the polymer, which is then purified in a liquid state (similar to purifying drinking water). The polymer is not depolymerised (broken into its component monomers), but rather purification removes colour, odour and contaminants from plastic waste in order to extract a virgin-quality polymer.
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.description: Solvent is used to separate out the polymer, which is then purified in a liquid state (similar to purifying drinking water). The polymer is not depolymerised (broken into its component monomers), but rather purification removes colour, odour and contaminants from plastic waste in order to extract a virgin-quality polymer.
tech_0069_recycling-via-solvent-dissolut.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: The British company Worn Again Technologies is developing a pilot R&D facility to validate and develop its proprietary process, which will separate, decontaminate and extract PET polymer from polyester textiles, bottles and packaging. The technology uses a solvent at an elevated temperature. It raised funds in 2022 to develop a large scale demonstration plant in Switzerland.

Loop Industries acquired land in Gujarat for the construction of plastic recycling facility. Initially, the plant will have the capacity to recycle 70 kt of waste per year and produce PET resin, with plans for a further 100 kt.

tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_name: Thermal decontamination in a vacuum reactor with integrated nitrogen flushing for PET
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_name_zh: 真空反應器整合氮氣沖洗熱去污法（PET）
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程透過反應器內的熔融與去污過程，由回收瓶片製成新的食品級瓶子。PET薄片在真空條件下去除水分，並以氮氣沖洗降低變色；加熱使聚合物結構打開，得以去除污染物與內部水分，再以離心方式造粒，經固態聚縮合後擠出成新塑膠。
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 改善回收可降低對原生生產的需求，並減少材料被降級回收成低價值產品的情形。然而仍須克服行為障礙以提高塑膠回收率，且二次生產不太可能完全取代原生生產的需求。
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.technology_status_summary_en: This process produces new food-grade bottles from used bottles through a melting and decontamination process in a reactor. Moisture is removed from PET flakes under vacuum conditions, and nitrogen flushing to reduce colouration. Heat causes the polymer structure to open, enabling removal of contaminants and internal moisture. Pellets are then formed in a centrifuge, then undergo solid state polycondensation, before being extruded into new plastic.
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.market_dynamics_summary_en: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.description: This process produces new food-grade bottles from used bottles through a melting and decontamination process in a reactor. Moisture is removed from PET flakes under vacuum conditions, and nitrogen flushing to reduce colouration. Heat causes the polymer structure to open, enabling removal of contaminants and internal moisture. Pellets are then formed in a centrifuge, then undergo solid state polycondensation, before being extruded into new plastic.
tech_0070_thermal-decontamination-in-a-v.NZErationale: Improved recycling would help reduce the need for virgin production and can reduce downcycling in which a material is recycled into a lower value end. However, behavioural barriers also need to be overcome to increase plastics collection rates, and secondary production is unlikely to ever fully replace the need for primary production.

initiatives.description: The company EREMA developed the Vacunite and Vacurema technologies, which can be used in combination. They are now commercially available. These have been used to produce many products such as PET trays.

tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_name: Methanol production using biomass and waste gasification
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_name_zh: 生質與廢棄物氣化法生產甲醇
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質原料可取代石油、天然氣與煤炭等化石燃料作為甲醇生產原料。此路徑將生質原料轉化為合成氣，再經調整並觸媒轉化為甲醇。
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此生產路徑可避免甲醇生產使用化石燃料。然而永續生質原料的供應量可能限制此路徑的規模與整體部署潛力。
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.technology_status_summary_en: Biomass can replace fossil fuels such as oil, natural gas and coal as a feedstock for methanol production. In this pathway, biomass is converted into synthesis gas (syngas), which is then conditioned and catalytically converted into methanol.
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route avoids the use of fossil fuels in methanol production. However, the availability of sustainable biomass may limit the scale and overall deployment potential of this pathway.
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.description: Biomass can replace fossil fuels such as oil, natural gas and coal as a feedstock for methanol production. In this pathway, biomass is converted into synthesis gas (syngas), which is then conditioned and catalytically converted into methanol.
tech_0071_methanol-production-using-biom.NZErationale: This production route avoids the use of fossil fuels in methanol production. However, the availability of sustainable biomass may limit the scale and overall deployment potential of this pathway.

initiatives.description: The Canadian company Enerkem operates a full-scale commercial facility in Edmonton as well as both an innovation centre and a pilot facility in Quebec. Since 2000, Enerkem has tested and validated a number of different feedstocks – from solid waste to dozens of other types of residues and its proprietary technology was scaled up from pilot to demonstration to commercial stage during a period of 10 years. The process converts the carbon contained in non-recyclable waste into a pure synthesis gas (syngas), which is then turned into biofuels and chemicals, using commercially available catalysts.
BioMCN produces biomethanol at the Chemical Park Delfzijl in Netherlands, with a capacity of 15kt of biomethanol.

A commercial scale biomethanol plant was announced in 2012 by VärmlandsMetanol AB in Hagfors, Sweden. In 2016 VärmlandsMetanol AB, together with ThyssenKrupp Industrial Solutions as technology supplier and engineering partner, announced that the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) and the Risk Assessment had been completed as required by the Municipal Planning and Building Act and the Swedish Environmental Act. The latest available information is from 2017 and suggests the project may still be pending permit approvals and funding in order to commence construction.
Vordingborg biofuels in Denmark are working towards a facility that produces 300kt/y of biomethanol, with the goal being an operational facility in 2024
Perpetual Next is developing two biomethanol production facilities: one in Estonia and one in the Netherlands. Both will have a production capacity of 220 kt per year.
Haffner Energy will supply OboCarbon with a 20 MW electrolyser for their 100 t/day biomethanol facility project in California.

tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.technology_name_zh: 二氧化碳直接合成甲醇
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術路徑透過二氧化碳與氫氣的直接觸媒加氫反應生產甲醇，兩者在單一製程步驟中反應形成甲醇，避免了合成氣的中間生產步驟，實現更為整合的轉化路徑。
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若二氧化碳來自永續生物源、直接空氣捕捉或難以避免的工業排放，且所用氫氣來自低排放來源，此生產路徑可降低目前甲醇生產相關的化石燃料排放。
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.technology_status_summary_en: This technology pathway produces methanol through the direct catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, using hydrogen. CO2 and hydrogen react in a single process step to form methanol, avoiding the intermediate production of synthesis gas and enabling a more integrated conversion route.
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.description: This technology pathway produces methanol through the direct catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, using hydrogen. CO2 and hydrogen react in a single process step to form methanol, avoiding the intermediate production of synthesis gas and enabling a more integrated conversion route.
tech_0072_direct-co2-to-methanol-synthes.NZErationale: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.

initiatives.description: The George Olah Renewable Methanol Plant was commissioned by Carbon Recycling International in 2012 in Iceland and designed for a 4kt/yr capacity with a EUR 7.1 million investment. There are plans for scaling up this plant to 40kt/yr.

In April 2021, Ningxia Baofeng Energy began operation of a hydrogen production facility partially powered by 200-MW solar power plant (100 MW electrolyser capacity) in Northwest China's Ningxia Hui autonomous region. The hydrogen replaces a portion of coal as a feedstock for producing methanol; however it is less than half of the total energy needs, and thus is not yet considered a full commercial-scale example of methanol production from variable renewables. The 16kt/yr of hydrogen production is enough to produce about 0.1 Mt methanol/yr.

CRI's first commercial plant (0.1 Mt methanol/yr) has started production in 2022 in Henan, China, though this used hydrogen from coke oven gas. They are also planning a commercial plant (0.1 Mt methanol/yr) in Finnfjord, Norway, expected to begin operation in 2024.

ABEL Energy conducted a feasibility study for a large-scale electrolysis-based hydrogen and methanol facility at the Bell Bay Advanced Manufacturing Zone in Tasmania, Australia. The 100 MW electrolysis plant would produce 60-70 kt of methanol per year, with production initially expected to begin in 2023. Development has been taken up again in 2022, with the start up date likely delayed to 2027.

Carbon Recycling International (CRI) is developing a commercial-scale e-methanol facility adjacent to the Finnfjord ferrosilicon plant in Troms and Finnmark county. The project will capture CO2 from the ferrosilicon plant’s flue gas and combine it with hydrogen produced via water electrolysis powered by renewable electricity, using CRI’s Emissions-to-Liquids (ETL) technology. The e-methanol produced will serve as a low-carbon fuel and chemical feedstock, while the integration with the ferrosilicon plant will enable the capture and reuse of more than half of its emissions—around 300 000 tonnes of CO2 per year.
CRI's second plant in China is operated by Jiangsu Sailboat. It produces 2.4 Mt per year using CO2 and hydrogen from nearby industries.
The largest plant using electrolytic hydrogen is European Energy’s Kassø Power-to-X facility in Denmark, which began operations in February 2025 with a capacity of 42 ktpa of synthetic methanol.

tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.technology_name_zh: 逆水煤氣反應法二氧化碳製甲醇
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.technology_status_summary_zh: 此路徑透過兩步驟製程由二氧化碳生產甲醇。第一步透過逆水煤氣反應將二氧化碳部分轉化為一氧化碳，第二步再將產生的合成氣（並補充額外氫氣）透過觸媒合成轉化為甲醇。
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若二氧化碳來自永續生物源、直接空氣捕捉或難以避免的工業排放，且所用氫氣來自低排放來源，此生產路徑可降低目前甲醇生產相關的化石燃料排放。
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.technology_status_summary_en: This pathway produces methanol from carbon dioxide through a two-step process. In the first step, CO2 is partially converted to carbon monoxide via the reverse water-gas shift reaction. In the second step, the resulting synthesis gas, adjusted with additional hydrogen, is converted into methanol through catalytic synthesis.
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.market_dynamics_summary_en: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture, or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.description: This pathway produces methanol from carbon dioxide through a two-step process. In the first step, CO2 is partially converted to carbon monoxide via the reverse water-gas shift reaction. In the second step, the resulting synthesis gas, adjusted with additional hydrogen, is converted into methanol through catalytic synthesis.
tech_0073_co2-to-methanol-via-reverse-wa.NZErationale: This production route could reduce fossil fuel–related emissions associated with today’s methanol production, provided that the CO2 comes from sustainable biogenic sources, direct air capture, or unavoidable industrial emissions, and that the hydrogen used is produced from low-emissions sources.

initiatives.description: Topsoe designed ModuLite, a methanol synthesis plant consuming CO2 and hydrogen. It is available in 4 sizes, ranging from 150 to 600 Mt per year.
The Leuna100 project is the first pilot plant to produce methanol using reverse water gas shift.

tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name: Methanol production with CO2 capture (low rates)
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉甲醇生產（低捕捉率）
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_zh: 在以天然氣蒸汽甲烷重組及煤炭氣化為基礎的甲醇生產中，化石二氧化碳排放同時來自合成氣生產與供應製程熱能的燃料燃燒。部分捕捉配置通常鎖定合成氣生產過程中產生的濃縮二氧化碳流，此類濃縮流的捕捉技術已相當成熟，並廣泛應用於其他化學製程。
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 部分二氧化碳捕捉可應用於現有的天然氣與煤炭基甲醇工廠，在不需全面重新設計生產製程的情況下降低化石二氧化碳排放，能帶來近期減排效益，可作為邁向更高捕捉率的過渡步驟。
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_en: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. Partial capture configurations typically target concentrated CO2 streams generated during syngas production, where some CO2 may be separated from the syngas feed to the methanol synthesis process. Capture from such concentrated streams is technically mature and widely deployed in other chemical production processes, including urea manufacturing.
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_en: Partial CO2 capture can be applied to existing natural gas and coal-based methanol facilities, enabling a reduction in fossil CO2 emissions without requiring a full redesign of the production process. While this approach does not address all emission sources, it can deliver near-term emission reductions and serve as a transitional step towards higher capture rates or low-emissions methanol production pathways.
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.description: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. Partial capture configurations typically target concentrated CO2 streams generated during syngas production, where some CO2 may be separated from the syngas feed to the methanol synthesis process. Capture from such concentrated streams is technically mature and widely deployed in other chemical production processes, including urea manufacturing.
tech_0074_methanol-production-with-co2-c.NZErationale: Partial CO2 capture can be applied to existing natural gas and coal-based methanol facilities, enabling a reduction in fossil CO2 emissions without requiring a full redesign of the production process. While this approach does not address all emission sources, it can deliver near-term emission reductions and serve as a transitional step towards higher capture rates or low-emissions methanol production pathways.

initiatives.description: A QPC Quimica methanol plant in Brazil has been capturing CO2 since 1997 using amine-base capture. Food-grade CO2 is supplied to local soft drink manufacturers.

At a methanol plant in Bahrain, owned by Gulf Petrochemicals Industries Company, a CO2 capture project began in 2007, using the Mitsubishi KS-1 amine-based solvent. The CO2 is used to enhance methanol and urea production.

Company Xinjiang Dunhua has been capturing around 100000 tonnes CO2 per year from a methanol plant in Karamay, Xinjiang (China) using pressure swing adsorption (PSA) since 2015, using the CO2 for enhanced oil recovery in a nearby oilfield.

tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name: Methanol production with CO2 capture (high rates)
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_name_zh: 二氧化碳捕捉甲醇生產（高捕捉率）
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_zh: 在以天然氣蒸汽甲烷重組及煤炭氣化為基礎的甲醇生產中，化石二氧化碳排放同時來自甲醇合成上游的合成氣生產，以及供應製程熱能的燃料燃燒。在高捕捉率配置中，會從濃縮製程流與燃燒廢氣中捕捉部分二氧化碳，整體捕捉率通常可超過90%。
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 高捕捉率配置可應用於新建或現有的天然氣與煤炭基甲醇工廠，透過捕捉製程相關與燃燒排放來降低現場化石二氧化碳排放，雖無法避免燃料供應相關排放，但能大幅降低生產的碳強度。
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.technology_status_summary_en: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production upstream of methanol synthesis and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. In high-capture configurations, some CO2 is captured from concentrated process streams generated during syngas production and from combustion exhaust gases from furnaces supplying process heat, enabling overall capture rates typically above 90% of emissions. Capture from concentrated streams can rely on commercially mature technologies widely applied in chemical production, including physical absorption, physical adsorption and cryogenic separation. Achieving high capture rates additionally requires capture from dilute combustion exhaust streams, most commonly using chemical absorption systems or integrated reforming configurations. Compared with partial capture options, this pathway enables substantially larger emissions reductions, but implies higher energy use and costs.
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.market_dynamics_summary_en: High CO2 capture rate configurations can be applied to new or existing natural gas and coal-based methanol plants to reduce on-site fossil CO2 emissions by capturing process-related and combustion emissions. Although emissions associated with the supply of fossil fuels are not prevented, this approach can significantly reduce the carbon intensity of producing methanol using fossil fuels.
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.description: In natural gas steam methane reforming (SMR) and coal gasification–based methanol production, fossil CO2 emissions arise both from syngas production upstream of methanol synthesis and from fuel combustion used to supply process heat. In high-capture configurations, some CO2 is captured from concentrated process streams generated during syngas production and from combustion exhaust gases from furnaces supplying process heat, enabling overall capture rates typically above 90% of emissions. Capture from concentrated streams can rely on commercially mature technologies widely applied in chemical production, including physical absorption, physical adsorption and cryogenic separation. Achieving high capture rates additionally requires capture from dilute combustion exhaust streams, most commonly using chemical absorption systems or integrated reforming configurations. Compared with partial capture options, this pathway enables substantially larger emissions reductions, but implies higher energy use and costs.
tech_0075_methanol-production-with-co2-c.NZErationale: High CO2 capture rate configurations can be applied to new or existing natural gas and coal-based methanol plants to reduce on-site fossil CO2 emissions by capturing process-related and combustion emissions. Although emissions associated with the supply of fossil fuels are not prevented, this approach can significantly reduce the carbon intensity of producing methanol using fossil fuels.

initiatives.description: Lake Charles Methanol is developing an industrial scale (3.6 Mt/yr) methanol plant with 90% capture using auto-thermal reforming, capturing around 1 Mt CO2 per year and storing it in a saline formation. In 2025 the project awarded a FEED study, and targets FID in 2026.
Sandpiper Chemicals are planning a new blue methanol plant in Texas City, using Topsoe SynCOR technology and CCS. Production is expected to begin in 2030 with an output of 3000 t per day.

tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_name: Electric arc and plasma arc furnaces (high temperature heating)
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_name_zh: 電弧與電漿弧爐（高溫加熱）
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_status_summary_zh: 電弧爐已廣泛用於二次鋼鐵生產，電熔爐則用於玻璃生產。電漿弧爐是一種特殊型式的電弧爐，透過將強力電流通過氬氣等特定氣體，可產生高達攝氏5000度的熱能，目前主要應用於有害廢棄物焚化及部分金屬（如鈦、鎢）加工。
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可取代多種製程中的燃料燃燒爐或熔爐，降低現場排放。使用再生能源可實現無二氧化碳排放的加熱製程，在電氣化高溫熱製程方面潛力可觀，不過對於部分關鍵新應用而言仍處於相對早期的發展階段。
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.technology_status_summary_en: Electric arc furnaces are already commonly used in secondary steel production, while electric glass melting furnaces are used in glass production. Plasma arc furnaces are a special type of electric arc furnace that can produce heat as high as 5000 degrees Celsius, by passing a powerful electric current through particular gases such as argon. These furnaces are used today in some applications, mainly hazardous waste incineration and processing some metals (ex. titanium, tungsten). The technology offers the possibility to be adapted to other high temperature heat processes that are currently difficult to electrify.
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential to electrify high temperature heat processes, although it remains at relatively early stages of development for key new applications.
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.description: Electric arc furnaces are already commonly used in secondary steel production, while electric glass melting furnaces are used in glass production. Plasma arc furnaces are a special type of electric arc furnace that can produce heat as high as 5000 degrees Celsius, by passing a powerful electric current through particular gases such as argon. These furnaces are used today in some applications, mainly hazardous waste incineration and processing some metals (ex. titanium, tungsten). The technology offers the possibility to be adapted to other high temperature heat processes that are currently difficult to electrify.
tech_0076_electric-arc-and-plasma-arc-fu.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential to electrify high temperature heat processes, although it remains at relatively early stages of development for key new applications.

initiatives.description: Europlasma commercialises plasma torches for high temperature heating. Their units are used for gas cleaning or for hazardous waste treatment. One such example is an asbestos waste vitrification plant capable of treating 40t per day.
The Steel Slab Electrified Induction Reheat Furnace Upgrade project plans to electrify the only production facility for high-silicon grain oriented electrical steel (GOES) in the United States
PyroGenesis announced signing a contract with an undisclosed US client to deploy a plasma torch system of 20 MW.

The project Hybrid-FIRE, funded by 2.2 million EUR from the German government, comissioned a 100 kW microwave plasma burner pilot furnace in 2025 to study electrification of furnaces for the ceramics and metals industry.

tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_zh: 感應加熱是指交流電流通過感應線圈時產生電磁場，進而在鄰近放置的導電材料中感應出電流，電流越強，物體內部產生的熱量越多。若磁場強度足以超過材料的熔點，材料便會發生相變，此技術常用於金屬熔煉，部分應用已商業化。
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 工業中高溫熱能目前多由化石燃料供應。此技術可取代多種製程中的燃料燃燒爐或熔爐，降低現場排放，使用再生能源可實現無二氧化碳排放的加熱製程，由於感應爐已在部分應用中商業化，發展潛力可觀。
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.technology_status_summary_en: During induction, an electromagnetic field is generated when AC current flows through an inductor: this induces a current flow in a conductive material appositely placed nearby. The higher the current flow, the more the heat generated inside the object itself. If the field is raised enough to overcome the melting point, the material changes phase: this technology is used commonly for the melting of metals. While already commercial for some applications, research and development could expand the range of applications, further improve efficiency and reduce costs.
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.market_dynamics_summary_en: High- to medium-temperature heat (greater than 100 °C) in industry is currently supplied mostly by fossil fuels. This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens and furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. Since induction furnaces are already commercial in some applications, they could provide good potential for further development for other applications.
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.description: During induction, an electromagnetic field is generated when AC current flows through an inductor: this induces a current flow in a conductive material appositely placed nearby. The higher the current flow, the more the heat generated inside the object itself. If the field is raised enough to overcome the melting point, the material changes phase: this technology is used commonly for the melting of metals. While already commercial for some applications, research and development could expand the range of applications, further improve efficiency and reduce costs.
tech_0077_induction-high-temperature-hea.NZErationale: High- to medium-temperature heat (greater than 100 °C) in industry is currently supplied mostly by fossil fuels. This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens and furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can enable a CO2-free heating process. Since induction furnaces are already commercial in some applications, they could provide good potential for further development for other applications.

initiatives.description: The National Steel Company (NASCO) in Saudi Arabia upgraded its technology to achieve a capacity of 100 t, partly through an Induction Furnace of 20 t in combination with Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) in its Dammam steel work.
The Indian company Viraj, one of the largest manufacturers of stainless steel, runs induction furnaces since its establishment in 1992, providing high quality products worldwide.

The German Company ABP is a global induction technology manufacturer and provides several steel producers with induction furnaces, such as the Chinese one Tisco with the installation of the world's strongest medium-frequency induction furnace system (with six 60 t induction furnaces and a 42 MW power supply)

Induction heating is also being investigated for non-metals related applications. For example, Boeing has developed an induction process for moulding thermoplastic composites, which significantly reduces energy requirements.

Siemens Energy and AES operated a 500 kW pilot of an induction heating system in 2025, with hopes to scale it up to 7.5MW by 2027 using molten salts and a 5 MW demonstrator providing industrial steam.

tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.technology_status_summary_zh: 微波加熱是在非導電材料內部產生熱能的技術，物體置於與高頻產生器連接的兩電極之間，分子受激發後在材料內部產生熱能，其優點為快速傳熱、不產生燃燒產物且系統啟動速度快。
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可取代多種製程中的燃料燃燒爐或熔爐，降低現場排放。使用再生能源可確保無二氧化碳排放的加熱製程，由於微波加熱已在部分應用中商業化，其他應用及更高溫度範圍仍有進一步發展的潛力。
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.technology_status_summary_en: Microwave heating is the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 100 to 10000 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems.
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Since microwave heating is already commercial in some applications, it could provide good potential for further development for other applications and for higher temperatures. In particular, its development for carbon fibre carbonisation could play an important role in substantially reducing emissions of producing carbon fibre, which may play an important role in lightweighting in a net zero world.
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.description: Microwave heating is the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 100 to 10000 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems.
tech_0078_photochemical-heating-high-tem.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. Since microwave heating is already commercial in some applications, it could provide good potential for further development for other applications and for higher temperatures. In particular, its development for carbon fibre carbonisation could play an important role in substantially reducing emissions of producing carbon fibre, which may play an important role in lightweighting in a net zero world.

initiatives.description: Oak Ridge National Laboratory in the US has developed a Microwave Assisted Plasma (MAP) process to carbonize PAN fibres, a set of carbon fibre production. MAP offers considerable time and energy savings over the current thermal pyrolysis process, which requires 1000-1500 degree Celsius heat. The technology concept has been validated in the lab and is ready for scaling up to a 25 tonne per year pilot.

A EU-funded research project DAPHNE (Development of adaptive ProductioN systems for Eco-efficient firing processes) from 2012-2015 demonstrated a package of integrated solutions for energy intensive processes, based on substituting high temperature heating with micro-wave technology. The DAPhNE project brings together three manufacturing sectors (ceramic, glass and cement) seeking common solutions via the implementation of high temperature microwaves technologies based on self-adaptive control and monitoring systems. Researchers first produced a lab-scale prototype and then a successful semi-industrial prototype.

tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_zh: 使用無線電波可在非導電材料內部產生熱能，物體置於與高頻產生器連接的兩電極之間，分子受激發後在材料內部產生熱能，其優點為快速傳熱、不產生燃燒產物且系統啟動速度快，射頻系統相較微波系統成本較低。
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可取代多種製程中的燃料燃燒爐或熔爐，降低現場排放。使用再生能源可確保無二氧化碳排放的加熱製程。雖然目前已應用於較低溫加熱場合，但用於更高溫加熱的相關研究似乎較為有限。
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.technology_status_summary_en: The use of radio waves allows the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 1 to 100 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems. RF systems are less expensive compared to microwave systems, but they are not as well suited for products with irregular shapes. Its potential for application includes drying, sintering, calcining, cooking, curing, pre-heating and speeding up chemical reactions.
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. While used today in lower temperature heating applications, investigation of its use for higher temperature heating appears more limited.
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.description: The use of radio waves allows the generation of heat, internally, in non-conductive materials. The object is placed between two electrodes connected with a high-frequency generator (operative frequency is in the 1 to 100 MHz range). The excitement of the molecules generates heat inside the material itself. The advantages are a rapid heat transfer, the absence of combustion products and the high speed of switching on the systems. RF systems are less expensive compared to microwave systems, but they are not as well suited for products with irregular shapes. Its potential for application includes drying, sintering, calcining, cooking, curing, pre-heating and speeding up chemical reactions.
tech_0079_radio-waves-high-temperature-h.NZErationale: This technology can replace fuel-fired ovens or furnaces in several processes, reducing on-site emissions. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. While used today in lower temperature heating applications, investigation of its use for higher temperature heating appears more limited.

initiatives.description: Radio wave heating is used commercially in various low to medium temperature heating applications. Exploration of its potential use for higher temperature heating appears to be so far relatively limited.

tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.technology_name_zh: 旋轉壓縮加熱器（高溫加熱）
tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.technology_status_summary_zh: 壓縮加熱是利用旋轉渦輪對氣體施加快速加速與減速，藉此提升其溫度。其中一例為Coolbrook公司的旋轉動力加熱器，是一種能達到攝氏1700度的電加熱器，可應用於化學品與水泥等多種工業領域。
tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.technology_status_summary_en: Compression heating involves the rotating turbines imposing rapid acceleration and deceleration to gases, increasing their heat. One example of these, Coolbrook's RotoDynamic Heater (RDH) is a type of electric heater able to reach temperatures of 1700°C. This could be used in many industrial sectors, such as chemicals and cement.
tech_0080_rotary-compression-heater-high.description: Compression heating involves the rotating turbines imposing rapid acceleration and deceleration to gases, increasing their heat. One example of these, Coolbrook's RotoDynamic Heater (RDH) is a type of electric heater able to reach temperatures of 1700°C. This could be used in many industrial sectors, such as chemicals and cement.

initiatives.description: After piloting their RotoDynamic heater technology since 2021, the Finnish company Coolbrook have now launched commercial demonstration projects of RotoDynamic heaters in 2022. Finland-based Coolbrook launched an 8 MWth RotoDynamic heater in 2024. Initial units have already entered manufacturing, with the first deployments in industrial sites expected early in 2026.

Coolbrook will install their RotoDynamic heater in a cement plant in India owned by Adani Cement. It will decarbonise the pre-calciner and reduce direct emissions by ~60,000 tonnes of carbon annually with potential to increase further. The system will be powered by Adani Cement's large renewables facilities.

tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.technology_status_summary_zh: 紫外線是電磁波譜中介於40至400奈米之間的部分，其頻率與能量皆高於可見光。利用UV光加熱物體是一種光化學製程，用於固化或瞬間硬化特殊化合物，UV燈提供驅動聚合反應所需的輻射能量，可應用於汽車零件製造、印刷、食品包裝等多種工業製程。
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可降低熱製程的現場排放，其加熱方案速度更快、效率更高。使用再生能源可確保無二氧化碳排放的加熱製程，在中溫熱應用方面潛力可觀，但在高溫應用方面仍待進一步發展。
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.technology_status_summary_en: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) is the part of the electromagnetic spectrum between 40 and 400 nm, with higher frequency and higher energy than the visible light. The use of UV light to heat objects is a photochemical process used to cure or instantly harden special compounds. The use of an UV lamp provides the radiant energy necessary to drive the polymerization reaction. This technology can be applied in several industrial processes like the automotive part manufacturing, printing, food packaging and electronics.
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.market_dynamics_summary_en: This technology can reduce the on-site emissions of thermal processes, with a much faster (both in start-up and shut-down phases) and more efficient heating solution. The use of renewable electricity can guarantee a CO2-free heating process. It may have good potential for medium temperature heat applications, but its applications in high temperature heat is likely limited.
tech_0081_ultra-violet-high-temperature-.description: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) is the part of the electromagnetic spectrum between 40 and 400 nm, with higher frequency and higher energy than the visible light. The use of UV light to heat objects is a photochemical process used to cure or instantly harden special compounds.
