initiatives.description: Across the EU member states over 1500 HDV electric recharging stations are deployed
In 2025 several electric truck models in China hit the market with a range of over 800 km
Over 10% of truck sales in China were electric in the first half of 2025
Pilot project to test megawatt charging in real-world trucks fleet operations

tech_0264_battery-electric-urban-transit.technology_status_summary_zh: 電動公車使用以電池組排列的電池（目前幾乎全為鋰離子電池），電池組結合變流器與電動馬達，將電池提供的電能轉化為機械能。長途公車若要具競爭力，可能需要專用基礎設施（如換電或適當的充電基礎設施）或高能量密度電池化學體系（如目前仍處於原型階段的固態電池）。
tech_0264_battery-electric-urban-transit.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 公車電氣化，加上再生能源與零碳發電滲透率提升，是大眾運輸去碳化最具前景的技術機會之一。
tech_0264_battery-electric-urban-transit.technology_status_summary_en: Electric buses use batteries (today almost exclusively Li-ion batteries) arranged in a battery pack. The battery pack is combined with inverters and an electric motor to convert electrical energy provided by the batteries into mechanical energy. Long haul transit buses might require either devoted infrastructure (e.g. battery swapping or adequate charging infrastructure) or high-energy density battery chemistries (e.g. solid-state batteries, currently at the prototype stage) to be competitive, and deployment of these solutions is still limited. Urban buses have significantly lower requirements thanks to recurrent stops in predefined locations.
tech_0264_battery-electric-urban-transit.market_dynamics_summary_en: Electrification of buses, together with increasing penetration of renewables and zero-carbon electricity generation, is one of the most promising technology opportunity for decarbonising public transport.
tech_0264_battery-electric-urban-transit.description: Electric buses use batteries (today almost exclusively Li-ion batteries) arranged in a battery pack. The battery pack is combined with inverters and an electric motor to convert electrical energy provided by the batteries into mechanical energy. Long haul transit buses might require either devoted infrastructure (e.g. battery swapping or adequate charging infrastructure) or high-energy density battery chemistries (e.g. solid-state batteries, currently at the prototype stage) to be competitive, and deployment of these solutions is still limited. Urban buses have significantly lower requirements thanks to recurrent stops in predefined locations.
tech_0264_battery-electric-urban-transit.NZErationale: Electrification of buses, together with increasing penetration of renewables and zero-carbon electricity generation, is one of the most promising technology opportunity for decarbonising public transport.

initiatives.description: In the first 3 quarters of 2025 nearly one in four new buses in Europe was electric. In other parts of the world deployment is gearing up as well, with over 70,000 electric buses in Latin America, and 500 in Africa
In 2020, the global e-bus fleet reached over half a million

tech_0265_cell-to-chassis-battery-techno.technology_status_summary_zh: 電芯到底盤（CTC）技術將電芯與底盤整合，接著整合馬達、電子控制與車輛高壓系統（如DC/DC轉換器、車載充電機等），並以智慧電力域控制器最佳化配電並降低耗電，透過移除標準電池組部分元件，可望大幅提升能量密度。
tech_0265_cell-to-chassis-battery-techno.technology_status_summary_en: Cell-to-Chassis (CTC) technology integrates the battery cell with the chassis and then integrates the motor, electronic control, and vehicle high voltage such as DC/DC, OBC, etc, and an intelligent power domain controller optimises power distribution and reduces energy consumption. It could lead to a significant increase of energy density by removing some components of the standard battery packs.
tech_0265_cell-to-chassis-battery-techno.description: Cell-to-Chassis (CTC) technology integrates the battery cell with the chassis and then integrates the motor, electronic control, and vehicle high voltage such as DC/DC, OBC, etc, and an intelligent power domain controller optimises power distribution and reduces energy consumption. It could lead to a significant increase of energy density by removing some components of the standard battery packs.

initiatives.description: First mass market deployment of cell-to-body technology, increeasing EV battery energy density and decreasing their costs.

In September 2020, Tesla unveiled CTC technology at Battery Day. The battery cells or modules are installed in the body, connecting the front and rear body castings, and replacing cockpit floor with a battery upper cover. The technology is to be used in 2022 Model Y. Tesla predicts a 55% reduction in investment per GWH and a 35% reduction in space occupied with CTC technology. However, by end 2024, it was still unclear wheter this technology achieved mass market volume.

tech_0266_cell-to-pack-battery-technolog.technology_status_summary_zh: 電芯到電池包（CTP）是指消除電池包內傳統模組的電池架構。CTP不再組裝多個各自含有電芯、熱管理元件與電氣連接的模組，而是將所有電芯直接整合至單一大型結構單元中，此設計減少元件數量、集中電池管理與冷卻系統，並提升體積效率，是介於傳統電池包與未來設計間的中間步驟。
tech_0266_cell-to-pack-battery-technolog.technology_status_summary_en: Cell-to-Pack (CTP) refers to a battery architecture that eliminates traditional modules within a pack. Instead of assembling multiple modules—each containing cells, thermal management components, and electrical connections—the CTP approach integrates all battery cells directly into a single, large structural unit. This design reduces the number of components, centralizes battery management and cooling systems, and improves volumetric efficiency. As an intermediate step between conventional pack designs and cell-to-chassis configurations, CTP enables higher energy density and cost savings through simplified assembly and reduced material use.
tech_0266_cell-to-pack-battery-technolog.description: Cell-to-Pack (CTP) refers to a battery architecture that eliminates traditional modules within a pack. Instead of assembling multiple modules—each containing cells, thermal management components, and electrical connections—the CTP approach integrates all battery cells directly into a single, large structural unit. This design reduces the number of components, centralizes battery management and cooling systems, and improves volumetric efficiency. As an intermediate step between conventional pack designs and cell-to-chassis configurations, CTP enables higher energy density and cost savings through simplified assembly and reduced material use.

initiatives.description: First deployment of cell-to-pack technology, increeasing EV battery energy density and decreasing their costs. This technology opened the possibility to widespread adoption of LFP batteries in EV applications.
In 2022, Contemporary Amperex Intelligence Technology (CATL) launched its new cell-to-pack concept to be commercialized in 2023, further increasing volume utilization efficiency.
BYD launch of its prismatic blade battery, which also has a structural role in cell-to-pack configuration.

tech_0267_automated-and-connected-vehicl.technology_name_zh: 自動化與聯網車輛（第4級以上）
tech_0267_automated-and-connected-vehicl.technology_status_summary_zh: 自動化與聯網車輛運用先進感測、通訊與運算技術，實現極少或無需人為介入的駕駛功能，這類車輛可透過生態駕駛、最佳化路線規劃與車輛編隊等策略，改善交通流量，達到比人類駕駛更高的能源效率，也更可能採用電氣化，進一步降低排放。若結合共享與共乘出行模式，自動化車輛。
tech_0267_automated-and-connected-vehicl.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若這類車輛為共享、自動化且聯網（並為電動車），有望主要透過共乘/共享出行與車輛適配化，減少車輛行駛里程並提升車輛效率。然而若這些技術僅部署於私家車，低移動成本可能導致反彈效應，及/或對大眾運輸產生不利影響。
tech_0267_automated-and-connected-vehicl.technology_status_summary_en:
Automated and connected vehicles leverage advanced sensing, communication, and computing technologies to enable driving functions with minimal or no human intervention. These vehicles can achieve higher energy efficiency than human-driven counterparts through strategies such as eco-driving, optimized routing, and platooning, which improve traffic flow. They are also more likely to adopt electrification, further lowering emissions.
When combined with shared and pooled mobility models, automated vehicles can deliver substantial reductions in energy use per passenger-kilometre. However, widespread deployment requires continued progress in software (artificial intelligence, machine learning for perception and decision-making) and hardware (sensors, high-speed communications, and onboard computing). Addressing challenges related to cybersecurity, interoperability, and infrastructure readiness will be critical for scaling these technologies.
tech_0267_automated-and-connected-vehicl.market_dynamics_summary_en: If these vehicles are shared, automated and connected (and electric), they may help to reduce vkm and increase vehicle efficiency, primarily through pooled/shared rides and vehicle right-sizing. However, if these technologies are deployed only in cars, low cost of mobility may lead to rebound, and/or adverse effects for public transit.
tech_0267_automated-and-connected-vehicl.description:
Automated and connected vehicles leverage advanced sensing, communication, and computing technologies to enable driving functions with minimal or no human intervention. These vehicles can achieve higher energy efficiency than human-driven counterparts through strategies such as eco-driving, optimized routing, and platooning, which improve traffic flow. They are also more likely to adopt electrification, further lowering emissions.
When combined with shared and pooled mobility models, automated vehicles can deliver substantial reductions in energy use per passenger-kilometre. However, widespread deployment requires continued progress in software (artificial intelligence, machine learning for perception and decision-making) and hardware (sensors, high-speed communications, and onboard computing). Addressing challenges related to cybersecurity, interoperability, and infrastructure readiness will be critical for scaling these technologies.
tech_0267_automated-and-connected-vehicl.NZErationale: If these vehicles are shared, automated and connected (and electric), they may help to reduce vkm and increase vehicle efficiency, primarily through pooled/shared rides and vehicle right-sizing. However, if these technologies are deployed only in cars, low cost of mobility may lead to rebound, and/or adverse effects for public transit.

initiatives.description: Ongoing trials for shared, automated, and connected fleets around the world. One example is Waymo's service in San Francisco, California.
Carnegie Mellon University's Navlab 1, a full-size van equipped with computers and sensors, demonstrated autonomous driving capabilities in suburban neighborhoods.
Google started its "Google Self-Driving Car Project"

In January 2023, Mercedes became the first automaker to receive certification for level 3 autonomous driving in the United States, in which all aspects of driving can be automated, but the driver must remain available to take control if prompted to do so, and the first models are now available in California and Nevada
BYD has begun incorporating its level 2 autonomous driving “God’s Eye” system into low-cost EV models.
Tesla continues to pursue level 3 and level 4 autonomy, but is limited to level 2 at present.
China Huaneng Group commissioned the world's first fleet of 100 autonomous, all-electric mining trucks at the Yimin open-pit mine in Inner Mongolia
Chinese autonomous driving company Pony.ai operates over 720 robotaxis and 170 robotrucks
China's autonomous driving company Pony.ai announced that it has been granted permission to carry out cross-provincial expressway testing of unmanned truck platoons, the first such testing in China.

tech_0268_2-3w-charging.technology_status_summary_zh: 換電是傳統電動車充電的替代方案，旨在縮短補能時間。在換電站，電動車用戶可自動將電量耗盡的電池換成充飽電的電池，通常僅需數分鐘，此方式可提升車輛使用率，並支持從乘用車到二三輪車及重型車輛等多個細分市場的電動車部署。
tech_0268_2-3w-charging.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 電動自行車與二三輪電動車換電極具吸引力，主要因為補能時間僅需數分鐘。此外，換電有助於緩解配電與輸電電網的負擔壓力，這在電動機車數量龐大的國家（如印度）尤為重要。
tech_0268_2-3w-charging.technology_status_summary_en: Battery swapping is an alternative to conventional electric vehicle (EV) charging, designed to reduce refuelling time. At a battery swapping station, an EV user can automatically exchange a depleted battery for a fully charged one, typically in a matter of minutes. This approach can improve vehicle utilisation and support the deployment of EVs across multiple segments, from passenger cars to two- and three-wheelers and heavy-duty vehicles.
tech_0268_2-3w-charging.market_dynamics_summary_en: Battery swapping for e-bikes and electric 2/3 wheelersis very attractive for many reasons, primarily refuelling time, just a few minutes. Furthermore, battery swapping can help to  alleviate that the issue of strains on distribution and transmission grids, which is relevant in countries with large numbers of electric motorcycles, such as India.
tech_0268_2-3w-charging.description: Battery swapping is an alternative to conventional electric vehicle (EV) charging, designed to reduce refuelling time. At a battery swapping station, an EV user can automatically exchange a depleted battery for a fully charged one, typically in a matter of minutes. This approach can improve vehicle utilisation and support the deployment of EVs across multiple segments, from passenger cars to two- and three-wheelers and heavy-duty vehicles.
tech_0268_2-3w-charging.NZErationale: Battery swapping for e-bikes and electric 2/3 wheelersis very attractive for many reasons, primarily refuelling time, just a few minutes. Furthermore, battery swapping can help to  alleviate that the issue of strains on distribution and transmission grids, which is relevant in countries with large numbers of electric motorcycles, such as India.

initiatives.description: Gogoro is considered to be the leader in battery swapping for e-bikes. It has now expanded to India and Singapore with the aim to decarbonise last-mile deliveries.
Gogoro has more than 12 000 battery swapping stations in Chinese Taipei as of 2022 and supplied more than 500 000 riders.
India's Battery Smart company successfully rolled out >1500 battery swapping stations in 2025

tech_0269_vehicles-exc-2-3w-charging.technology_name_zh: 車輛（不含二三輪車）（換電）
tech_0269_vehicles-exc-2-3w-charging.technology_status_summary_zh: 換電是傳統電動車充電的替代方案，旨在縮短補能時間。在換電站，電動車用戶可自動將電量耗盡的電池換成充飽電的電池，通常僅需數分鐘，此方式可提升車輛使用率，並支持從乘用車到二三輪車及重型車輛等多個細分市場的電動車部署。
tech_0269_vehicles-exc-2-3w-charging.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 由於電動車充電時間對部分消費者構成限制，若能發展出可在與燃油車補能相當的時間內完成的高效換電技術，將極具吸引力。此外，換電站可作為儲能設施，有助於在尖峰時段減輕電網負擔。
tech_0269_vehicles-exc-2-3w-charging.technology_status_summary_en: Battery swapping is an alternative to conventional electric vehicle (EV) charging, designed to reduce refuelling time. At a battery swapping station, an EV user can automatically exchange a depleted battery for a fully charged one, typically in a matter of minutes. This approach can improve vehicle utilisation and support the deployment of EVs across multiple segments, from passenger cars to two- and three-wheelers and heavy-duty vehicles.
tech_0269_vehicles-exc-2-3w-charging.market_dynamics_summary_en: With the electric vehicle (EV) charging time being a constraint for some consumers, an efficient battery swapping technology that could be implemented in a timeframe comparable to that of refuelling internal combustion engines could be attractive. Furthermore, battery swapping stations could act as energy storage and help unload the grid during peak consumption hours. Today, the market is more inclined to prioritise fast and ultra fast chargers due to the cost of swapping stations and their complexity. Furthermore, the technology is not ready to be commercialised at a large scale as stations are not compatible with all EV models.
tech_0269_vehicles-exc-2-3w-charging.description: Battery swapping is an alternative to conventional electric vehicle (EV) charging, designed to reduce refuelling time. At a battery swapping station, an EV user can automatically exchange a depleted battery for a fully charged one, typically in a matter of minutes. This approach can improve vehicle utilisation and support the deployment of EVs across multiple segments, from passenger cars to two- and three-wheelers and heavy-duty vehicles.
tech_0269_vehicles-exc-2-3w-charging.NZErationale: With the electric vehicle (EV) charging time being a constraint for some consumers, an efficient battery swapping technology that could be implemented in a timeframe comparable to that of refuelling internal combustion engines could be attractive. Furthermore, battery swapping stations could act as energy storage and help unload the grid during peak consumption hours. Today, the market is more inclined to prioritise fast and ultra fast chargers due to the cost of swapping stations and their complexity. Furthermore, the technology is not ready to be commercialised at a large scale as stations are not compatible with all EV models.

initiatives.description: In 2023, Nio is the leader in battery swapping infrastructure with more than 1 000 stations deployed in China and a further 1 000 planned to be deployed. The upcoming batch deployment will be capable of swapping a battery in under 5 minutes and replace around 400 batteries per day.

NIO further developed its battery swapping infrastructure stock in Europe by opening the first in Denmark. This arrives after deployment in Netherlands, Norway, Germany and Sweden. In total, NIO deployed 12 stations in Europe with the ambition to expand even more.

CATL has introduced a new technology for trucks called Modul to bracket (MTB) which integrates the battery directly into the vehicle's chassis. This enables the battery to be lighter and ease battery swapping. The company will be testing the swap project at Qiyuan Core Power in 2023.
CATL partnered with a Chinese highway operator in the Fujian province to construct 4 battery swap stations for heavy trucks.
Nio provided nearly 100 000 daily car battery swap services at the start of 2025 and has over 3 300 battery swap stations in China
About 30% of new electric heavy-trucks (nearly 30 000) sold in China in 2024 are battery swap-capable.
CATL and Sinopec announced a plan for 10 000 battery swap stations in China, which would mean that 1 in 3 petrol stations of Sinopec would have a battery swap station.
About 100 Fiat 500e's that are part of a car sharing fleet in Madrid are fitted with battery swap technology

CATL unveils two standardized battery models, #20 and #25, to ease the deployment of battery swapping. At the same time, they announced the creation of battery swap ecosystem using those battery standards with nearly 100 partners. The co-development of 10 new EV models with swappable batteries and the construction of 1000 new battery swapping stations have also been announced.
Nio surpassed 2000 installed battery stations, most of which in China and 27 in Europe.
Geely joins Nio in its battery swap alliance. By early 2024, the NIO battery swap alliance included Chinese automakers - including Geely, Chery, JAC, and Changan.
Nio and CATL forms strategic partership on battery swapping.

tech_0270_vehicle-to-grid-v2g.technology_status_summary_zh: 雙向充電使電力能流入與流出電動車，讓電動車電池能支援住家（V2H）、建築（V2B）、設備負載（V2L）或更廣泛的電網（V2G）。透過讓電動車在電力便宜或充裕時儲電、在電力稀缺或高價時放電，V2G可提供削峰、頻率調節與在地靈活性等服務，實現這些功能需要雙向充電器與能提供放電的電動車。
tech_0270_vehicle-to-grid-v2g.technology_status_summary_en: Bidirectional charging enables power to flow both to and from an electric vehicle, allowing EV batteries to support homes (V2H), buildings (V2B), appliances loads (V2L) or the wider grid (V2G). By enabling EVs to store electricity when it is cheap or abundant and discharge when it is scarce or valuable, V2G can provide services such as peak shaving, frequency regulation and local flexibility. Delivering these capabilities requires bidirectional chargers, electric cars that enable  delivering electricity, communication protocols that ensure safe and secure power export, and market frameworks that allow consumers or aggregators to participate in grid-service markets. While technically part of the broader smart-charging ecosystem, V2G is considered a separate technology given its additional hardware, interoperability and regulatory requirements.
tech_0270_vehicle-to-grid-v2g.description: Bidirectional charging enables power to flow both to and from an electric vehicle, allowing EV batteries to support homes (V2H), buildings (V2B), appliances loads (V2L) or the wider grid (V2G). By enabling EVs to store electricity when it is cheap or abundant and discharge when it is scarce or valuable, V2G can provide services such as peak shaving, frequency regulation and local flexibility. Delivering these capabilities requires bidirectional chargers, electric cars that enable  delivering electricity, communication protocols that ensure safe and secure power export, and market frameworks that allow consumers or aggregators to participate in grid-service markets. While technically part of the broader smart-charging ecosystem, V2G is considered a separate technology given its additional hardware, interoperability and regulatory requirements.

initiatives.description: Launch of large-scale car-sharing service (500 Evs), using V2G technology to balance local electricity generated from Solar PV and demand for electricity by shared mobility
Octopus Energy and BYD have launched the UK’s first bundled V2G solution, enabling free home charging and grid integration via a V2G-capable EV, bi-directional charger, and smart tariff.

The release of OCPP 2.1 in January 2025 introduced native support for bidirectional charging via ISO 15118-20, enabling standardized vehicle-to-grid (V2G) functionality and positioning EVs as active energy resources within smart grid ecosystems
In 2024 there were over 30 bi-directionalcapable electric car models available, an increase of nearly 20% from 2023.

tech_0271_smart-charging-transport-deman.technology_name_zh: 智慧充電（運輸需量反應）
tech_0271_smart-charging-transport-deman.technology_status_summary_zh: 智慧充電是指以有利於系統的方式，協調並管理電動車電池的充電，避免電網尖峰需求或壅塞。雖然不需要非常不同的基礎設施，但仍需創新以整合資訊科技（IT）與操作科技（OT），包括遠端感測、大數據分析、遠端感測與控制。從技術角度而言，最簡單的部署策略是開發後端系統。
tech_0271_smart-charging-transport-deman.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 由於瞬時負載超過一般家庭的額定容量，大量整合電動車最終將對地方配電網絡以及上游輸電與發電構成負擔，如今在高滲透率高容量電動車的特定地區已出現熱點問題。
tech_0271_smart-charging-transport-deman.technology_status_summary_en: Smart charging refers to the co-ordinated and managed charging of the batteries in electric vehicles in a way that benefits the system, avoiding peak demand or congestion on the grid. While it does not require a very different infrastructure, innovation is needed to integrate IT and OT (Operational Technologies), including remote sensing, big data analytics, remote sensing and control. The simplest strategy for deployment, from a technology perspective, would be to develop back-end systems that would function without much involvement from consumers, and incorporate information on grid needs and services that can be provided by EV batteries, and allow for these services to be delivered to the grid by default contracts, rather than relying on opt-in from customers.
tech_0271_smart-charging-transport-deman.market_dynamics_summary_en: With instantaneous loads above the rated capacity of the average household, integrating large numbers of electric vehicles (EVs) will eventually impose burdens on local distribution grids as well as upstream transmission and generation. Today, hotspots are already seen in specific areas with high penetration of high-capacity EVs.
tech_0271_smart-charging-transport-deman.description: Smart charging refers to the co-ordinated and managed charging of the batteries in electric vehicles in a way that benefits the system, avoiding peak demand or congestion on the grid. While it does not require a very different infrastructure, innovation is needed to integrate IT and OT (Operational Technologies), including remote sensing, big data analytics, remote sensing and control. The simplest strategy for deployment, from a technology perspective, would be to develop back-end systems that would function without much involvement from consumers, and incorporate information on grid needs and services that can be provided by EV batteries, and allow for these services to be delivered to the grid by default contracts, rather than relying on opt-in from customers.
tech_0271_smart-charging-transport-deman.NZErationale: With instantaneous loads above the rated capacity of the average household, integrating large numbers of electric vehicles (EVs) will eventually impose burdens on local distribution grids as well as upstream transmission and generation. Today, hotspots are already seen in specific areas with high penetration of high-capacity EVs.

initiatives.description: Amsterdam launches smart charging pilot to deal with congension, this shows that smart charging is being picked up by other regions outside of UK, and progressing towards TRL 9 - adoption across multiple regions

tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.sector_zh: 動態充電或電動道路系統
sector: Dynamic charging or electric road system
tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.sector_en: Dynamic charging or electric road system
tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.technology_status_summary_zh: 電動道路系統（ERS）是一種基礎設施網絡，讓電動車能在行駛中透過道路本身供應的能源進行充電。ERS主要有兩種形式：傳導式，使用嵌入車道的供電軌或軌道；感應式，使用電磁線圈。透過實現動態充電，ERS可減少對大型車載電池的需求，延長續航里程，並提高運作靈活性，尤其適用於重型與長途運輸。
tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 動態充電可補充或替代傳統電動車充電（例如讓道路車輛能以較小電池實現電動運行）。電動道路系統有望加速採用，尤其適用於重型及密集使用及/或長途運輸，如城際巴士（客車）與區域運輸。
tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.technology_status_summary_en:
Electric Road Systems (ERS) are infrastructure networks that allow electric vehicles (EVs) to charge while in motion, using energy supplied through the road itself. ERS can take two main forms: conductive, using power rails or rails embedded in lanes, and inductive, using electromagnetic coils. By enabling dynamic charging, ERS reduce the need for large onboard batteries, extend driving range, and increase operational flexibility, particularly for heavy-duty and long-haul transport.
Conductive charging (ERS) delivers electrical energy to EVs in motion through physical contact with rails or power conductors embedded in the roadway or along the roadside. Vehicles collect energy via a mounted contact device, allowing continuous charging during travel. This method provides high energy transfer efficiency and can significantly reduce battery size requirements, but requires substantial infrastructure investment, precise vehicle alignment, and rigorous safety measures.
tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.market_dynamics_summary_en: Dynamic charging can complement or serve as a substitute to traditional electric vehicle charging (e.g. by enabling road vehicles to run on electric with smaller batteries). Electric Road Systems could accelerate adoption, particularly for heavy-duty and intensive usage and/or long-distance operations like intercity buses (coaches), and regional and long-haul trucking. For other vehicle types and operations, it is of less critical importance than some of the other charging strategies.
tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.description:
Electric Road Systems (ERS) are infrastructure networks that allow electric vehicles (EVs) to charge while in motion, using energy supplied through the road itself. ERS can take two main forms: conductive, using power rails or rails embedded in lanes, and inductive, using electromagnetic coils. By enabling dynamic charging, ERS reduce the need for large onboard batteries, extend driving range, and increase operational flexibility, particularly for heavy-duty and long-haul transport.
Conductive charging (ERS) delivers electrical energy to EVs in motion through physical contact with rails or power conductors embedded in the roadway or along the roadside. Vehicles collect energy via a mounted contact device, allowing continuous charging during travel. This method provides high energy transfer efficiency and can significantly reduce battery size requirements, but requires substantial infrastructure investment, precise vehicle alignment, and rigorous safety measures.
tech_0272_conductive-electric-road-syste.NZErationale: Dynamic charging can complement or serve as a substitute to traditional electric vehicle charging (e.g. by enabling road vehicles to run on electric with smaller batteries). Electric Road Systems could accelerate adoption, particularly for heavy-duty and intensive usage and/or long-distance operations like intercity buses (coaches), and regional and long-haul trucking. For other vehicle types and operations, it is of less critical importance than some of the other charging strategies.

initiatives.description: In Sweden, Trafikverket have studied the financial impact on ERS compared with diesel, with two sections to be opened in 20149 and 2020.
South Coast Air Quality Management District and Siemens demonstrated ERS in the Ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach.
In Sweden, the Volvo automotive test track near Gothenburg is developing technology for trucks and buses through a current collector in the form of a pantograph.

India was conducting trials for the deployment of their National Highway for Electric Vehicles (NHEV) which will be five times bigger than the current longest e-highway in Germany (109 km). The trials ended in late 2022 and the Delhi-Mumbai highway is expected to be EV ready by March 2023.
Siemens Mobility and Continental will co-operate, aiming to achieve serial production of trucks equipped with pantographs, capable of running on ERS.
A kilometer long conductive electric road system has been tested in South of Sweden between 2019 and 2024.
Sweden is building the World's first permanent electrified road, with 2 kilometers already active and potentially thousands of kilometer to be electrified by 2035.

tech_0273_inductive-charging.sector_zh: 動態充電或電動道路系統
sector: Dynamic charging or electric road system
tech_0273_inductive-charging.sector_en: Dynamic charging or electric road system
tech_0273_inductive-charging.technology_status_summary_zh: 感應式充電透過嵌入道路基礎設施的線圈與車輛內接收器間的電磁場，實現無線能量傳輸。雖然此概念可追溯至20世紀初，但應用於電動車仍處於部署早期階段。靜態感應充電通常安裝於停車位，提供比插電式系統更友善且安全的替代方案，支持更廣泛的電動車採用，動態感應充電則是電動道路系統的一種形式。
tech_0273_inductive-charging.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 動態充電可補充或替代傳統電動車充電（例如讓道路車輛能以較小電池實現電動運行）。電動道路系統有望加速採用，尤其適用於重型及密集使用及/或長途運輸，如城際巴士（客車）與區域運輸。
tech_0273_inductive-charging.technology_status_summary_en:
Inductive charging enables wireless energy transfer via electromagnetic fields between coils embedded in road infrastructure and receivers in vehicles. While the concept dates back to the early 20th century, its application to electric vehicles (EVs) remains at an early stage of deployment.
Static inductive charging, typically installed in parking spaces, offers a user-friendly and safe alternative to plug-in systems, supporting broader EV adoption. Dynamic inductive charging, a form of Electric Road System (ERS), allows vehicles to charge while in motion via coils embedded beneath traffic lanes. This can reduce battery size requirements, extend driving range, and improve operational flexibility, particularly for heavy-duty and long-haul freight.
Despite its potential, inductive charging faces challenges including lower energy transfer efficiency, higher infrastructure costs (estimated at over USD 1 million per lane-km for ERS), and greater material and construction demands compared to conductive systems.
tech_0273_inductive-charging.market_dynamics_summary_en: Dynamic charging can complement or serve as a substitute to traditional electric vehicle charging (e.g. by enabling road vehicles to run on electric with smaller batteries). Electric Road Systems could accelerate adoption, particularly for heavy-duty and intensive usage and/or long-distance operations like intercity buses (coaches), and regional and long-haul trucking. For other vehicle types and operations, it is of less critical importance than some of the other charging strategies.

initiatives.description: Electreon, partnered with EnBW, will deploy a 1km Electric Road System along a road in Balingen, Germany. The technology deployed will enable static and dynamic wireless charging infrastructure and be Germany's first-ever public wireless road project. No dates announced yet.
Qualcomm recently developed and tested one of the world’s first DEVC (Dynamic EV Charging) test tracks, proving EV charging dynamically at up to 20 kW at highway speeds (100 km/h)
ElectReon has started trials on the use of electric roads with a 40 tonnes electric truck in Sweden.

Following successful tests in labs and on closed test tracks, the Charge as you Drive project has cleared a new milestone with real-world dynamic wireless charging trial, on a 1.5 km stretch of the A10 motorway near Paris. Four prototype vehicles (a truck, van, car and bus) equipped with the necessary pickup coils are now driving in real-life traffic conditions on a pilot motorway section to assess the system's performance. And the initial results are very encouraging: the wireless technology can transfer more than 300 kW of instantaneous power and more than 200 kW of average power under optimal steady-state conditions.
“Arena del Futuro” Demonstrates Capability of Dynamic Inductive Recharging Technology for Electric Vehicles
In the E|MPOWER project for inductive charging of EVs while driving, the construction phase on the A6 motorway near Amberg has been completed. The test phase is now beginning
Porche announced that its soon to be released Cayenne model will be wireless charging capable.
The city of Detroit in the United States finalized works for the first public EV-charging roadways, albeit for less than a mile.
The first infrastructure deployment and road tests on inductive charging applied to public buses have been carried out in Korea between 2013 and 2017.
Virginia-based Evatran partnered with six commercial EV users to equip their vehicle with inuctive charging systems.

Society of Automotive Engineers International publishes the SAE J2954, their standard for wireless power transfer for light-duty electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles using magnetic resonance. This has also been adopted outside the United States, notably in Europe and Korea, among others.
An Overview of Dynamic Inductive Charging for Electric Vehicles

tech_0274_fast-and-ultra-fast-charging.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前多數商用直流（DC）快充站的功率介於250至350千瓦。350千瓦至1兆瓦的快充技術是加速電動車部署的關鍵驅動力，可提升城際旅行的便利性並降低續航焦慮，尤其電動貨運與較大型電動乘用車型需要快充樞紐，突破高容量門檻仍需多項創新，包括電池技術。
tech_0274_fast-and-ultra-fast-charging.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 長途公路旅行對輕型與重型電動車的採用是特別的障礙，儘管輕型車輛的城際客運需求僅占總客運量的一小部分。部署能以與燃油補給相當的時間實現長途旅行的超快速充電技術。
tech_0274_fast-and-ultra-fast-charging.technology_status_summary_en: Most commercially available direct current (DC) fast charging stations currently enable power levels ranging from 250-350 kW. Fast charging technology of 350 kW up to 1 MW is a key driver for faster electric vehicle (EV) deployment, to increase the convenience of inter-city travel and reduce range anxiety. In particular, fast charging hubs are required for electric road freight and larger EV passenger models. A number of innovations are needed to go beyond high capacity thresholds, including battery and ultracapacitor combinations and integration of high capacity charging equipment within passenger EV and truck frames. Charging stations must provide high power output with minimal impact on the grid, which also will require further integration efforts including application of battery storage, capacitors or smart management systems. Finally, battery chemistries and designs will need to be developed that can be rapidly charged by ultrafast charging systems without risking accelerated degradation (e.g. via dendrite formation). Innovations are also needed on materials that also achieve ultrahigh discharge rates, comparable to those of supercapacitors.
tech_0274_fast-and-ultra-fast-charging.market_dynamics_summary_en: Long-distance road travel is a particular hurdle for light- and heavy-duty electric vehicle adoption, despite the fact that inter-city passenger transport demand from light duty vehicles comprises a small share of total passenger travel. Deploying ultra-fast charging technology that can make trips over long distances possible with refuelling times comparable to those of internal combustion engines could greatly accelerate adoption and reduce emissions from intercity travel.

initiatives.description: By 2024, the global fast-charging network surpassed 2 million units, with ultra-fast chargers (≥150 kW) expanding by over 50% to represent nearly one-tenth of all fast chargers, supported by falling costs—down about 20% between 2022 and 2024.

In March 2025, BYD set a new benchmark for charging speed with its Super-e platform, which is claimed to deliver around 400 km of range in 5 minutes. This leap was made possible by nextgeneration silicon carbide power chips, all-liquid-cooling, and a 1 000 V architecture, which allows for coupling with 1 MW charging.

tech_0275_ultra-fast-charging-1-mw.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前正開發多種充電標準，用於以超過1兆瓦功率為中重型車輛充電，超快速充電的技術規格仍在制定中。在中國，中國電力企業聯合會與CHAdeMO共同開發「超ChaoJi」重型電動車充電標準，功率可達數兆瓦；在歐洲與美國，CharIN兆瓦充電系統（MCS）的規格也在制定中。
tech_0275_ultra-fast-charging-1-mw.technology_status_summary_en: Multiple charging standards are currently being developed for charging medium- and heavy-duty vehicles at power rates of greater than 1 MW. Technical specifications for ultra-fast charging are under development. In China, co-developers China Electricity Council and CHAdeMO’s “ultra ChaoJi” are developing a charging standard for heavy-duty electric vehicles for up to several megawatts. In Europe and the United States, specifications for the CharIN Megawatt Charging System (MCS), with a potential maximum power of 4.5 MW, are under development by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) and other organisations. The final MCS specifications, which will be needed for commercial roll-out of medium- and heavy-duty electric vehicles, are expected for 2025. Ensuring maximum possible convergence of charging standards and interoperability for heavy-duty EVs will be needed to avoid the cost, inefficiency, and challenges for vehicle importers and international operators that would be created by manufacturers following divergent paths.

initiatives.description: Pilot project to test megawatt charging in real-world fleet operations
The European Union and the United States issued a joint statement recognising the MCS as adopted by international standardisation bodies.
The world’s first megawatt chargers for trucks were deployed in the United States and Europe, marking a key technological milestone.

In March 2025, BYD set a new benchmark for charging speed with its Super-e platform, which is claimed to deliver around 400 km of range in 5 minutes. This leap was made possible by nextgeneration silicon carbide power chips, all-liquid-cooling, and a 1 000 V architecture, which allows for coupling with 1 MW charging.

tech_0278_hydrogen-fuel-cell-truck.technology_status_summary_zh: 參見高分子電解質膜燃料電池。
tech_0278_hydrogen-fuel-cell-truck.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在長途重型卡車運輸中，若開發並部署高流速加氫站，燃料電池卡車可能在成本上與電池電動卡車相當競爭，並在加氣時間方面具有優勢。
tech_0278_hydrogen-fuel-cell-truck.market_dynamics_summary_en: In long-haul heavy-duty trucking, fuel cell trucks may become cost-competitive with battery electric trucks and offer advantages in terms of fuelling times if high flow rate refueling stations are developed and deployed.
tech_0278_hydrogen-fuel-cell-truck.NZErationale: In long-haul heavy-duty trucking, fuel cell trucks may become cost-competitive with battery electric trucks and offer advantages in terms of fuelling times if high flow rate refueling stations are developed and deployed.

initiatives.description: The first-of-a-kind commercial hydrogen fuel cell truck developed by Hyundai has obtained 1 600 orders for the Swiss market
FedEx and UPS are trialling fuel cell range-extender Class 6 delivery vehicles.
In October 2024, Accelera by Cummins set a world record for the longest distance travelled by a hydrogen fuel cell heavy-duty truck without refuelling, covering 2 900 km in the United States.

tech_0280_hydrogen-onboard-storage-tank-.technology_name_zh: 車載氫氣儲存槽（道路車輛）
tech_0280_hydrogen-onboard-storage-tank-.technology_status_summary_zh: 車輛須配備氫氣槽以氣態或液態儲存氫氣，由於氫氣體積能量密度低，需要極高壓儲存（35至70百萬帕）及/或極低溫儲存（液態儲存約攝氏零下253度）。氫氣槽的關鍵挑戰包括安全性（加壓氫氣具火災風險，尤其是洩漏情況下）、耐久性、取證、標準化，以及系統重量與體積的最佳化。
tech_0280_hydrogen-onboard-storage-tank-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 若燃料電池電動車成為電池電動車的策略性互補方案，氫氣槽設計與量產的進一步改良，將是確保安全性及在可接受成本下實現高續航里程的關鍵。
tech_0280_hydrogen-onboard-storage-tank-.technology_status_summary_en: Hydrogen tanks are required to store hydrogen on board vehicles, either in gaseous or liquid form. Due to its low volumetric energy density, hydrogen requires very high pressure storage (between 35 and 70 Mpa) and/or at very low temperature (around 20°K for liquid storage). Key challenges related to hydrogen tanks include safety (as H2 under pressure poses a fire risk, especially in case of leakage), durability, certification, standardisation, and optimization of system gravimetric and volumetric energy density. Vehicle integration is another challenge, for example in developping alternatives to the conventional cylindrical tank design. To address safety and integration issues, the industry is moving towards standardisation and modularity, exemplified by hydrogen buses that can be equipped with as many as ten individual cylindrical tanks.
tech_0280_hydrogen-onboard-storage-tank-.market_dynamics_summary_en: In case fuel cell electric vehicles emerge as a strategic complementary solution to battery electric vehicles, further improvements in the design and mass production of hydrogen tanks will be key to ensuring safety and high vehicle ranges at an acceptable cost.
tech_0280_hydrogen-onboard-storage-tank-.description: Hydrogen tanks are required to store hydrogen on board vehicles, either in gaseous or liquid form. Due to its low volumetric energy density, hydrogen requires very high pressure storage (between 35 and 70 Mpa) and/or at very low temperature (around 20°K for liquid storage). Key challenges related to hydrogen tanks include safety (as H2 under pressure poses a fire risk, especially in case of leakage), durability, certification, standardisation, and optimization of system gravimetric and volumetric energy density. Vehicle integration is another challenge, for example in developping alternatives to the conventional cylindrical tank design. To address safety and integration issues, the industry is moving towards standardisation and modularity, exemplified by hydrogen buses that can be equipped with as many as ten individual cylindrical tanks.
tech_0280_hydrogen-onboard-storage-tank-.NZErationale: In case fuel cell electric vehicles emerge as a strategic complementary solution to battery electric vehicles, further improvements in the design and mass production of hydrogen tanks will be key to ensuring safety and high vehicle ranges at an acceptable cost.

tech_0281_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_name: Polymer electrolyte membrane hydrogen fuel cell for FCEVs
tech_0281_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_name_zh: 燃料電池電動車用高分子電解質膜氫燃料電池
tech_0281_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.sector_zh: 燃料電池電動車用氫燃料電池
tech_0281_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫燃料電池系統由氫氣產生電力。燃料電池電動車（FCEV）的電池遠小於電池電動車（至少小10倍），因為能量儲存於氫氣中。透過利用氫氣較高的重量能量密度，FCEV可提供比BEV更高的續航里程。然而其持續部署面臨多項技術與經濟挑戰，包括氫氣處理安全性（加氣、殘留洩漏）。
tech_0281_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 對電動車而言，燃料電池技術是降低對電池依賴的互補解決方案，可緩解對關鍵鋰離子電池材料的需求（但會增加對鉑的需求）。
tech_0281_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_status_summary_en:
A hydrogen fuel cell system generates electric power from hydrogen. Fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEV) have much smaller batteries than battery electric vehicles (at least by a factor of 10), as the energy is stored in the hydrogen. By exploiting the higher gravimetric energy density of hydrogen, FCEVs can offer a higher range than BEVs. However their continuing deployment faces multiple technical and economic challenges, including: safety of hydrogen handling (refuelling, residual leakage), on-board hydrogen storage (see the dedicated entry below) and the high cost of the fuel cell stack (the electrochemical reaction inside the stack requires a proton exchange membrane (PEM) coated with a platinum-based catalyst, a costly material) and system. Costs of the fuel cell stack and system are expected to decline significantly with economies of scale.
For FCEVs to be competitive with other powertrain technologies, hydrogen must be delivered to hydrogen refuelling stations using technologies that lead to zero or very low lifecycle emissions, at prices that bring per-kilometre costs into the same range as conventional ICEs, or of battery electric vehicles powered by grid electricity. This will require further cost reductions in technologies for low- and zero-carbon hydrogen production technologies (e.g. SMR with CCS, renewable electricity generation such as wind and solar coupled to electrolysers), as well as in hydrogen transmission and distribution networks and in hydrogen refuelling stations (HRS).
tech_0281_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.market_dynamics_summary_en: For electric vehicles, fuel cell technology represents a complementary solution to reduce dependency on batteries. The need for critical Li-ion battery materials would be alleviated (at the expense of an increased demand for platinum).

tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_name: Polymer electrolyte membrane hydrogen fuel cell as range-extender in EVs
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_name_zh: 電動車增程用高分子電解質膜氫燃料電池
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.sector_zh: 燃料電池電動車用氫燃料電池
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_status_summary_zh: 與高分子電解質膜燃料電池相同，但尺寸僅設計為對電池電動動力系統提供增程功能。
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 對電動車而言，燃料電池技術是降低對電池依賴的互補解決方案，可緩解對關鍵鋰離子電池材料的需求（但會增加對鉑的需求）。市場上已推出多款增程式燃料電池電動車（許多已停產）。
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.technology_status_summary_en: Same as PEM fuel cell, but sized to only provide range extension to the battery electric powertrain.
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.market_dynamics_summary_en: For electric vehicles (EVs), fuel cell technology represents a complementary solution to reduce dependency on batteries. The need for critical Li-ion battery materials would be alleviated (at the expense of an increased demand for platinum). Various range-extended fuel-cell EVs have been introduced (and many discontinued) in vehicle markets across different segments. Many light commercial vehicles and medium-freight trucks sold in China are, in fact, fuel-cell range extended EVs.
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.description: Same as PEM fuel cell, but sized to only provide range extension to the battery electric powertrain.
tech_0282_polymer-electrolyte-membrane-h.NZErationale: For electric vehicles (EVs), fuel cell technology represents a complementary solution to reduce dependency on batteries. The need for critical Li-ion battery materials would be alleviated (at the expense of an increased demand for platinum). Various range-extended fuel-cell EVs have been introduced (and many discontinued) in vehicle markets across different segments. Many light commercial vehicles and medium-freight trucks sold in China are, in fact, fuel-cell range extended EVs.

initiatives.description: Several major bus manufacturers (Safra, Mercedes, Iveco, Caetano) unveiled their first electric buses equipped with a fuel cell range extender.

tech_0283_hydrogen-fuelled-light-commerc.technology_status_summary_zh: 在內燃機中直接燃燒氫氣，提供了不依賴燃料電池的另一種氫能運輸應用方式。雖然目前效率低於燃料電池（氫能引擎峰值效率為35-45%，燃料電池為50-60%），但氫能引擎不需要鉑等關鍵礦物，可能是具成本效益的解決方案。與燃料電池不同，氫內燃機可使用較低品質等級的氫氣。
tech_0283_hydrogen-fuelled-light-commerc.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氫能引擎不需要鉑等稀有材料，在極寒天氣應用中可能是特別合適的解決方案，因為燃料電池在此類環境可能遇到運作挑戰，且引擎散逸的熱量可用於車廂供暖，此外內燃機對氫氣品質的敏感度較低。
tech_0283_hydrogen-fuelled-light-commerc.technology_status_summary_en: Burning hydrogen directly in an internal combustion engine offers an alternative use of hydrogen in transport, one that does not rely on fuel cells. Although less energy efficient than fuel cells today (35-45% peak efficiency for hydrogen engines vs. 50-60% for fuel cells), the hydrogen engine does not require critical minerals like platinum and could represent a cost-effective solution. Unlike fuel cells, hydrogen internal combustion engines can run on low-quality grade hydrogen, which allows for a broader range of hydrogen production pathways. However, several technical challenges remain, including safety, engine efficiency and power density, and control of exhaust nitrogen oxides (NOₓ) and soot emissions generated by lubricant combustion near the cylinder walls. Research and development (at TRL 6) is currently underway to tackle these challenges.
tech_0283_hydrogen-fuelled-light-commerc.market_dynamics_summary_en:

initiatives.description: Powertrain and engine costs are difficult to estimate, but are likely to be similar in terms of mark-up relative to conventional spark-ignition or compression ignition engines as natural gas powertrain costs (see 'Compressed biogas').

A start-up has developed a kit to convert diesel engines into hydrogen engines for truck and bus applications. The kit includes dedicated fuel, ignition and after-treatment systems, and adapts existing powertrain components, as well as a turbocharger and combustion chamber.
The Aachen-based FEV is developing engine, powertrain, and aftertreatment components for H2 ICE engines.

The Munich-based start-ups KEYOU and DEUTZ are intensifying their cooperation. Both sides intend to expand their existing development partnership and enter into a cooperation for the joint development, industrialisation and commercialisation of CO2-free hydrogen engines for off- and on-road applications.
Cummins is testing a hydrogen fuelled internal combustion engine.
MAN is also testing hydrogen combustion engines.
MAN launched its first commercial heavy-duty H2-fueled ICE truck model, the MAN hTGX. Around 200 vehicles for selected markets expected to be delivered in 2025.
Hylane, an European zero-emission commercial vehicle rental company, includes MAN hTGX to its portfolio.

tech_0287_liquid-hydrogen-road-transport.technology_status_summary_zh: 液態氫相較壓縮氫氣具更高能量密度，可讓車輛擁有更長續航里程，若考量液態氫的集中生產與後續配送，也能提升可運輸的能量總量。
tech_0287_liquid-hydrogen-road-transport.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氫氣用於道路運輸在部分利基應用中很重要，但對絕大多數運輸模式與使用情境而言，已有替代方案存在。
tech_0287_liquid-hydrogen-road-transport.technology_status_summary_en: Liquid hydrogen provides greater energy density than compressed hydrogen, allowing for greater range of vehicles. It also increases the amount of energy that can be transported when considering centralised production and subsequent distribution of the liquid hydrogen.
tech_0287_liquid-hydrogen-road-transport.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen for road transport is important in some niche applications, but alternatives exist for the vast majority of modes and use cases.
tech_0287_liquid-hydrogen-road-transport.description: Liquid hydrogen provides greater energy density than compressed hydrogen, allowing for greater range of vehicles. initiatives.description: Air Liquide and Daimler joint project advancing liquid hydrogen refuelling for heavy-duty vehicles.

tech_0288_high-flow-rate-120-g-s.technology_status_summary_zh: 為使重型氫能卡車能以類似柴油卡車的補油時間完成加氣，加氣機向車輛輸送氫氣的流速需要比目前為乘用車與公車設計的加氣站更快，這需要設計新元件並制定新的加氣協定。
tech_0288_high-flow-rate-120-g-s.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在長途重型卡車運輸中，若開發並部署高流速加氫站，燃料電池卡車可能在成本上與電池電動卡車相當競爭，並在加氣時間方面具有優勢。
tech_0288_high-flow-rate-120-g-s.technology_status_summary_en: To fuel heavy hydrogen trucks in times similar to diesel truck refueling, the flow rate of hydrogen from the dispenser and into the vehicle needs to be faster than it is currently at stations designed for cars and buses. This requires new components to be desgined and new fueling protocols to be developed.
tech_0288_high-flow-rate-120-g-s.market_dynamics_summary_en: In long-haul heavy-duty trucking, fuel cell trucks may become cost competitive with battery electric trucks and offer advantages in terms of fuelling times if high flow rate refueling stations are developed and deployed.
tech_0288_high-flow-rate-120-g-s.description: To fuel heavy hydrogen trucks in times similar to diesel truck refueling, the flow rate of hydrogen from the dispenser and into the vehicle needs to be faster than it is currently at stations designed for cars and buses. This requires new components to be desgined and new fueling protocols to be developed.
tech_0288_high-flow-rate-120-g-s.NZErationale: In long-haul heavy-duty trucking, fuel cell trucks may become cost competitive with battery electric trucks and offer advantages in terms of fuelling times if high flow rate refueling stations are developed and deployed.

initiatives.description: In January 2025, Cavendish announced plans to commercialise its 700-bar hydrogen refuelling stations in 2025 capable of refuelling heavy-duty trucks in 10 minutes

tech_0289_standard-flow-rate-60-120-g-s.technology_status_summary_zh: 一般而言，加氫站運作壓力為350或700巴，多數乘用車加氫站設計於700巴運作，公車加氫站則通常使用350巴。因此目前多數加氫站以700巴運作，其中許多可雙壓運作，能同時提供350與700巴的加氣服務，加氫可為獨立設站，或與製氫站連結。
tech_0289_standard-flow-rate-60-120-g-s.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氫氣用於道路運輸在部分利基應用中很重要，但對絕大多數運輸模式與使用情境而言，已有替代方案存在。
tech_0289_standard-flow-rate-60-120-g-s.technology_status_summary_en:
Normally, hydrogen refuelling stations (HRS) operate at 350 or 700 bar. Most of the stations for passenger cars are designed for operating at 700 bar, while stations for buses typically use 350 bar. For this reason, the majority of the stations currently operate at 700 bar, with many of them operating on a dual basis, being able to deliver fuel to both at 350 and 700 bar. Hydrogen refuelling can be standalone, or linked to a hydrogen production station. If the hydrogen is delivered to the station in intermediary form, storage and compression systems are needed. Hydrogen storage systems are generally low pressure, around 50-200 bar. Compressors overcome the pressure difference between storage and refuelling (which can be up to 1 000 bar), and they are a central area of innovation in hydrogen refuelling stations. Most of the time, storage buffers (at 450 or 950 bar) are used to refuel vehicles (without a direct connection of vehicles to compressor outlet).
A range of technologies can be employed for compressing H2 from low pressure states to up to those needed at the point of use, depending on whether hydrogen is in gaseous or liquid form, and on the throughput vehicle type. High levels of purity are needed in fuel cell applications, so technologies like ionic compressors are needed to reduce the possibility of contamination. During compression the hydrogen gas heats up, and precooling systems are needed to stay within the limits of the vehicle’s fuel storage system. These systems add complexity and increase energy consumption, and are a key area of development for improving the efficiency and reducing costs of hydrogen refuelling systems.
tech_0289_standard-flow-rate-60-120-g-s.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen for road transport is important in some niche applications, but alternatives exist for the vast majority of modes and use cases.

initiatives.description: At the end of 2022, over 1 000 hydrogen refuelling stations (HRS) were in operation worldwide. This stock of HRS has more than doubled from number of stations available in operation at the end of 2019 (460). China leads with 320 stations, followed by Korea (213), Japan (164), Germany (095), and the United States (71). Leading countries have announced targets to build a total of 1 000 hydrogen refuelling stations during 2025‑30.

tech_0290_ethanol-fuelled-diesel-engine.sector_zh: 先進燃燒引擎與動力系統
tech_0290_ethanol-fuelled-diesel-engine.technology_status_summary_zh: 特別適用於可使用生質乙醇的重型貨運，ED95引擎可使用95%乙醇加5%添加劑（含助燃劑）為燃料，這些是經調整、具高壓縮比的柴油引擎，因點燃燃料所需，也需要專用噴射系統，以彌補乙醇較低的能量密度。
tech_0290_ethanol-fuelled-diesel-engine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 具高壓縮比的原生火花點火引擎在效率與污染物排放方面可望取得更好成效，是壓燃式（柴油）引擎的替代方案。乙醇目前是最便宜且全球產量最高的生質燃料，在柴油引擎中的應用具有潛力。
tech_0290_ethanol-fuelled-diesel-engine.technology_status_summary_en: Specifically applied to heavy duty tracking that can use bioethanol, ED95 engines can be fuelled by 95% ethanol and 5% additives (including ignition improvers). They are adapted diesel engines with high compression ratios, which are required to ignite the fuel. A dedicated injection system is also needed, to compensate for the lower energy density of ethanol.
tech_0290_ethanol-fuelled-diesel-engine.market_dynamics_summary_en: Native spark ignition engines with high compression ratios could attain better results in terms of efficiency and pollutant emissions, and present an alternative to compression ignition (diesel) engines. Ethanol is currently the cheapest biofuel available and is the biofuel that is produced in the highest volumes, globally. Its use in diesel engines does not require major changes to truck production lines.
tech_0290_ethanol-fuelled-diesel-engine.description: Specifically applied to heavy duty tracking that can use bioethanol, ED95 engines can be fuelled by 95% ethanol and 5% additives (including ignition improvers). They are adapted diesel engines with high compression ratios, which are required to ignite the fuel. A dedicated injection system is also needed, to compensate for the lower energy density of ethanol.
tech_0290_ethanol-fuelled-diesel-engine.NZErationale: Native spark ignition engines with high compression ratios could attain better results in terms of efficiency and pollutant emissions, and present an alternative to compression ignition (diesel) engines. Ethanol is currently the cheapest biofuel available and is the biofuel that is produced in the highest volumes, globally. Its use in diesel engines does not require major changes to truck production lines.

initiatives.description: Scania has been producing commercially-available trucks using ED95 for several years. They are currently producing the third generation of ED95 engines

tech_0291_compressed-biogas.sector_zh: 先進燃燒引擎與動力系統
tech_0291_compressed-biogas.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術可應用於以生質甲烷為燃料、由內燃機驅動的道路車輛（通常為重型），生質甲烷儲存於高壓槽中（20-25百萬帕）。此技術面臨的主要技術挑戰與引擎中的甲烷逸散（洩漏）有關，通常發生於進氣岐管層級或因燃燒不完全發生於燃燒室中。間接噴射引擎的逸散率高於直噴引擎。
tech_0291_compressed-biogas.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 由於可再生生質甲烷供應有限，此技術的市場預期將維持利基規模，具前景的應用機會為由市政或農業廢棄物供應生質甲烷的市政車隊。若合成甲烷（由低碳電力電解）能以具成本競爭力且豐富的方式生產，前景可望更廣。
tech_0291_compressed-biogas.technology_status_summary_en:
This technology can be adopted in road vehicles (typically heavy duty) powered by an internal combustion engine fuelled by biomethane. The biomethane is stored in high pressure tanks (20-25 MPa). Technical challenges that apply to this technology mostly relate to methane slip (leakage) in the engine, which occurs either at the intake manifold level or in the combustion chamber due to incomplete combustion. Indirect injection engines have higher slip (5% unburnt methane in the exhaust gas) than direct high pressure injection engines (reference: Advanced Motor Fuels Technology Collaboration Programme Annex 51). For this technology to deliver net emissions reductions, the methane would need to be produced from renewable sources (e.g. biomethane or synthetic methane) and burned in direct injection high pressure gas engines, as use of fossil methane in current engine technologies has no CO2 emissions benefit relative to diesel powertrains.
Exhaust gas after-treatment catalysts should also be improved to increase the conversion rates of unburnt methane, especially at low temperatures (e.g. during engine warm-up, or when the engine is running at low loads).
tech_0291_compressed-biogas.market_dynamics_summary_en:
The market for this technology is expected to remain niche because of the limited availability of renewable biomethane. Promising application opportunities are municipal fleets supplied by biomethane from municipal or agricultural waste.
In the case of the cost-competitive, abundant production of synthetic methane (from electrolysis from low-carbon electricity with a carbon-source) in the future and in countries where gas grids already exist, this technology could be applied on a wider scale.

In the case of the cost-competitive, abundant production of synthetic methane (from electrolysis from low-carbon electricity with a carbon-source) in the future and in countries where gas grids already exist, this technology could be applied on a wider scale.

tech_0292_liquefied-biogas.sector_zh: 先進燃燒引擎與動力系統
tech_0292_liquefied-biogas.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術可應用於通常為重型應用、由內燃機驅動並以生質甲烷為燃料的車輛，液態生質甲烷儲存於低溫槽中，可實現比壓縮甲烷更高的能量密度，是長途卡車的具成本效益解決方案，液化生質甲烷動力系統（包含專用活塞引擎、直噴裝置與低溫槽）面臨若干技術挑戰：隔熱等。
tech_0292_liquefied-biogas.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 由於可再生生質甲烷供應有限，此技術的市場預期將維持利基規模，具前景的應用機會為由市政或農業廢棄物供應生質甲烷的市政車隊。若合成甲烷（由低碳電力電解）能以具成本競爭力且豐富的方式生產，前景可望更廣。
tech_0292_liquefied-biogas.technology_status_summary_en:
This technology can be applied in vehicles (typically for heavy-duty applications) powered by an internal combustion engine, and fuelled by biomethane. The liquid biomethane is stored in cryogenic tanks, which enables it to be stored at a higher energy density than compressed methane, and is a cost-efficient solution for long-haul trucks. The liquefied biomethane powertrain, including dedicated piston engine, direct injection device and cryogenic tank, faces some technological challenges:
* insulation of on-board liquefied biogas (LBG) storage in cryogenic tank (-162 °C)
* risk of methane slip and incomplete methane combustion (requires combustion system optimisation and dedicated exhaust after-treatment system) 
Methane slip can also occur from liquefied natural gas (LNG) tank, which releases the evaporative phase methane under high pressure if the engine is not capable of using it.
Exhaust gas after-treatment catalysts should also be improved to increase the conversion rates of unburnt methane, especially at low temperatures (e.g. during engine warm-up, or when the engine is running at low loads).
tech_0292_liquefied-biogas.market_dynamics_summary_en:
The market for this technology is expected to remain niche because of the limited availability of renewable biomethane. Promising application opportunities are municipal fleets supplied by biomethane from municipal or agricultural waste.
In the case of the cost-competitive, abundant production of synthetic methane (from electrolysis from low-carbon electricity with a carbon-source) in the future and in countries where gas grids already exist, this technology could be applied on a wider scale.

tech_0293_flex-fuel-and-hybrid-flex-fuel.technology_name_zh: 彈性燃料與混合動力彈性燃料車輛
tech_0293_flex-fuel-and-hybrid-flex-fuel.sector_zh: 先進燃燒引擎與動力系統
tech_0293_flex-fuel-and-hybrid-flex-fuel.technology_status_summary_zh: 彈性燃料車輛（FFV）或雙燃料車輛（稱為彈性燃料車輛），其內燃機設計為可使用一種以上燃料，兩種燃料儲存於同一油箱，燃料成分感測器讓引擎能依燃料調整噴射與點火時機，多數彈性燃料車輛使用汽油與乙醇，或汽油與甲醇，當使用生質乙醇或以生質原料為基礎的合成甲醇或合成液態燃料時。
tech_0293_flex-fuel-and-hybrid-flex-fuel.technology_status_summary_en: A flexible-fuel vehicle (FFV) or dual-fuel vehicle (referred to as a flex-fuel vehicle) has an internal combustion engine that is designed to run on more than one fuel. Both fuels are stored in the same tank and a fuel composition sensor allows the engine to adjust its fuel injection and spark timing to the fuel. Most flex-fuel vehicles run either on gasoline and ethanol or gasoline and methanol. When running on bioethanol or fuels such as synthetic methanol or synthetic liquid fuels that are based on hydrogen from renewable electricity, lifecycle emissions can potentially be low.
tech_0293_flex-fuel-and-hybrid-flex-fuel.description: A flexible-fuel vehicle (FFV) or dual-fuel vehicle (referred to as a flex-fuel vehicle) has an internal combustion engine that is designed to run on more than one fuel. Both fuels are stored in the same tank and a fuel composition sensor allows the engine to adjust its fuel injection and spark timing to the fuel. Most flex-fuel vehicles run either on gasoline and ethanol or gasoline and methanol. When running on bioethanol or fuels such as synthetic methanol or synthetic liquid fuels that are based on hydrogen from renewable electricity, lifecycle emissions can potentially be low.

tech_0294_methanol-fuelled-engine.sector_zh: 先進燃燒引擎與動力系統
tech_0294_methanol-fuelled-engine.technology_status_summary_zh: 甲醇引擎（即所謂「M100」，指以100%甲醇為燃料的車輛）在設計上與汽油引擎相似，僅需適度調整：材料相容性以防腐蝕、調整噴射系統、專用冷啟動裝置與策略，以及調整廢氣後處理裝置。由於燃料辛烷值高，甲醇引擎可受益於高壓縮比，進而提升熱效率，甚至可能高於柴油引擎。
tech_0294_methanol-fuelled-engine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 以永續方式取得的生質原料供應有限，且相較其他替代低排放運輸燃料選項，生產合成甲醇的成本相對較高。
tech_0294_methanol-fuelled-engine.technology_status_summary_en:
Methanol engines (so-called ''M100'', as vehicles fuelled by 100% methanol) are similar in design to gasoline engines, with moderate changes: material compatibility to prevent corrosion, adapted injection system, a dedicated cold start device and strategy, and adapted after-treatment for exhaust gas. Due to the high octane of the fuel, methanol engines can benefit from a high compression ratio, thus increasing thermal efficiency, possibly up to higher levels than for diesel engines. Methanol engines generate very low particulate emissions levels due to the molecule's specificity of having a single carbon atom - just as in the case of methane. The methanol is in liquid form at standard temperature and pressure, making it relatively easy to handle and store, although it is toxic for humans. 
Methanol can be produced as a biofuel or as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-carbon electricity with a carbon source). However, the availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited.
tech_0294_methanol-fuelled-engine.market_dynamics_summary_en: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel is quite high relative to other alternative low-emission fuel options for transport.
tech_0294_methanol-fuelled-engine.description:
Methanol engines (so-called ''M100'', as vehicles fuelled by 100% methanol) are similar in design to gasoline engines, with moderate changes: material compatibility to prevent corrosion, adapted injection system, a dedicated cold start device and strategy, and adapted after-treatment for exhaust gas. Due to the high octane of the fuel, methanol engines can benefit from a high compression ratio, thus increasing thermal efficiency, possibly up to higher levels than for diesel engines. Methanol engines generate very low particulate emissions levels due to the molecule's specificity of having a single carbon atom - just as in the case of methane. The methanol is in liquid form at standard temperature and pressure, making it relatively easy to handle and store, although it is toxic for humans. 
Methanol can be produced as a biofuel or as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-carbon electricity with a carbon source). However, the availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited.
tech_0294_methanol-fuelled-engine.NZErationale: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel is quite high relative to other alternative low-emission fuel options for transport.

initiatives.description: In 2012, the Chinese government initiated a methanol vehicle pilot program led by the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT). In 2019 a government plan was launched to expand the ''M100'' fleet. The Chinese car manufacturer Geely has invested in factories with production capacity of more than 300 000 methanol cars annually.
In 2019, Chinese truck maker Geely released the first Methanol (M100)-powered heavy duty truck.
In 2025, Chinese OEM Geely claims to have launched a cumulative 50,000 methanol vehicles since the release of its first model.

tech_0295_platooning-and-road-train.technology_status_summary_zh: 車輛編隊與道路列車是透過降低空氣動力阻力，減少道路貨運能耗的解決方案。車輛編隊是指卡車緊密跟隨彼此，配備最先進的駕駛輔助系統，透過智慧車輛通訊與自動化（CAV）技術形成車隊，讓卡車能以接近恆定的速度更緊密行駛，降低空氣阻力（進而降低油耗）並提升道路容量。
tech_0295_platooning-and-road-train.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 無論卡車動力系統技術為何，車輛編隊都是具成本效益的策略（「智慧」技術），可協助將道路貨運部門的能耗降低4-15%，並可緩解交通壅塞。
tech_0295_platooning-and-road-train.technology_status_summary_en: Platooning and road trains are solutions to reduce energy consumption in road freight transport by reducing aerodynamic drag. Platooning refers to trucks that closely follow each other, and are equipped with state-of-the-art driving support, forming a platoon of trucks driven by smart vehicle communication and automation (CAV) technologies. This allows trucks to drive closer together at near-constant speeds, which reduces air resistance (and thereby fuel consumption) and increases the capacity of roads. The fuel savings of truck platooning are estimated to range from 5% (at 20 metres distance) to 15% (at 4 metres between trucks) for a three-truck platoon travelling at 80 km/h. Among the challenges is the operation of multi-brand trucks in a single platoon, requiring communication standards. A road train consists of several trailers hauled by a single tractor.
tech_0295_platooning-and-road-train.market_dynamics_summary_en:
Whatever the truck powertrain technology, platooning is a cost-effective strategy (''smart'' technology'). It can contribute to the reduction of energy consumption of the road freight sector by 4-15% and can reduce traffic congestion.
(Sources: https://clepa.eu/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/CLEPA-Platooning-Panel-A4-V4-HD.pdf ; http://www.nrel.gov/transportation/fleettest-platooning.html )

Platooning refers to trucks that closely follow each other, and are equipped with state-of-the-art driving support, forming a platoon of trucks driven by smart vehicle communication and automation (CAV) technologies. This allows trucks to drive closer together at near-constant speeds, which reduces air resistance (and thereby fuel consumption) and increases the capacity of roads. The fuel savings of truck platooning are estimated to range from 5% (at 20 metres distance) to 15% (at 4 metres between trucks) for a three-truck platoon travelling at 80 km/h. Among the challenges is the operation of multi-brand trucks in a single platoon, requiring communication standards. A road train consists of several trailers hauled by a single tractor.

(Sources: https://clepa.eu/wp-content/uploads/2017/10/CLEPA-Platooning-Panel-A4-V4-HD.pdf ; http://www.nrel.gov/transportation/fleettest-platooning.html )

initiatives.description: Pilot project to test semiautonomous  platooning through a joint initiative between Ohio and Indian Departments of Transportation
ENSEMBLE project to test multi-brand platooing
Pony.ai was selected as the first company in China to start testing Autonomous truck platooning, in one year their robotrucks drove more than 5 million km in tests

tech_0296_li-air-battery.technology_status_summary_zh: 此概念中，空氣中的氧氣作為正極，鋰作為負極，這種材料組合的理論能量密度與液態燃料同等數量級。然而目前有多項重大技術障礙，使任何設計都無法達到如此高的密度，雖然此技術在理論上極具前景，但其實際可行性與可行性能仍待驗證。
tech_0296_li-air-battery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 除鋰離子外，能量密集、動力密集且長途運輸應用的車輛電氣化（包括飛機與長途船舶）可能需要能量密度更高的技術。
tech_0296_li-air-battery.technology_status_summary_en: In this concept the oxygen in the air would act as the cathode and lithium as the anode. This combination of materials offers a theoretical energy density of the same order of magnitude as liquid fuels. However, there are several significant technical barriers that prevent any design from reaching such a high density. While this technology is very promising in theory, its practical feasibility and viable performance are still to be demonstrated.
tech_0296_li-air-battery.market_dynamics_summary_en: Beyond lithium-ion, technologies that have higher energy density might be needed to electrify vehicles that operate in energy- and power-intensive and long-distance operations, including aircrafts and long-haul ships.
tech_0296_li-air-battery.description: In this concept the oxygen in the air would act as the cathode and lithium as the anode. This combination of materials offers a theoretical energy density of the same order of magnitude as liquid fuels. However, there are several significant technical barriers that prevent any design from reaching such a high density. While this technology is very promising in theory, its practical feasibility and viable performance are still to be demonstrated.
tech_0296_li-air-battery.NZErationale: Beyond lithium-ion, technologies that have higher energy density might be needed to electrify vehicles that operate in energy- and power-intensive and long-distance operations, including aircrafts and long-haul ships.

initiatives.description: A lithium-air battery able to operate at room temperature is developed.

tech_0297_li-s-battery.technology_status_summary_zh: 這種電池通常以鋰作為負極，正極則以硫製成，此概念提供實現極高重量能量密度的前景，且不需要昂貴的正極材料，因為硫的成本非常低廉且蘊藏豐富，原型電芯的能量密度已達400 Wh/kg以上。然而其較低的體積能量密度與目前較短的循環壽命，限制了其對主流電動車應用的吸引力。
tech_0297_li-s-battery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 除鋰離子外，需要能量密度更高、材料成本更低的技術，以實現能量與動力密集且長途運輸應用（包括飛機、船舶與重型卡車）的車輛電氣化。
tech_0297_li-s-battery.technology_status_summary_en: This battery typically uses lithium as an anode while the cathode is made of sulphur. This concept offers the prospect of achieving a very high gravimetric energy density and does not require expensive cathode materials, as sulphur is very inexpensive and abundant. Prototype cells have already been developed with energy densities above 400 Wh/kg. However, their lower volumetric energy density and currently short cycle life limit their attractiveness for mainstream EV applications. Defence may prove an early adopter, if technical obstacles such as short cycle life and safety concerns can be addressed.
tech_0297_li-s-battery.market_dynamics_summary_en: Beyond lithium-ion, technologies that have higher energy density and lower material costs are needed to electrify vehicles that operate in energy- and power-intensive and long-distance operations, including aircrafts, ships and heavy-duty (e.g. drayage, regional and long-haul) trucks.
tech_0297_li-s-battery.description: This battery typically uses lithium as an anode while the cathode is made of sulphur. This concept offers the prospect of achieving a very high gravimetric energy density and does not require expensive cathode materials, as sulphur is very inexpensive and abundant. Prototype cells have already been developed with energy densities above 400 Wh/kg. However, their lower volumetric energy density and currently short cycle life limit their attractiveness for mainstream EV applications. Defence may prove an early adopter, if technical obstacles such as short cycle life and safety concerns can be addressed.
