initiatives.description: In June 2020, EU commission mentioned the strong and weak points about Li-Sufar battery as the result of Horizon, Research and Innovation project.

In March 2021, "2021 roadmap pm lithium sulfur batteries" was published as a technical paper on Journal of Physics Energy. It gave an implication of the possibility LiS batteries would be used in  the aerospace sector.
In May 2023, Stellantis announced to invest in Lyten’s Breakthrough Lithium-Sulphur EV Battery Technology.
In 2024, US-based startup Lyten announced plans to invest more than 1 billion US dollars to build the world’s first Lithium-Sulfur battery gigafactory.
In 2024, Stellantis announced to partner with Zeta Energy to bring lithium-suplur batteries to the market.

China’s GNE develops a prototype of lithium-sulfur battery, claiming an energy density of 700Wh/kg, more than double than of state-of-the-art lithium-ion batteries, but without reporting information on volumetric energy density or costs.
Researchers in the United States found that the addition of iodine into the sulphur structure to increase its electronic conductivity and the overall batter cycle life.
In 2024, Chrysler announced that will test Lythen lithium-sulphur batteries for its Halcyon concept car.

In 2025, researchers between Germany and China identified a material acting as redox mediator and accelerating solid-solid sulfur reaction in all solid-state lithium-sulfur batteries, demonstrating high cycle life and opening up a potential pathway for the development of these type of batteries in the future.

tech_0298_lithium-ion-battery.technology_status_summary_zh: 鋰離子電池是電動車應用、電池儲能系統（BESS）、可攜式及其他電子裝置的主導電池技術。2024年，電動車電池部署約占電池需求的四分之三，電池儲能系統占15%，可攜式電子產品占5%，電動自行車與無人機等其他應用占5%。鋰離子電池的核心元件是電芯，可有不同尺寸格式（方形、圓柱形或軟包）。
tech_0298_lithium-ion-battery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 鋰離子電池目前是汽車業與電池儲能系統領域的主導電池技術，支撐道路運輸電氣化，並支持電網中間歇性再生能源占比提升，預期未來十年仍將維持主導技術地位，儘管可能由其他技術補充。
tech_0298_lithium-ion-battery.technology_status_summary_en:
Lithium-ion (Li-ion) is the dominant battery technology for electric vehicle (EV) applications, battery energy storage systems (BESS),  portable and other electronic devices. In 2024, EV battery deployment accounted for about three-qaurters of battery demand, against 15% for BESS, 5% for portable electronics, and 5% for other applications, such as electric bikes and drones. The core element of lithium-ion batteries is the cell, which can have different size formats (prismatic, cylindrical, or pouch), which is assembled in modules and packs for uses in EVs, in full containerized systems for BESS, or used as such in portable electronics. Battery cells have three key components are the anode, cathode, and electrolyte. The anode and cathode store the lithium-ion, which are transfered from one to the other during battery discharge and charge, while the electrolyte ensure the movement of the li-ion from the anode to the cathode (and vice versa) while insultaing them electronically. The anode is typically composed of graphite with increasing shares of silicon, and various cathode chemistries coexist (the most common being the lithium iron phosphate (LFP) and nickel-based chemistries - lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide (NMC) and lithium nickel cobalt aluminium oxide (NCA)). Within NMC cathodes, the elements can be found in various proportions. These range, for example, from NMC333, with equal shares of Ni, Mn and Co, to NMC811, with composition ratios of Ni, Mn, and Co of 8:1:1, with a current trend towards moving to lower cobalt content and higher nickel content. In 2024, LFP accounted for almost half of the EV battery market, with most of the rest using NMC batteries. LFP also accounted for over 90% of BESS installations in the same year. Portable electronics are still dominated by the older lithium cobalt oxyde (LCO) thanks to its high volumetric energy density.
Li-ion is already a mature technology, but it innovation continue at a fast pace. Recent innovation in LFP technologies, particularly starting from its 4th generation, are increasing the importance of this chemistry to decrease EV production costs as they now enable largely sufficient electric ranges and longer lifetime while being less costly than their NMC eqquivalent, albeit the latter still provide greater energy density and therefore range. At the same time, safety standards are increasing, battery lifetime is demonstrating to be even longer than what initially predicted by manufacturers, and production costs continue to decline. The 2024 sales-weighted average cost was around USD 115/kWh at pack level. The best energy density of this technology is around 250 Wh/kg at the EV battery pack level for NMC batteries, and 200 Wh/Kg for LFP batteries.
tech_0298_lithium-ion-battery.market_dynamics_summary_en: Li-ion batteries are the dominant battery technology in the automotive and battery energy storage system sectors today, underpining road trasport electriffication and support greater share in intermitted renewables in power grids. It is expected to remain the dominant technology in the next decade to come, even if it could be complemented by other technologies such as sodium-ion and solid-state batteries.

initiatives.description: First clarification of reaction types supporting lithium-ion batteries.
Development of the first cathode material powering first lithium-ion batteries, still used today.
The first development of the rocking chair theory, the fundation and key working principle of  lithium-ion batteries.
The first  unsymmetrical intercalation cell that shuttled Li ion back and forth was demonstrated.
Integration of intercalation cathode, graphitic anode, and carbonate electrolyte in the first commercially viable lithium-ion battery, led by Yoshino for Tokyo-based chemical company Asahi Kasei.
In 1991, Japanese electronics company Sony worked with Asahi Kasei to bring the world's first lithium-ion batteries to the market (for portable electronics).
Nissan introduced the Altra, the first series-produced electric car powered by lithium-ion batteries, making lithium-ion EV technology available beyond prototype fleets.
Development of lithium iron phosphate cathode, one of the two lithium-ion battery chemistry dominating the market today.
Development of lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxyde cathode, one of the two lithium-ion battery chemistry dominating the market today.
First EV developed for mass market using lithium-ion batteries, the Tesla Roadster.
First mass-produced EV using lithium-ion batteries, the Nissan Leaf.

First deployment of cell-to-pack technology, increeasing EV battery energy density and decreasing their costs. This technology opened the possibility to widespread adoption of LFP batteries in EV applications.
BYD launch of its prismatic blade battery, which also has a structural role in cell-to-pack configuration.

In 2023, China-based CATL unveiled the first 4th generation LFP battery for electric vehicles, offering 700 km range for large light-duty vehicles and the ability to recharge from 20% to 80% in 10 minutes.
Multi-graded anode microstructure to enable ultra-fast charging.
CATL launches condensed battery with an energy density of up to 500 Wh/kg, aiming electrification of passenger aircrafts.

In 2024, CATL unveiled TENER, a dedicated stationary storage battery with a capacity of 6.25 MWh with long cycle life and almost no degradation in the first 5 years of operation thanks to the use of additives to increase the battery lifetime.

First mass market deployment of cell-to-body technology, increeasing EV battery energy density and decreasing their costs. This technology opened the possibility to widespread adoption of LFP batteries in EV applications.
Deployment of MW-scale charging for electric car battereies, enabling up to 500 km of additional ranges in 5 minutes.

Development of No Propagation 3.0 technology, able to prevent battery fires or smoke and to continue power the vehicle for up to one hour in the event of thermal runaway because of a battery failure, provided the incident does not result from significant mechanical damage to the battery pack such as that caused by a vehicle collision.
First commissione commercial plant of LFP material production.
Nissan teamed up with Sony to launch the world's first lithium-ion–powered car — the Prairie Joy EV — although it remained a limited prototype intended for fleet use.

tech_0299_manganese-rich-cathode-battery.technology_status_summary_zh: 鋰離子電池的富錳正極有潛力以低成本錳大量取代鎳、鈷等昂貴關鍵礦物，這些正極對降低鎳鈷錳氧化物（NMC）電池生產成本具顯著優勢，縮小與磷酸鋰鐵（LFP）電池的差距，LFP電池也正朝更高錳含量發展，以在維持低生產成本的同時提升能量密度。
tech_0299_manganese-rich-cathode-battery.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 富錳化學體系有助於降低鎳/鈷需求，同時仍能實現更高能量密度，並縮小NMC與LFP電池的生產成本差距，有望在長期提供更多市場多元化。
tech_0299_manganese-rich-cathode-battery.technology_status_summary_en: Manganese-rich cathodes for Li-ion can potentially substitute significant amounts of expensive critical minerals such as nickel and cobalt with low-cost manganese. These cathodes have significant advantages for reducing lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxyde (NMC) battery production costs, reducing the gap with lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries. Higher manganese content is also being pursued in LFP batteries to increase their energy density while maintaining low production costs. Two significant manganese-rich chemistries include lithium nickel manganese oxide (LNMO) and lithium-manganese-rich NMC (LMR-NMC). LNMO contains no cobalt and has a notably higher energy density than LFP (though lower than high-nickel chemistries). LMR-NMC actually has a higher energy density than many high-nickel chemistries due to being lithium-rich (increasing lithium exposure) but it contains significantly less nickel than the top performance high-nickel chemistries.
tech_0299_manganese-rich-cathode-battery.market_dynamics_summary_en: Manganese-rich chemistries can be important to reduce nickel/cobalt demand while still achieving higher energy densities while reducing the production cost gap between NMC and LFP batteries, potentially offering more long term market diversification.
tech_0299_manganese-rich-cathode-battery.description: Manganese-rich cathodes for Li-ion can potentially substitute significant amounts of expensive critical minerals such as nickel and cobalt with low-cost manganese. These cathodes have significant advantages for reducing lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxyde (NMC) battery production costs, reducing the gap with lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries. Higher manganese content is also being pursued in LFP batteries to increase their energy density while maintaining low production costs. Two significant manganese-rich chemistries include lithium nickel manganese oxide (LNMO) and lithium-manganese-rich NMC (LMR-NMC). LNMO contains no cobalt and has a notably higher energy density than LFP (though lower than high-nickel chemistries). LMR-NMC actually has a higher energy density than many high-nickel chemistries due to being lithium-rich (increasing lithium exposure) but it contains significantly less nickel than the top performance high-nickel chemistries.
tech_0299_manganese-rich-cathode-battery.NZErationale: Manganese-rich chemistries can be important to reduce nickel/cobalt demand while still achieving higher energy densities while reducing the production cost gap between NMC and LFP batteries, potentially offering more long term market diversification.

initiatives.description: In September 2022, Nano One and Euro Manganese  have jointly demonstrated cathode materials made directly from Euro Manganese sourced electrolytic manganese metal using Nano One’s One-Pot Process. In this first milestone, pilot plant samples from Euro Manganese’s Chvaletice Manganese Project have been successfully validated as feedstock for Nano One’s patented One-Pot and M2CAM cathode production technologies.

In January 2022, Arkema and Morrow have signed a Memorandum of Understanding for the development, screening and testing of novel electrolyte formulations for the next generation of high voltage batteries, including LNMO.
Umicore starts pilot production of mangnanese rich NMC.
General Motors and LG Energy Solutions developed lithium-manganese-rich prismatic battery, aiming at commercialization in 2028.
Ford has announced a breakthrough in lithium-manganese-rich battery technology, which the company is now working to scale up for commercial application.

tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.technology_name: Low silicon content graphite anode battery
tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.technology_name_zh: 低矽含量石墨負極電池
tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.technology_status_summary_zh: 矽是一種有前景的負極材料，可與傳統石墨負極整合或取代之，矽的儲鋰容量約為石墨的十倍，代表其在提升能量密度方面具有龐大潛力，低矽含量（低於10%）可為負極能量密度帶來漸進式提升。
tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 石墨負極摻雜矽可提升負極、進而提升電池的能量密度，實現更長續航里程，並有望降低每千瓦時的價格。
tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.technology_status_summary_en: Silicon is a promising anode material that can either be integrated with conventional graphite anode, or substitute it. The lithium storage capacity of silicon is roughly ten times higher than that of graphite, meaning that it holds great potential for increased energy density. Low silicon content (<10%) provides an incremental improvement in anode energy density.
tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.market_dynamics_summary_en: Silicon doping in graphite anodes increase the energy density of the anode and therefore of the battery, enabling longer ranges and potentially decreasing prices per kWh.
tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.description: Silicon is a promising anode material that can either be integrated with conventional graphite anode, or substitute it. The lithium storage capacity of silicon is roughly ten times higher than that of graphite, meaning that it holds great potential for increased energy density. Low silicon content (<10%) provides an incremental improvement in anode energy density.
tech_0300_low-silicon-content-graphite-a.NZErationale: Silicon doping in graphite anodes increase the energy density of the anode and therefore of the battery, enabling longer ranges and potentially decreasing prices per kWh.

tech_0301_medium-high-silicon-content-an.technology_status_summary_zh: 矽是一種有前景的負極材料，可與傳統石墨負極整合或取代之，矽的儲鋰容量約為石墨的十倍，代表其在提升能量密度方面具有龐大潛力，中矽含量介於10%至50%之間，鋰離子電池要達到中高矽含量具有挑戰性，因矽顆粒的膨脹與收縮（可達原體積三倍以上）。
tech_0301_medium-high-silicon-content-an.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 負極矽含量提升（超過10%）可在不改變正極化學體系的情況下大幅提升電池能量密度，此技術若在2030年前部署，可能格外重要。
tech_0301_medium-high-silicon-content-an.technology_status_summary_en: Silicon is a promising anode material that can either be integrated with conventional graphite anode, or substitute it. The lithium storage capacity of silicon is roughly ten times higher than that of graphite, meaning that it holds great potential for increased energy density. Medium silicon content ranges from over 10 to 50%. Reaching medium to high silicon content in lithium-ion batteries is challenging because of the expansion and contraction of silicon particles (up to over three times their initial volume) during battery charging and discharging, even though strategies such as pre-lithiation can help reduce this range.
tech_0301_medium-high-silicon-content-an.market_dynamics_summary_en: Higher silicon content in anodes (>10%) can significantly increase the energy density of batteries without requiring a change to cathode chemistry; this technology can be particularly important if deployed in the period before 2030.
tech_0301_medium-high-silicon-content-an.description: Silicon is a promising anode material that can either be integrated with conventional graphite anode, or substitute it. The lithium storage capacity of silicon is roughly ten times higher than that of graphite, meaning that it holds great potential for increased energy density. Medium silicon content ranges from over 10 to 50%. Reaching medium to high silicon content in lithium-ion batteries is challenging because of the expansion and contraction of silicon particles (up to over three times their initial volume) during battery charging and discharging, even though strategies such as pre-lithiation can help reduce this range.
tech_0301_medium-high-silicon-content-an.NZErationale: Higher silicon content in anodes (>10%) can significantly increase the energy density of batteries without requiring a change to cathode chemistry; this technology can be particularly important if deployed in the period before 2030.

initiatives.description: In April 2021, Sionic Energy has relaunched complete advanced battery cell designs that incorporate its technologies into a drop-in, rapidly commercialized, low-cost, high performance, safer Li-ion technology. It's incorporate the company’s complete technology innovations that deliver up to 50% greater energy density, 30% lower cost, and increased safety, and can be integrated into cylindrical, pouch, or prismatic cell formats in existing cell production supply chains and infrastructure. Production-scale prototype cells will be available in Q4 2022 for customer evaluations and testing.

In October 2022, Solid Power announced to intends to commercialize high-content silicon cells in 2026 and internal testing shows the potential benefits.  Solid Power’s coin pouch cells had a stack-level-specific energy of ~350 Wh/Kg, despite Tesla’s industry-leading 2170 batteries provide 260 Wh/kg.

In April 2023, Sila, a next-generation battery materials company, announced the availability of its high-performance nano-composite silicon (NCS) anode, Titan Silicon. Titan Silicon can also dramatically improve battery charging time, charging a battery from 10% to 80% in just 20 minutes – even if your charge time is currently as long as 60 minutes.

In May 2022, Mercedes-Benz will incorporate silicon-based anodes battery in its upcoming electric G-Class . The battery, made by Sila Nanotechnologies is 20 percent to 40 percent more energy dense than comparable cells currently available and reaching more than 800 Wh/l at cell level, Mercedes said.

tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.technology_name: High energy density lithium-ion batteries (>400 wh/kg)
tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.technology_name_zh: 高能量密度鋰離子電池（逾400 Wh/kg）
tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.technology_status_summary_zh: 高能量密度（逾400 Wh/kg）鋰離子電池通常基於鋰金屬或矽負極。若鋰枝晶與體積膨脹等技術挑戰能獲得解決或安全管理，將有助於加速需要更長續航里程應用（如重型道路運輸）的電氣化。
tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 高能量密度（逾400 Wh/kg）鋰離子電池通常基於鋰金屬或矽負極。若鋰枝晶與體積膨脹等技術挑戰能獲得解決或安全管理，將有助於加速需要更長續航里程應用（如長途重型道路運輸）的電氣化。
tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.technology_status_summary_en: High energy density (>400 Wh/kg) lithium-ion batteries are typically based on either lithium metal or silicon anodes. If technical challenges such as lithium dendrites and volume expansion are solved or safely managed, they could enable faster electrification of applications requiring longer driving ranges, such as heavy-duty road transport.
tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.market_dynamics_summary_en: High energy density (>400 Wh/kg) lithium-ion batteries are typically based on either lithium metal or silicon anodes. If technical challenges such as lithium dendrites and volume expansion are solved or safely managed, they could enable faster electrification of applications requiring longer driving ranges, such as long-haul heavy-duty road transport.
tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.description: High energy density (>400 Wh/kg) lithium-ion batteries are typically based on either lithium metal or silicon anodes. If technical challenges such as lithium dendrites and volume expansion are solved or safely managed, they could enable faster electrification of applications requiring longer driving ranges, such as heavy-duty road transport.
tech_0302_high-energy-density-lithium-io.NZErationale: High energy density (>400 Wh/kg) lithium-ion batteries are typically based on either lithium metal or silicon anodes. If technical challenges such as lithium dendrites and volume expansion are solved or safely managed, they could enable faster electrification of applications requiring longer driving ranges, such as long-haul heavy-duty road transport.

initiatives.description: In April 2023, Sila, a next-generation battery materials company, announced the availability of its high-performance nano-composite silicon (NCS) anode, Titan Silicon. Titan Silicon can also dramatically improve battery charging time, charging a battery from 10% to 80% in just 20 minutes – even if your charge time is currently as long as 60 minutes.
CATL launches condensed battery with an energy density of up to 500 Wh/kg, aiming electrification of passenger aircrafts.
Coreshell Launches 60 Ah Battery Cells using domestically sourced metallurgical silicon anode.
Altech Batteries raised $4MM to develop a silicon-alumina anode pilot plant, with additional plans for a 4 GWh battery factory.
SES AI’s 100 Ah Li-Metal Is First to Successfully Pass the Global Electric Vehicle EV Safety Standard Test GB38031-2020.
SK On is developing polymer electrolytes for lithium metal batteries.

tech_0303_fast-charging.technology_status_summary_zh: 快充設備是為電動船舶電池充電所必需的，充電器可由插頭構成（通常具自動連接功能），或由無線裝置構成，電池電動渡輪主要在每次靠泊時充電，由於所需功率高（數兆瓦至數十兆瓦），通常會為電纜與連接器（港側與船側）採用主動冷卻系統。
tech_0303_fast-charging.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 為電動船舶充電所必需，由於能量密度與續航里程受限，此類船舶可能僅適用於短程航線。
tech_0303_fast-charging.technology_status_summary_en:
Fast charging equipment is necessary to recharge the batteries of electric ships. Chargers can be constituted by a plug, typically with automated connection, or by a wireless device.
Battery-electric ferries are mainly recharged on each docking.
Due to the high power needed (several MW to tens of MW), an active cooling system will typically be used for the cable and connector (on the port side and on the vessel side). Available technical solutions include water cooling, air cooling, other fluid cooling or super conductive cables.
tech_0303_fast-charging.market_dynamics_summary_en: Necessary to recharge electric ships, which due to the low energy density and range will likely find application only in short-distance routes.
tech_0303_fast-charging.description:
Fast charging equipment is necessary to recharge the batteries of electric ships. Chargers can be constituted by a plug, typically with automated connection, or by a wireless device.
Battery-electric ferries are mainly recharged on each docking.
Due to the high power needed (several MW to tens of MW), an active cooling system will typically be used for the cable and connector (on the port side and on the vessel side). Available technical solutions include water cooling, air cooling, other fluid cooling or super conductive cables.
tech_0303_fast-charging.NZErationale: Necessary to recharge electric ships, which due to the low energy density and range will likely find application only in short-distance routes.

initiatives.description: ABB provided the automated fast charging stations for five new electric ferries on the round-the-clock services crossing Amsterdam’s North Sea Canal. The ferries will operate constantly, day and night, with only three minutes allowed for battery charging after each 20-minute round trip.
Similarly, Wärtsilä provided the charging stations for Norwegian ferry operator Boreal Sjö. The 2 ferries recharge after every 20 mn round trip.
The electric ferry Ellen, operating in Southern Denmark, was comissionned in 2019 with a battery of  4.3 MWh and uses an automatic charger with a peak power of 4.4 MW,  for a turnaround time of 20mn.

Norwegian Electric Systems (NES) already installed 30 onshore charging stations for ferries with power up to 5 MW, and was awarded a contract by Norwegian ferry operator Norled to supply 3 DC chargers based on the Megawatt Charging System (MCS) of 4MW each, expected to be operational from 2027.

Scandlines’ new freight-oriented RoPax for the Rødby–Puttgarden route, the Baltic Whaler has been delivered in October 2025. It will operate fully electrically via a 10-MWh battery pack. It will be able to charge in just 17 minutes in Rødbyhavn, Denmark, thanks to investment in a powerful 50 kV / 25 MW power cable and a new transformer station. A complete charging system has also been installed in Puttgarden, Germany, so that from the end of 2025, the ferry will be able to charge in just 12 minutes.

The world’s largest fully electric ferry China Zorrilla’s is expected to start operations in early 2026. Its charging infrastructure at both Buenos Aires and Montevideo ports is built around ultra-high-capacity DC fast chargers, specifically designed to handle the vessel’s massive 40 MWh battery system. These chargers can deliver several megawatts of instantaneous power, enabling the ferry to recharge from 20% to 80% (about 24 MWh) in just 40 minutes during turnaround between trips.

tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.technology_status_summary_zh: 氨相較氫氣更易儲存，可以液態形式儲存於加壓槽中（1百萬帕，常溫），或於常壓下以攝氏零下33度儲存（氫氣則需零下253度），僅需製冷技術，無需低溫技術。氨的體積能量含量高於氫氣（多70%），氨目前已在全球大量生產與運輸，因此理論上加注供應可輕易調度。
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 氨相較氫氣更易儲存，並受惠於既有的基礎設施與配送網絡（因其工業用途，主要用於肥料合成），若廣泛應用於航運業則需擴大規模，低生產成本且不排放二氧化碳，使氨成為極具前景的低排放燃料。
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.technology_status_summary_en:
Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen. It can be stored in a liquid form in pressurised tanks (1 Mpa, ambient temperature) or at ambient pressure with a temperature of -33 °C (instead of -253 °C for H2). Refrigeration techniques are required, not cryogenics. Ammonia offers a higher volumetric energy content compared to hydrogen (+70%).
Ammonia is already produced and transported in large quantities around the world. Therefore bunker supplies could, in theory, be readily accommodated. 
Ammonia is very toxic and thus specific design and operation measures need to be implemented: no bunkering at the same time as cargo loading/unloading; layout with ample space and minimized distances; if possible, remote operation to reduce personnel exposure; special procedures and protective equipment for the personnel; purging of the lines with inert gas and the possibility to drain ammonia back from ship to bunker.
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_en: Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen, and benefits from an already existing infrastructure and distribution network (due to its industrial use, mainly for fertiliser synthesis)  which would need scaling up in case of broad application in shipping. Low production costs and lack of carbon dioxide emissions make ammonia a very promising low-emission fuel option, especially for long voyages, provided that safety and toxicity issues can be adequately addressed.
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.description:
Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen. It can be stored in a liquid form in pressurised tanks (1 Mpa, ambient temperature) or at ambient pressure with a temperature of -33 °C (instead of -253 °C for H2). Refrigeration techniques are required, not cryogenics. Ammonia offers a higher volumetric energy content compared to hydrogen (+70%).
Ammonia is already produced and transported in large quantities around the world. Therefore bunker supplies could, in theory, be readily accommodated. 
Ammonia is very toxic and thus specific design and operation measures need to be implemented: no bunkering at the same time as cargo loading/unloading; layout with ample space and minimized distances; if possible, remote operation to reduce personnel exposure; special procedures and protective equipment for the personnel; purging of the lines with inert gas and the possibility to drain ammonia back from ship to bunker.
tech_0304_ammonia-bunkering.NZErationale: Ammonia is easier to store than hydrogen, and benefits from an already existing infrastructure and distribution network (due to its industrial use, mainly for fertiliser synthesis)  which would need scaling up in case of broad application in shipping. Low production costs and lack of carbon dioxide emissions make ammonia a very promising low-emission fuel option, especially for long voyages, provided that safety and toxicity issues can be adequately addressed.

initiatives.description: In 2024, the first ship fuelling with 5 m³ of ammonia was carried out in Singapore by Fortescue, an Australian mining company,  with the support of the Maritime Port Authority.
In 2024, Trafigura, a commodity trading company, performed the first ship-to-ship (STS) transfer of ammonia in the strait of Gibraltar.

Trafigura, a commodity trading company, performed the first ship-to-ship (STS) transfer of ammonia in the strait of Gibraltar. Approximately 6,000 metric tonnes of ammonia were transferred between the Green Power medium gas carrier (MGC), and Gas Aegean, a small gas carrier.

Japanese shipping company NYK achieved a landmark ship-to-ship transfer of 23,000 tons of ammonia, from the ammonia carrier Berlian Ekuator  to the ammonia carrier Eco Enchanted, on the high seas off the coast of Ceuta, Spain.

A successful pilot ship-to-ship bunkering was conducted in the port of Rotterdam, where 800 m³ of ammonia at -33°C were transferred over the course of 2.5 h. The demonstration was an important step in the port's readiness and validated its safety framework for ammonia bunkering.

In September 2024, Pilbara Ports, Yara, and Singapore´s Global Centre for Maritime Decarbonisation completed the world’s first ship-to-ship ammonia transfer at anchorage, exchanging 2 700 tonnes of ammonia between a mid-sized and a handy-size gas carrier in the Port of Dampier, Australia.

tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.technology_status_summary_zh: 建立氫氣加注基礎設施，是將氫能推進系統引入船舶的重要一步。然而目前尚未設計出相關船舶，也尚無加注船標準可依循，其技術系統完全取決於氫氣儲存方式（液態或壓縮氣態）。配備液態氫加注槽的船舶需要液化氫供應，壓縮氣態則可由配備再氣化裝置的液態氫加注船補給。
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在淨零情境中，氫氣預期將在中小型船舶中扮演重要角色，氫氣加注基礎設施對確保氫能船舶運作至關重要。
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.technology_status_summary_en:
Establishing hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is an important step in introducing hydrogen propulsion in ships. However, vessels have not yet been designed, and there isn't currently a bunker vessel standard to work to. The technology systems depend entirely on the method for hydrogen storage (liquid or compressed gas). A vessel that has bunker tanks for liquid hydrogen needs a liquefied hydrogen supply. Compressed gas could be refuelled by a liquid hydrogen bunker vessel equipped with a regasification plant, or if stored as gas in the port, transferred by pressure balancing or compressing the gas into the ship. 
The choice of the hydrogen storage method has implications for the technology used to power the vessel.  A gas engine is preferred for liquid hydrogen as the excess heat from combustion can be used to evaporate hydrogen. Gaseous hydrogen generally works better with on-board fuel cells, even if it can also be made suitable for gas engines, particularly if co-fired with natural gas. 
Cost-effective liquefaction chains for hydrogen are key for bunkering, as liquid hydrogen is expected to offer advantages over pressurised hydrogen gas in relation to transportation costs. In contrast with LNG where the gas is transported into the port, the economics of hydrogen mean that hydrogen liquefaction plants are likely to be located close to port, requiring stronger integration between systems.
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_en: Hydrogen is expected to play an important role for small- and medium-size ships in the NZE Scenario. Hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is essential to guarantee the operation of hydrogen-fuelled vessels
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.description:
Establishing hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is an important step in introducing hydrogen propulsion in ships. However, vessels have not yet been designed, and there isn't currently a bunker vessel standard to work to. The technology systems depend entirely on the method for hydrogen storage (liquid or compressed gas). A vessel that has bunker tanks for liquid hydrogen needs a liquefied hydrogen supply. Compressed gas could be refuelled by a liquid hydrogen bunker vessel equipped with a regasification plant, or if stored as gas in the port, transferred by pressure balancing or compressing the gas into the ship. 
The choice of the hydrogen storage method has implications for the technology used to power the vessel.  A gas engine is preferred for liquid hydrogen as the excess heat from combustion can be used to evaporate hydrogen. Gaseous hydrogen generally works better with on-board fuel cells, even if it can also be made suitable for gas engines, particularly if co-fired with natural gas. 
Cost-effective liquefaction chains for hydrogen are key for bunkering, as liquid hydrogen is expected to offer advantages over pressurised hydrogen gas in relation to transportation costs. In contrast with LNG where the gas is transported into the port, the economics of hydrogen mean that hydrogen liquefaction plants are likely to be located close to port, requiring stronger integration between systems.
tech_0305_hydrogen-bunkering.NZErationale: Hydrogen is expected to play an important role for small- and medium-size ships in the NZE Scenario. Hydrogen bunkering infrastructure is essential to guarantee the operation of hydrogen-fuelled vessels

initiatives.description: India has set a deadline of 2035 to establish green hydrogen bunkering and refuelling facilities at 12 major ports in the drive to cut its carbon footprint, the shipping ministry said in guidelines issued in 2023. Three of its ports would initially have bunker facilities for green hydrogen and ammonia.
Since 2023, the hydrogen fuel cell ferry Hydra has been refuelling with hydrogen every 3 weeks, using quick connectors from Manntek operating at -253 degrees.

JERA Nex BP, a joint venture between energy major BP and Japan’s largest utility JERA, has commissioned a new hydrogen bunkering facility at the Port of Ostend. During its demonstration phase in 2025, the station in Ostend will initially serve one hydrogen-powered CTV, the Hydrocat 48, owned and operated by Windcat. However, the station can refuel several vessels per day.

tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.technology_status_summary_zh: 甲醇長期以來一直作為貨物處理，目前全球有許多港口設有甲醇儲存設施，哥德堡等多個港口已制定或正準備制定甲醇加注規則。短期內，船舶可透過槽車加注甲醇；長期解決方案可能是加注船，因其簡便靈活。
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 以永續方式取得的生質原料供應有限，且相較其他替代低排放運輸燃料選項，生產合成甲醇（由低碳電力電解並搭配碳源）的成本相對較高。然而此替代方案較高的技術成熟度使其較具吸引力。
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.technology_status_summary_en: Methanol is routinely handled as cargo. There are currently many ports with methanol storage facilities worldwide, and various ports – such as Gothenburg – have issued methanol bunkering rules or are preparing to do so.  In the short run, ships can bunker methanol from tank trucks. The long-term solution will probably be bunker vessels because of their simplicity and flexibility.
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.market_dynamics_summary_en: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-emission electricity with a carbon-source) is quite high relative to alternative low-emission fuel options for transport. However, the higher technologial maturity of this alternative makes it an important mid-term solution for shipping decarbonisation.
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.description: Methanol is routinely handled as cargo. There are currently many ports with methanol storage facilities worldwide, and various ports – such as Gothenburg – have issued methanol bunkering rules or are preparing to do so.  In the short run, ships can bunker methanol from tank trucks. The long-term solution will probably be bunker vessels because of their simplicity and flexibility.
tech_0306_methanol-bunkering.NZErationale: The availability of sustainably sourced biomass to produce methanol is limited and the cost of producing methanol as a synthetic fuel (from electrolysis from low-emission electricity with a carbon-source) is quite high relative to alternative low-emission fuel options for transport. However, the higher technologial maturity of this alternative makes it an important mid-term solution for shipping decarbonisation.

initiatives.description: In 2024, China's first methanol bunkering ship, Hai Gang Zhi Yuan, embarked on its maiden voyage. With 16 000 cbm, it is the largest in operation so far, and will start operation in Shanghai.
In 2023 in the port of Gothenburg, the ferry Stena Germanica was the first non-tanker vessel in the world to be bunkered with methanol ship to ship
In 2023, Maersk and CMA CGM have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to explore commercial feasibility of establishing a green methanol bunkering hub at the Port of Melbourne

The Port of Rotterdam served as the initial base for X-Press Feeder's "Green Baltic X-PRESS" due  to its infrastructure and capability to handle green methanol refueling. The company has also signed a MOU with the ports of: Antwerp Bruges, Klaipeda in Lithuania, Riga in Latvia, Tallinn in Estonia, Helsinki and HaminaKotka in Finland to promote shipping powered by green methanol.

X-Press Feeders, Global Energy Trading Pte Ltd (GET), and PSA Singapore (PSA) have successfully completed the first simultaneous methanol bunkering and cargo operation (SIMOPS) in Singapore on 27 May 2024. The SIMOPS was conducted at the new Tuas Port with the support of the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA). A X-Press Feeder container vessel on her maiden voyage from Asia to Europe was successfully refuelled with approximately 300 metric tonnes (MT) of bio-methanol.
Ulsan Port Authority (UPA) has successfully completed South Korea’s first methanol bunkering for  65,000 DWT dual-fuel bulk carrier Green Future.

tech_0307_cold-ironing.technology_status_summary_zh: 船舶靠泊時仍需要能源：如旅館用電（如郵輪）、冷藏（冷藏貨櫃）、船上吊車操作等。所謂「岸電供電」或替代海事電力（AMP）是指將船舶接上電網，取代運轉船舶輔助引擎，典型相關功率從滾裝船的數兆伏安到郵輪的逾10兆伏安不等，也能降低船上噪音與振動，延長機械壽命。
tech_0307_cold-ironing.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 降低二氧化碳排放（若使用低碳電力，減排效果更佳）以及港區的局部空氣污染，此處暴露程度最高。
tech_0307_cold-ironing.technology_status_summary_en:
When at berth, a ship still requires energy: for hotelling (e.g. cruise ships), refrigeration (refrigerated containers), on-board crane operation, etc.
So-called ''cold ironing'' or Alternate Maritime Power (AMP) consists of plugging in the vessel to the grid instead of running the auxiliary engine(s) of the ship.  Typical associated power can be in from several MVA for Ro-Ro ships, up to more than 10 MVA for cruise ships. 
This also reduces on-board noise and vibrations, extends machinery lifetime and reduces engine maintenance needs.
Dedicated installations are required in the harbour and on-board. These must manage the variations in electric features (voltage, frequency, connectors). In particular,  dedicated substation transformer of adequate rating is necessary, with secondary voltage of either 6.6 or 11.0 kV, and potentially a frequency transformer from 50 Hz to 60 Hz. The use of fuel cells for port-side power has already been demonstrated.
tech_0307_cold-ironing.market_dynamics_summary_en: Reduces CO2 emissions (reductions are greater with low-carbon electricity) and local air pollution in the harbour, where maximum exposure occurs.
tech_0307_cold-ironing.description:
When at berth, a ship still requires energy: for hotelling (e.g. cruise ships), refrigeration (refrigerated containers), on-board crane operation, etc.
So-called ''cold ironing'' or Alternate Maritime Power (AMP) consists of plugging in the vessel to the grid instead of running the auxiliary engine(s) of the ship.  Typical associated power can be in from several MVA for Ro-Ro ships, up to more than 10 MVA for cruise ships. 
This also reduces on-board noise and vibrations, extends machinery lifetime and reduces engine maintenance needs.
Dedicated installations are required in the harbour and on-board. These must manage the variations in electric features (voltage, frequency, connectors). In particular,  dedicated substation transformer of adequate rating is necessary, with secondary voltage of either 6.6 or 11.0 kV, and potentially a frequency transformer from 50 Hz to 60 Hz. The use of fuel cells for port-side power has already been demonstrated.
tech_0307_cold-ironing.NZErationale: Reduces CO2 emissions (reductions are greater with low-carbon electricity) and local air pollution in the harbour, where maximum exposure occurs.

initiatives.description: In 2023, the European Parliament and Council reached an agreement on the Alternative Fuel Infrastructure Regulation (AFIR). Maritime ports that see at least 50 port calls by large passenger vessels, or 100 port calls by container vessels, must provide shore-side electricity for such vessels by 2030.

Cold ironing has already been included and planned for in the National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP), with a considerable investment: 400 million euros to co-finance, from now until March 2026, the implementation of at least 15 cold ironing infrastructures to supply electricity to at least ten Italian ports within the Trans-European Transport Network (TEN-T). The European Commission recently gave the green light to a 570 million euros package of state aid from the Italian government, running until 2033, to incentivise ships to use energy from shore-side electricity grids while they are at berth in ports, wherever and whenever possible.
Greece’s maritime industry is advancing its green transition, with 12 domestic ports set to be equipped with cold ironing systems by 2029.
Germany’s Port of Hamburg has become the first port in Europe to offer shore power for both container and cruise vessels.

tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_name: Proton exchange membrane fuel cell electric ship
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_name_zh: 質子交換膜燃料電池電動船舶
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_status_summary_zh: 此類船舶以氫燃料電池運行，由於功率輸出有限，此技術可能較適用於中小型船舶，目前進行中的示範計畫也證實了這一點，燃料電池種類多樣，其名稱反映電解質膜所用材料。DNV GL評估了7種燃料電池技術，並得出結論：以下技術對海事應用最具前景。
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 燃料電池相較內燃式船用引擎具更高能源效率——雖然差距不如道路車輛那麼大——且不排放污染物，雖然質子交換膜（PEM）是汽車應用中最普及的燃料電池類型，但固態氧化物燃料電池等其他類型也具潛力。
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.technology_status_summary_en:
This type of vessel is operated by a hydrogen fuel cell. Due to limited power output, this technology is likely to be used preferably for small and medium vessels, as currently proven by on-going demonstrations.
Different fuel-cell types exist and their names reflect the materials used in the electrolyte membrane.
DNV GL evaluated 7 fuel-cell technologies and concluded that the following are the most promising for maritime applications.
According to the study from DNV-GL, the proton exchange membrane (PEM) fuel cell is considered a mature technology. Its operating temperature is 50-100 °C, and has a typical efficiency of 50-60% and a moderate lifetime. It has medium sensitivity to impurity, thus requiring hydrogen as a fuel, and a low cost (compared to other fuel cell technologies for maritime applications). 
Manufacturing compact fuel cells with high output power is quite challenging. This technology is therefore likely to be used preferably for small and medium vessels.
tech_0308_proton-exchange-membrane-fuel-.market_dynamics_summary_en:
Fuel cells have higher energy efficiency than internal combustion maritime engines - though the gap is not as high as it is in the case of road vehicles - and do not emit pollutants.

While proton exchange membranes (PEMs) are the most widespread fuel cell type for automotive applications, other types of fuel cells such as Solid Oxide Fuel Cells (SOFC) and alkaline seem more suitable for maritime applications (where high operative temperature requirements do not pose problems).

Possible applications could be in short distance inland shipping, rather than in oceangoing vessels.
