tech_0355_plasma-catalytic-processes-for.technology_name_zh: 電漿觸媒甲烷減排製程
tech_0355_plasma-catalytic-processes-for.technology_status_summary_zh: 電漿觸媒在多種氣體轉化應用中日益受到關注，例如將二氧化碳轉化為高附加價值化學品與燃料、將甲烷活化為氫氣、更高碳氫化合物或含氧化合物，以及氨的合成，其他應用已更為成熟，例如空氣污染控制（如揮發性有機化合物整治、懸浮微粒與氮氧化物去除），也可用於處理雙燃料船用引擎排放的未燃甲烷逸散。
tech_0355_plasma-catalytic-processes-for.technology_status_summary_en: Plasma catalysis is gaining increasing interest for various gas conversion applications, such as CO2 conversion into value-added chemicals and fuels, CH4 activation into hydrogen, higher hydrocarbons or oxygenates, and NH3 synthesis. Other applications are already more established, such as for air pollution control, e.g. volatile organic compound remediation, particulate matter and NOx removal. it can also be used to address Methane slip, the unburned methane released from dual-fuel marine engine s for LNG-fueled vessels.
tech_0355_plasma-catalytic-processes-for.description: Plasma catalysis is gaining increasing interest for various gas conversion applications, such as CO2 conversion into value-added chemicals and fuels, CH4 activation into hydrogen, higher hydrocarbons or oxygenates, and NH3 synthesis. Other applications are already more established, such as for air pollution control, e.g. volatile organic compound remediation, particulate matter and NOx removal. it can also be used to address Methane slip, the unburned methane released from dual-fuel marine engine s for LNG-fueled vessels.

initiatives.description: Approval in Principle (AiP) by Lloyd's Register (LR) for its Plasma-Catalytic technology after Pilot project - first shipboard plasma-catalytic methane abatement system deployed by Daphne Technology and Maran Gas in late October 2025

tech_0356_post-combustion-carbon-capture.technology_name_zh: 燃燒後碳捕捉（製程加熱爐）
tech_0356_post-combustion-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_zh: 製程加熱爐、鍋爐與公用設施約占煉油廠總二氧化碳排放的30-60%，從這些製程煙道氣中捕捉二氧化碳所需的流程與發電廠相似。氫氣生產約占煉油廠總二氧化碳排放的5-20%，以天然氣為基礎的製氫二氧化碳捕捉已在商業規模上得到驗證，但尚未應用於煉油廠。
tech_0356_post-combustion-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 以燃燒後捕捉改裝，是解決現有煉油廠二氧化碳排放的機會，但也取決於可用空間及取得二氧化碳封存或潛在二氧化碳用戶的管道；RECAP專案估計不同煉油廠配置與捕捉情境下的二氧化碳避免成本約為每噸160-210美元。
tech_0356_post-combustion-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_en:
Process heaters, boilers and utilities account for around 30-60% of the total CO2 emissions of a refinery. Processes needed to capture CO2 from the flue gas of these processes is similar to those in power generation.

Hydrogen production is responsible for around 5-20% of the total CO2 emissions of a refinery. CO2 capture from natural-gas based hydrogen production has been demonstrated on a commercial scale, but not yet been applied to a refinery.
tech_0356_post-combustion-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_en: Retrofitting with post-combustion capture is an opportunity to address CO2 emissions in existing refineries, but also depends on available space and access to CO2 storage or potential CO2 users; the RECAP project estimated the avoidance costs of CO2 from different refinery configurations and capture cases to be in the range of USD 160-210/tCO2.
tech_0356_post-combustion-carbon-capture.description:
Process heaters, boilers and utilities account for around 30-60% of the total CO2 emissions of a refinery. Processes needed to capture CO2 from the flue gas of these processes is similar to those in power generation.

Hydrogen production is responsible for around 5-20% of the total CO2 emissions of a refinery. CO2 capture from natural-gas based hydrogen production has been demonstrated on a commercial scale, but not yet been applied to a refinery.
tech_0356_post-combustion-carbon-capture.NZErationale: Retrofitting with post-combustion capture is an opportunity to address CO2 emissions in existing refineries, but also depends on available space and access to CO2 storage or potential CO2 users; the RECAP project estimated the avoidance costs of CO2 from different refinery configurations and capture cases to be in the range of USD 160-210/tCO2.

initiatives.description: Prax Lindsey Oil Refinery has joined the Viking CCS cluster, led by Harbour Energy, to capture and store around 1.1 MtCO2 per year from refinery heaters and process units. The captured CO2 will be transported via pipeline to the depleted Viking gas fields in the North Sea for permanent storage. The project will be implemented in two phases, with the first phase targeted for 2027 and the second for 2029.

tech_0357_oxy-fuelling-carbon-capture-cr.technology_status_summary_zh: 流體觸媒裂解（FCC）裝置約占典型煉油廠總二氧化碳排放的20-55%，煙道氣中的二氧化碳濃度約為8-20%（體積比），富氧燃燒可讓FCC裝置煙道氣中的二氧化碳濃縮並捕捉。
tech_0357_oxy-fuelling-carbon-capture-cr.market_dynamics_summary_zh: FCC裝置的燃燒後捕捉相較富氧燃燒捕捉的二氧化碳避免成本更低，但富氧燃燒具有提升FCC處理量的優勢。
tech_0357_oxy-fuelling-carbon-capture-cr.technology_status_summary_en: The fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit is responsible for 20-55% of total CO2 emissions from a typical refinery. CO2 concentration in the flue gas is around 8-20% (volumetric). Oxy-combustion enables the concentration and capture of CO2 in the flue gas from FCC units.
tech_0357_oxy-fuelling-carbon-capture-cr.market_dynamics_summary_en: Post-combustion capture at an FCC has lower CO2 avoidance costs than oxy-fuelling combustion, but oxy-fuelling combustion has the advantage of increasing the throughput of the FCC
tech_0357_oxy-fuelling-carbon-capture-cr.description: The fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit is responsible for 20-55% of total CO2 emissions from a typical refinery. CO2 concentration in the flue gas is around 8-20% (volumetric). Oxy-combustion enables the concentration and capture of CO2 in the flue gas from FCC units.
tech_0357_oxy-fuelling-carbon-capture-cr.NZErationale: Post-combustion capture at an FCC has lower CO2 avoidance costs than oxy-fuelling combustion, but oxy-fuelling combustion has the advantage of increasing the throughput of the FCC

tech_0358_post-combustion-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_zh: 流體觸媒裂解（FCC）裝置約占典型煉油廠總二氧化碳排放的20-55%，燃燒後技術可從體積二氧化碳濃度10-20%的煙道氣中捕捉二氧化碳，此技術已可取得，但尚未在煉油廠環境中得到驗證。
tech_0358_post-combustion-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 流體觸媒裂解（FCC）裝置的燃燒後捕捉相較富氧燃燒捕捉的二氧化碳避免成本更低，但富氧燃燒具有提升FCC處理量的優勢。
tech_0358_post-combustion-carbon-capture.technology_status_summary_en: The fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit is responsible for 20-55% of total CO2 emissions from a typical refinery. Post-combustion technology to capture the CO2 from the flue gas, with a volumetric CO2 concentration of 10-20% is available, but has not yet been demonstrated in a refinery context.
tech_0358_post-combustion-carbon-capture.market_dynamics_summary_en: Post-combustion capture at a fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit has lower CO2 avoidance costs than oxy-fuelling combustion, but oxy-fuelling combustion has the advantage of increasing the throughput of the FCC.
tech_0358_post-combustion-carbon-capture.description: The fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit is responsible for 20-55% of total CO2 emissions from a typical refinery. Post-combustion technology to capture the CO2 from the flue gas, with a volumetric CO2 concentration of 10-20% is available, but has not yet been demonstrated in a refinery context.
tech_0358_post-combustion-carbon-capture.NZErationale: Post-combustion capture at a fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit has lower CO2 avoidance costs than oxy-fuelling combustion, but oxy-fuelling combustion has the advantage of increasing the throughput of the FCC.

initiatives.description: EET Fuels is developing an Industrial Carbon Capture (ICC) facility at the Stanlow refinery, integrating Topsoe’s SNOX™ flue gas treatment system, combined with MHI’s capture technology. The plant could capture about 1 MtCO2 per year from the refinery’s full-residue fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit, with permanent storage planned in depleted gas fields in Liverpool Bay via the HyNet cluster. Toyo Engineering India is conducting the FEED phase, with start-up targeted for 2028.

Svante Technologies will deploy its solid sorbent-based carbon capture system at Delek US’s Big Spring refinery in Texas. Supported by up to $95 million in DOE cost-shared funding, the project will capture around 145 000 tCO2 per year from the refinery’s fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) unit. The first-of-a-kind commercial-scale plant will demonstrate Svante’s solid sorbent filter technology for CO2 capture, with startup planned for 2027–2028.

tech_0359_advanced-acid-gas-removal.technology_status_summary_zh: 先進酸性氣體去除潔淨技術，聚焦於低能耗溶劑系統（如混合型與高容量胺類），可減少蒸汽用量，進而降低再生過程的二氧化碳排放，同時也將碳氫化合物與溶劑損耗降至最低，並搭配高效硫回收或濕式氣體硫酸製程，將廢氣轉化為可銷售的硫或硫酸，同時回收熱能，逐步達到更高的二氧化碳濃度也是漸進式改良的一部分。
tech_0359_advanced-acid-gas-removal.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在2030年前削減油氣加工的二氧化碳排放，以及能源部門甲烷排放75%，是短期內限制全球暖化最具成本效益的機會之一，此為降低油氣甲烷排放的可行技術之一，可再生酸性氣體去除化學品產業在其中扮演關鍵角色。
tech_0359_advanced-acid-gas-removal.technology_status_summary_en: Advanced acid gas removal clean technologies focus on lower‑energy solvent systems, such as hybrid and high‑capacity amines, that cut steam use and therefore CO2 emissions from regeneration. They also minimize hydrocarbon and solvent losses and pair with high‑efficiency sulfur recovery or wet‑gas sulfuric acid processes that turn waste gases into saleable sulfur or sulfuric acid while recovering heat.Reaching ever higher levels of CO2 concentration for example are part of the oncremental improvements especially as oil and gas companies may be produing from higher CO2 fields to meet demand
tech_0359_advanced-acid-gas-removal.market_dynamics_summary_en: Cutting CO2 emissions from oil ans gas processing, and methane emissions from the energy sector by 75% by 2030 is one of the least cost opportunities to limit global warming in the near term. This is one of the technologies available to reduce oil and gas methane emissions. Regenerable acid gas removal chemicals industry plays a critical role in industrial gas purification and emissions control, enabling removal and repeated regeneration of contaminants such as carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrogen sulfide (H2S), sulfur oxides (SOx), and other acidic gases from fossil fuel streams and industrial emissions.
tech_0359_advanced-acid-gas-removal.description: Advanced acid gas removal clean technologies focus on lower‑energy solvent systems, such as hybrid and high‑capacity amines, that cut steam use and therefore CO2 emissions from regeneration. They also minimize hydrocarbon and solvent losses and pair with high‑efficiency sulfur recovery or wet‑gas sulfuric acid processes that turn waste gases into saleable sulfur or sulfuric acid while recovering heat.Reaching ever higher levels of CO2 concentration for example are part of the oncremental improvements especially as oil and gas companies may be produing from higher CO2 fields to meet demand
tech_0359_advanced-acid-gas-removal.NZErationale: Cutting CO2 emissions from oil ans gas processing, and methane emissions from the energy sector by 75% by 2030 is one of the least cost opportunities to limit global warming in the near term. This is one of the technologies available to reduce oil and gas methane emissions. Regenerable acid gas removal chemicals industry plays a critical role in industrial gas purification and emissions control, enabling removal and repeated regeneration of contaminants such as carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrogen sulfide (H2S), sulfur oxides (SOx), and other acidic gases from fossil fuel streams and industrial emissions.

initiatives.description: New technology implemented in designing AGRU with this amine system is the first in the world, which will process gas with a CO2 content of 65% mole

tech_0360_hydropower.technology_status_summary_zh: 水力發電將落水的能量轉化為電力，是一項成熟且具成本競爭力的技術，目前提供全球16%的發電量，水力發電廠可分為三類：川流式、水庫（儲水）式與抽蓄式電廠。
tech_0360_hydropower.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 仍是重要的清潔電力來源，也為電力系統提供靈活性。
tech_0360_hydropower.technology_status_summary_en: Hydropower converts the energy from falling water into electricity. It is a mature and cost-competitive technology, today providing 16% of global electricity generation. Hydropower plants can be classified in three functional categories: run-of-river, reservoir (or storage), and pumped storage plants.
tech_0360_hydropower.market_dynamics_summary_en: Remains an important clean electricity source, also providing flexibility to the electricity system.
tech_0360_hydropower.description: Hydropower converts the energy from falling water into electricity. It is a mature and cost-competitive technology, today providing 16% of global electricity generation. Hydropower plants can be classified in three functional categories: run-of-river, reservoir (or storage), and pumped storage plants.
tech_0360_hydropower.NZErationale: Remains an important clean electricity source, also providing flexibility to the electricity system.

initiatives.description: Global installed hydropower capacity, including  pumped storage plants, stood at almost 1,450 GW in 2025, with 25 GW added this year. More than 1,000 GW of additional capacity is under development.

tech_0361_ocean-cross-cutting.technology_status_summary_zh: 海洋能技術是一個廣泛的技術家族，涵蓋多種從海洋能量發電的設計，潮汐範圍發電以類似風力發電的方式利用潮汐能量，波浪發電捕捉表面波浪的能量，海洋溫差發電（OTEC）從深海汲取熱能並轉化為電力或商品，鹽度梯度發電則是利用鹹水濃度差異產生化學壓力所生成的能量。
tech_0361_ocean-cross-cutting.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 整體而言，作為再生能源，多項生命週期評估分析已證實海洋能帶來整體碳減排效益，以及與開發部署相關的經濟效益，此外海洋能技術在系統效益方面潛力龐大，尤其是在平衡電力方面。
tech_0361_ocean-cross-cutting.technology_status_summary_en: Ocean technologies are a broad technology family, encompassing a range of designs to generate electricity from energy in the sea. Tidal range power harnesses energy from tides in a similar way to wind power. Wave power captures the energy from surface waves. Ocean thermal energy conversion (OTEC) draws thermal energy from the deep ocean and converts it into electricity or commodities. Salinity gradient power is energy produced from the chemical pressure that results from the difference in salt concentration between fresh water and saltwater. Ocean current technology can harvest energy from sea currents, which, for a given depth, always flow in one direction and are driven by the thermohaline convection system that moves water around the world. For all these technologies exept tidal range ones, only pilot plants have been operated in the past, but scale-ups are planned. For tidal range, two large-scale plants have been operational since the 1960s.
tech_0361_ocean-cross-cutting.market_dynamics_summary_en: Overall, as a renewable energy source, ocean energy brings overall carbon reduction benefits as demonstrated by several lifecycle assessment analyses as well as economic benefits associated with development and deployment.  Furthermore, ocean energy technologies have high potential to create system benefits, specifically in balancing the electricity grid and improving the quality of power supply (i.e. leading to fewer frequency changes and reducing the need of expensive control systems). Unlike the more intermittent renewable sources, the energy of the sea is constant and the tides are entirely predictable.
tech_0361_ocean-cross-cutting.description: Ocean technologies are a broad technology family, encompassing a range of designs to generate electricity from energy in the sea. Tidal range power harnesses energy from tides in a similar way to wind power. Wave power captures the energy from surface waves. Salinity gradient power is energy produced from the chemical pressure that results from the difference in salt concentration between fresh water and saltwater. Ocean current technology can harvest energy from sea currents, which, for a given depth, always flow in one direction and are driven by the thermohaline convection system that moves water around the world. For all these technologies exept tidal range ones, only pilot plants have been operated in the past, but scale-ups are planned. For tidal range, two large-scale plants have been operational since the 1960s.

initiatives.description: The IEA's Ocean Energy Systems (IEA-OES) Technology Collaboration Programme publishes each year a report highlighting global ocean energy progress, policies, and collaborative achievements in RD&D and deployment. This programme, launched in 2001 in response to increased activity around the development of ocean energy technology, facilitates the co-ordinated advancement of research, development and demonstration of this technology. With members from 25 countries it is overseen by an Executive Committee formed of representatives from governmental departments, national energy agencies, research and scientific bodies and academia.

tech_0362_ocean-thermal.technology_status_summary_zh: 海洋溫差發電（OTEC，含海水空調SWAC）利用不同海洋深度間的溫差，全球目前僅運行少數示範電廠，若原理獲得驗證，仍有諸多規模化挑戰，尤其與高營運成本及低效率相關。除發電外，SWAC在歐洲商業建築與資料中心冷卻方面已具商業競爭力，此技術也可用於供應熱能。
tech_0362_ocean-thermal.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在具備適宜海洋條件的特定地區，OTEC可作為穩定的無碳能源，例如可用於氫氣生產的去碳化。但若無顯著成本下降與營運經驗累積，此技術不太可能對能源結構產生重大影響。
tech_0362_ocean-thermal.technology_status_summary_en: Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion (OTEC), including Sea-Water Air Conditioning (SWAC), exploits temperature differences found at different ocean depths. Only a few pilot plants have been operated worldwide. If the principle is proven, there are numerous scale-up challenges, particularly those related to high operational costs and low efficiency. Beyond power production, SWAC is commercially competitive in commercial and data centre cooling in Europe. The technology can also be harnessed to deliver desalination.
tech_0362_ocean-thermal.market_dynamics_summary_en: In certain regions with suitable ocean characteristics, OTEC could be a stable source of carbon-free energy, and could, for example, decarbonise hydrogen production. But without significant cost reductions and operational experience, it is unlikely that this technology will have a significant impact on energy mixes.
tech_0362_ocean-thermal.description: Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion (OTEC), including Sea-Water Air Conditioning (SWAC), exploits temperature differences found at different ocean depths. Only a few pilot plants have been operated worldwide. If the principle is proven, there are numerous scale-up challenges, particularly those related to high operational costs and low efficiency. Beyond power production, SWAC is commercially competitive in commercial and data centre cooling in Europe. The technology can also be harnessed to deliver desalination.
tech_0362_ocean-thermal.NZErationale: In certain regions with suitable ocean characteristics, OTEC could be a stable source of carbon-free energy, and could, for example, decarbonise hydrogen production. But without significant cost reductions and operational experience, it is unlikely that this technology will have a significant impact on energy mixes.

initiatives.description: The Okinawa Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion Demonstration Facility in Japan with a capacity of 100 kW started operation in 2013.
MOL’s OTEC project on Kume Island, selected by Japan’s Ministry of the Environment, will expand the 100 kW plant to 1 MW by 2026—targeting the world’s first demonstration.
A UK based start-up is developping a OTEC pilot power plant, with potential applications in some african countries

Experimental plants have also been operated in Korea, China, India and the USA/Hawaii. These projects provide a slighlty better understanding of the economics of OTEC (see IEA OES TCP analysis, updated in 2024).

tech_0363_wave-energy-converters-wave-en.technology_status_summary_zh: 波浪能轉換器（WEC）利用波浪運動所含的能量，WEC的部署位置具靈活性；可部署於岸邊或近岸，或距岸100公尺以上，波浪技術的發展階段仍早於潮流技術，新型裝置原型正在真實海況中測試，全球正測試多種創新波浪裝置設計概念，波浪能相較其他技術距離商業化更遠。
tech_0363_wave-energy-converters-wave-en.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 整體而言，WEC技術尚未達到超越實驗性示範電廠的規模製造與安裝，需要降低成本才能實現大規模部署。它可能是可預測的無碳能源，但其獲利能力將高度取決於該地區的海洋特性，可能大幅影響其表現。
tech_0363_wave-energy-converters-wave-en.technology_status_summary_en: Wave Energy Converters (WECs) harness the energy contained in the movement of the waves. WEC placement is flexible; WECs can be deployed on or near the shoreline, or at a distance of over 100 metres from the shore. Wave technology remains at an earlier stage of development than tidal stream technology, with novel device prototypes undergoing testing in real sea conditions. A range of innovative wave device design concepts are in testing globally; wave energy is comparatively further from technological convergence. Successful design convergence may not resemble that of tidal technology; instead, a wider variety of different designs may be successful, given the broad spectrum of feasible ways to harness energy from the waves. Wave prototypes are currently found in four main forms. The point absorber is a floating structure that absorbs energy through the movement of the waves at the water’s surface. The attenuator sits across the wave front, capturing energy by selectively constraining the movement caused by the passing wave. The hinged flap is mounted on the seabed in shallower water, and harnesses energy through an oscillating flap. Finally, the Oscillating Water Column (OWC) is a partially-submerged, hollow structure open to the sea water below the surface, trapping air above the water. The rising and falling waves compress and decompress this air, which is channelled through an air turbine. WEC technology developers are seeking to improve the power rating of their devices through design optimisation. This will allow for proving of the technology at higher TRLs, and proceeding to commercialisation.
tech_0363_wave-energy-converters-wave-en.market_dynamics_summary_en: In general, WEC technologies have not been manufactured and installed on a scale beyond the experimental pilot plant, and cost reductions will be necessary to see significant deployment. It could be a predictable source of carbon-free energy, but its profitability will be highly dependent on the ocean characteristics of the region, which could drastically reduce the areas with potential for deployment.

initiatives.description: In 2025, there are several MW-scale operational or planned WEC power plants, mainly in Europe, Australia and North America. The IEA-OES TCP tracks these different projects with an interactive GIS tool. In IEA-OES member countries, there is 3MW of installed operational capacity, and 3MW under construction or awarded.
For example, in September 2025, the first onshore wave energy project in the U.S. launched in Los Angeles, as the city inaugurated a 1.5 MW grid connected pilot facility.

tech_0364_salinity-gradient-blue-energy.technology_name_zh: 鹽度梯度發電（藍色能源）
tech_0364_salinity-gradient-blue-energy.technology_status_summary_zh: 鹽度梯度發電利用海水與淡水間的滲透壓，雖然部署潛力可觀，但鹽度梯度技術仍需進一步發展才能實現，收集鹽度梯度能量（即所謂「藍色能源」）最廣泛採用的方法是壓力延遲滲透（PRO）與逆電滲析（RED），PRO利用淡水與海水透過半透膜混合產生的滲透壓驅動渦輪機。
tech_0364_salinity-gradient-blue-energy.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 可作為與海水淡化製程結合的可預測碳能源，然而過高的營運成本似乎仍阻礙大規模示範專案的完成。
tech_0364_salinity-gradient-blue-energy.technology_status_summary_en: Salinity gradient exploits the osmotic pressure between seawater and fresh water. While there is significant potential for deployment, salinity gradient technology requires further development before this will be possible. The most extensive approaches for harvesting salinity gradient energy (so-called "blue energy") are pressure-retarded osmosis (PRO)and reverse electrodialysis (RED). PRO uses osmotic pressure from mixing freshwater and seawater through a semi-permeable membrane to drive a turbine and produce electricity, wearhas RED harnesses converts the chemical potential difference between saltwater and freshwater directly into electrical power using alternating ion-exchange membranes.
tech_0364_salinity-gradient-blue-energy.market_dynamics_summary_en: Can be a predictable source of carbon energy that can be combined with seawater desalination processes. However, excessively high operating costs still seem to be preventing the completion of a large-scale demonstration.
tech_0364_salinity-gradient-blue-energy.description: Salinity gradient exploits the osmotic pressure between seawater and fresh water. While there is significant potential for deployment, salinity gradient technology requires further development before this will be possible. The most extensive approaches for harvesting salinity gradient energy (so-called "blue energy") are pressure-retarded osmosis (PRO)and reverse electrodialysis (RED). PRO uses osmotic pressure from mixing freshwater and seawater through a semi-permeable membrane to drive a turbine and produce electricity, wearhas RED harnesses converts the chemical potential difference between saltwater and freshwater directly into electrical power using alternating ion-exchange membranes.
tech_0364_salinity-gradient-blue-energy.NZErationale: Can be a predictable source of carbon energy that can be combined with seawater desalination processes. However, excessively high operating costs still seem to be preventing the completion of a large-scale demonstration.

initiatives.description: REDstack, a company from the Netherlands, is testing a Reverse Electro Dialysis (RED) technology at a 50 kW pilot facility in Afsluitdijk. Their automated manufacturing line in Heerenveen is expected to be completed by the end of 2025.
2D materials as advanced ion-exchange membranes could boost salinity gradient energy (blue energy) output—from W/m² to kW/m² scales.
In 2026, Fukuoka’s Mamizupia plant will commercialy desalinate seawater for drinking water and generate 880,000 kWh of electricity using osmotic power from wastewater streams.

SaltPower, a Danish start-up, has been operating a 100kW pilot plant (Pressure Retarded Osmosis, PRO) for several years and has just ceased commercial operations in 2026 due to operating costs that are currently too high.

Several other startups are contributing to the development of more cost effective solutions. For example,  French startup Sweetch Energy is set to scale up production of its innovative membrane technology

tech_0365_tidal-range.technology_status_summary_zh: 潮差發電採用傳統水力發電原理，利用漲潮與退潮間的海平面高度差發電，潮差發電開發主要集中於英國、荷蘭、法國與韓國。
tech_0365_tidal-range.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 兩座大型電廠已運行數十年，其他電廠也可能興建，但廣泛部署似乎受限於地理條件及/或資本成本。
tech_0365_tidal-range.technology_status_summary_en: Tidal range adopts conventional hydropower principles to harvest energy from the difference in sea level between high and low tides. Tidal range development is focused in the UK, the Netherlands, France and Korea.
tech_0365_tidal-range.market_dynamics_summary_en: Two large-scale plants have been operational for decades, and others could be built, but widespread deployment seems limited by geographical constraints and/or capital costs.
tech_0365_tidal-range.description: Tidal range adopts conventional hydropower principles to harvest energy from the difference in sea level between high and low tides. Tidal range development is focused in the UK, the Netherlands, France and Korea.
tech_0365_tidal-range.NZErationale: Two large-scale plants have been operational for decades, and others could be built, but widespread deployment seems limited by geographical constraints and/or capital costs.

initiatives.description: The La Rance tidal range plant in France generates with a capacity of 240 MW around 600GWh annually, fed into the electricity grid.
The Sihawa tidal power plant in Korea started operation in 2011 and is with a capacity of 254 MW the largest tidal range power plant in the world.
The Jiangxia Pilot Tidal Power Plant in China has a capacity of 4 MW and started operation in 1980.
The Annapolis Tidal Power Plant in Canada operated between in 1984 and 2019, with an installed capacity of 20 MW.

tech_0366_tidal-stream-ocean-current-ene.technology_status_summary_zh: 潮流渦輪機利用洋流的流動發電，原理與風力渦輪機利用風流相同，潮流渦輪機可直接安裝於海床，或以浮動方式繫泊於海床，隨著歐洲企業主導在真實海況下測試全尺寸裝置，此技術正邁向商業化，潮流渦輪機的設計正趨於收斂，收斂後的設計通常包含二至三葉片的水平軸。
tech_0366_tidal-stream-ocean-current-ene.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 裝置及其輔助技術預計在約十年（估計）的進一步研發與實際海況經驗後達到商業化，現有潮流技術的額定功率從0.1兆瓦的小型裝置到兆瓦級的大型裝置不等，潮流技術近年來的進展相當可觀。
tech_0366_tidal-stream-ocean-current-ene.technology_status_summary_en: Tidal stream turbines harness the flow of ocean currents in the same way that wind turbines harness the flow of wind. Tidal stream turbines can be mounted directly on the seabed, or floating and moored to the seabed. Technologies are approaching commercialisation, with the testing of full-scale devices in real-sea conditions, led by European companies. The design of tidal stream turbines is approaching design convergence. Converging designs generally comprise two- to three-bladed horizontal-axis turbines. Alternative designs include: vertical axis turbines, which work under the same principles as horizontal axis turbines, except the rotor turns on a vertical axis; oscillating hydrofoils, that have a hydrofoil attached to an oscillating arm, which is lifted by the tidal stream to generate power; enclosed tips, or Venturi Effect devices increase the velocity of the tidal stream by funnelling it through a duct; tidal kites, which are tethered to the seabed with a turbine attached below its ‘wing’, and ‘flies’ in a figure-of-eight path to exaggerate the speed of the waterflow through the turbine; and Archimedes screws, a helical corkscrew device which draws power from the tidal stream as the water flows up the spiral, turning the turbine.
tech_0366_tidal-stream-ocean-current-ene.market_dynamics_summary_en: Devices and their auxiliary technology are expected to reach commercialisation following around ten years (estimated) of further research, development and real-sea experience. The rated power of existing tidal technology ranges between smaller-scale devices of 0.1 MW, and larger scale at a MW scale. The progress of tidal stream in recent years is demonstrated by the operating hours accumulated, capacity deployed and electricity generated, with companies operating at all scales active in Europe and globally. In IEA-OES member countries, the installed capacity reach almost 12MW, and an impressive aditional 170MW capacity is under construction or awarded. It is a predictable and carbon-free source of energy.
tech_0366_tidal-stream-ocean-current-ene.description: Tidal stream turbines harness the flow of ocean currents in the same way that wind turbines harness the flow of wind. Tidal stream turbines can be mounted directly on the seabed, or floating and moored to the seabed. The design of tidal stream turbines is approaching design convergence. Converging designs generally comprise two- to three-bladed horizontal-axis turbines. Alternative designs include: vertical axis turbines, which work under the same principles as horizontal axis turbines, except the rotor turns on a vertical axis; oscillating hydrofoils, that have a hydrofoil attached to an oscillating arm, which is lifted by the tidal stream to generate power; enclosed tips, or Venturi Effect devices increase the velocity of the tidal stream by funnelling it through a duct; tidal kites, which are tethered to the seabed with a turbine attached below its ‘wing’, and ‘flies’ in a figure-of-eight path to exaggerate the speed of the waterflow through the turbine; and Archimedes screws, a helical corkscrew device which draws power from the tidal stream as the water flows up the spiral, turning the turbine.

The rated power of existing tidal technology ranges between smaller-scale devices of 0.1 MW, and larger scale at a MW scale. The progress of tidal stream in recent years is demonstrated by the operating hours accumulated, capacity deployed and electricity generated, with companies operating at all scales active in Europe and globally. In IEA-OES member countries, the installed capacity reach almost 12MW, and an impressive aditional 170MW capacity is under construction or awarded. It is a predictable and carbon-free source of energy.

initiatives.description: Scottish Enterprise’s latest report reveals that a thriving domestic tidal stream energy sector could boost Scotland’s economy by more than £4.5 billion by 2050.
The British Hydropower Association recognises the beneficial potential that tidal energy could have on the UK's energy mix, as the country holds 10% of the world's tidal resources.
To date, in the UK, contracts for difference have been awarded for over 120 MW of tidal stream projects.
In 2024 and 2025, several dozen MW have been added to the capacity under construction or awarded, mainly in Europe. Different projects use different device type.
Several studies analyse the possible pathways for tidal stream energy deployment
MeyGen is an the biggest operational tidal stream array, producing 6MWe, with planed scale-up (60MW awarded, but site resource allows for a capacity out to 400MW)

tech_0367_airborne-wind-energy-system.technology_status_summary_zh: 空中風力能源系統（AWES）透過自主風箏或無人機（以一條或多條繫繩連接地面）將風能轉化為電力，AWES設計多樣，可大致分為透過繫繩對地面發電機產生扭矩的升力型裝置，或搭載空中發電機與導電繫繩的阻力型裝置，AWES相較傳統風機提供多項潛在優勢，所需材料較少。
tech_0367_airborne-wind-energy-system.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 離岸平台的空中風力能源系統（AWES）有望成為離岸風機的替代方案，但目前經驗有限，運行可靠性尚待驗證。
tech_0367_airborne-wind-energy-system.technology_status_summary_en: Airborne wind energy systems (AWES) convert wind energy into electricity through autonomous kites or unmanned aircraft, linked to the ground by one or more tethers. There is a diversity of designs of AWES but these can be broadly categorised as either lift type devices creating torque on a ground generator via their tether, or drag type devices with airborne generators and conductive tethers. AWES offer several potential advantages over conventional wind turbines. They require less material than tower-based turbines, have the potential to be manufactured at lower cost, can be deployed faster and can harness stronger and steadier winds by flying at higher altitudes. AWES technologies are at varying stages of maturity for a variety of applications ranging from small scale off-grid power provision in remote locations to large scale offshore power production.
tech_0367_airborne-wind-energy-system.market_dynamics_summary_en: Airborne Wind Energy Systems (AWES) at offshore platforms could become an alternative to offshore wind turbines, but so far there is limited experience and lack of operational reliability.
tech_0367_airborne-wind-energy-system.description: Airborne wind energy systems (AWES) convert wind energy into electricity through autonomous kites or unmanned aircraft, linked to the ground by one or more tethers. There is a diversity of designs of AWES but these can be broadly categorised as either lift type devices creating torque on a ground generator via their tether, or drag type devices with airborne generators and conductive tethers. AWES offer several potential advantages over conventional wind turbines. They require less material than tower-based turbines, have the potential to be manufactured at lower cost, can be deployed faster and can harness stronger and steadier winds by flying at higher altitudes. AWES technologies are at varying stages of maturity for a variety of applications ranging from small scale off-grid power provision in remote locations to large scale offshore power production.
tech_0367_airborne-wind-energy-system.NZErationale: Airborne Wind Energy Systems (AWES) at offshore platforms could become an alternative to offshore wind turbines, but so far there is limited experience and lack of operational reliability.

initiatives.description: SAWES started to test a Buoyant Air Turbine (BAT) one of the largest airborne wind energy systems at a height of 1 500 meters with a said output of 1 MW using 12 lightweight generators
Commercial pilots are ramping up such as SkySails 450 kW system and the announced 100 kW Falcon project of KitePower

tech_0368_floating-offshore-wind-turbine.technology_status_summary_zh: 相較主流的固定式離岸風機，浮動式離岸風機不在海床上設置基礎，而是基於浮動（駁船式）、半潛式、張力腿或立柱式平台，並以不同的繫泊與錨定系統固定，浮動式離岸風機有望降低對海床的影響、簡化安裝與除役程序，並可在超過50至60公尺的水深取得額外風力資源。
tech_0368_floating-offshore-wind-turbine.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 開發浮動式離岸風機對取得更遠端的離岸資源相當重要。
tech_0368_floating-offshore-wind-turbine.technology_status_summary_en: Compared with mainstream offshore fixed structure mounted turbines, floating offshore wind turbines have no foundation on the sea-floor, but are instead based on either floating (barge), semi-submersible, tension leg or spar platforms, kept in place by different mooring and anchoring systems. Floating offshore turbines offer the potential for lower seabed impact, simplified installation and decommissioning, and access to additional wind resource at water depths exceeding 50 to 60 metres. Floating platforms may also be attractive for mid-depth projects, where saturation of onshore or near-shore potential or the possibility of standardising floating platform designs do not necessarily need heavy-lift vessels to transport platforms or install turbine towers and nacelles. They may therefore ultimately permit greater upscaling of wind turbines than seabed fixed technology.
tech_0368_floating-offshore-wind-turbine.market_dynamics_summary_en: It is important to develop floating offshore to reach more distant offshore resources.
tech_0368_floating-offshore-wind-turbine.description: Compared with mainstream offshore fixed structure mounted turbines, floating offshore wind turbines have no foundation on the sea-floor, but are instead based on either floating (barge), semi-submersible, tension leg or spar platforms, kept in place by different mooring and anchoring systems. Floating offshore turbines offer the potential for lower seabed impact, simplified installation and decommissioning, and access to additional wind resource at water depths exceeding 50 to 60 metres. Floating platforms may also be attractive for mid-depth projects, where saturation of onshore or near-shore potential or the possibility of standardising floating platform designs do not necessarily need heavy-lift vessels to transport platforms or install turbine towers and nacelles. They may therefore ultimately permit greater upscaling of wind turbines than seabed fixed technology.
tech_0368_floating-offshore-wind-turbine.NZErationale: It is important to develop floating offshore to reach more distant offshore resources.

initiatives.description: Turbine manufacturer Mingyang started operating its OceanX a dual-rotor floating turbine, with an generation capacity of 16.6 MW, the following year they annouced the plan to develop an even bigger similar turbine with a capacity of 50 MW
First floating offshore wind park over 50 MW

tech_0369_offshore-renewable-hydrogen-pr.technology_status_summary_zh: 在資源條件良好的地區，離岸風能可成為氫氣生產的來源，與其透過電纜將電力輸送至陸上電解槽，不如在離岸現地生產氫氣，再透過管線將氫氣輸送至海岸，離岸製氫的優勢在於管線相較電纜成本更低、可靠性更佳，除電解槽外，離岸製氫也需要海水淡化。
tech_0369_offshore-renewable-hydrogen-pr.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 有潛力將環境衝擊降至最低，因離岸管線相較電纜可輸送多得多的能量，因此對沿海地區的影響可能小於電纜，現有離岸天然氣管線也可改用於氫氣輸送，開發能承受環境的電解槽與淡化系統。
tech_0369_offshore-renewable-hydrogen-pr.technology_status_summary_en: Offshore wind energy can become a source for hydrogen production in regions with good resource conditions. Instead of transporting the electricity via cable to an onshore electrolyser, hydrogen can be produced offshore, with the hydrogen being transported through a pipeline to the coast. Advantages of offshore hydrogen production are the lower costs and better reliability of pipelines compared to cables. In addition to the electrolyser, offshore hydrogen production also requires a seawater desalination unit. Various approaches are being explored, from retrofitting an electrolyser to an oil and gas platform, over using a man-made island or a new platform close to the wind farm, to integration of the electrolyser and the desalination unit into the wind turbine.
tech_0369_offshore-renewable-hydrogen-pr.market_dynamics_summary_en: Potential to minimise environmental impacts, since offshore pipelines can transport significantly larger amounts of energy than a cable and thus can have fewer impacts on coastal areas than cables. Existing offshore natural gas pipelines could be also repurposed for hydrogen transport. Developing electrolyser and desalination systems that can withstand offshore conditions and integrating them on platforms or wind turbines is one of the main development challenges.
tech_0369_offshore-renewable-hydrogen-pr.description: Offshore wind energy can become a source for hydrogen production in regions with good resource conditions. Instead of transporting the electricity via cable to an onshore electrolyser, hydrogen can be produced offshore, with the hydrogen being transported through a pipeline to the coast. Advantages of offshore hydrogen production are the lower costs and better reliability of pipelines compared to cables. In addition to the electrolyser, offshore hydrogen production also requires a seawater desalination unit. Various approaches are being explored, from retrofitting an electrolyser to an oil and gas platform, over using a man-made island or a new platform close to the wind farm, to integration of the electrolyser and the desalination unit into the wind turbine.
tech_0369_offshore-renewable-hydrogen-pr.NZErationale: Potential to minimise environmental impacts, since offshore pipelines can transport significantly larger amounts of energy than a cable and thus can have fewer impacts on coastal areas than cables. Existing offshore natural gas pipelines could be also repurposed for hydrogen transport. Developing electrolyser and desalination systems that can withstand offshore conditions and integrating them on platforms or wind turbines is one of the main development challenges.

initiatives.description: China Energy is commissioning a semi-submersible offshore platform for producing electrolytic hydrogen, ammonia and methanol, located 5 km off the coast of Yantai City in Shandong.
Ohmium signed a term sheet with SwitcH2 to develop 300 MW PEM electrolysers for a floating production, storage and offloading (FPSO) vessel, expected to be operational by 2029.
Between May and November 2023, Lhyfe's Sealhyfe project successfully tested for the first time a 1 MW electrolyser on an offshore platform off the French coast, despite extreme weather conditions.

In the European Union-backed HOPE project, a consortium of companies led by Lhyfe aims to operate a 10 MW offshore hydrogen production plant off the coust of Belgium by 2026, with public consultation conducted in 2025.

The PosHYdon project in the Dutch North Sea plans to produce hydrogen on an operating gas platform 13 km offshore using a 1 MW electrolyser, following onshore testing that has been under way since 2024.

H2Mare commissioned an offshore platform for hydrogen and synthetic fuel production, installed on a barge in Bremerhaven and intended to operate off Heligoland, integrating DAC, seawater desalination, a high-temperature electrolyser for syngas production and Fischer–Tropsch synthesis.

tech_0370_onshore-wind.technology_status_summary_zh: 風力渦輪機利用風的動能發電，轉子將風能轉化為旋轉能，再由發電機轉化為電力，陸域風機幾乎設置於各種地點與地區——沿海、平坦與複雜地形、炎熱與寒冷氣候、森林與沙漠——是一項成熟的創新技術，其尺寸、性能與輔助服務能力仍持續提升。
tech_0370_onshore-wind.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 陸域風力仍是重要的再生能源資源。
tech_0370_onshore-wind.technology_status_summary_en: Wind turbines harness the kinetic energy of wind to produce electricity. The rotor converts the wind energy into rotational energy, which is then used in a generator to produce electricity. Onshore wind turbines are located on land in almost all kinds of locations and regions – at the coast, in flat and complex terrain, in hot and cold climates, forests and deserts – and are an established innovative technology, still growing in size, performance and ancillary services capabilities.
tech_0370_onshore-wind.market_dynamics_summary_en: Onshore wind remains an important renewable resource.
tech_0370_onshore-wind.description: Wind turbines harness the kinetic energy of wind to produce electricity. The rotor converts the wind energy into rotational energy, which is then used in a generator to produce electricity. Onshore wind turbines are located on land in almost all kinds of locations and regions – at the coast, in flat and complex terrain, in hot and cold climates, forests and deserts – and are an established innovative technology, still growing in size, performance and ancillary services capabilities.
tech_0370_onshore-wind.NZErationale: Onshore wind remains an important renewable resource.

initiatives.description: Global installed onshore wind capacity increased by 84 GW in 2022 to a total of 858 GW. The trend towards larger wind turbines, e.g. higher hub heights and larger rotor diameters, continues to improve the capacity factors and reduce generation costs, allowing the installation of turbines in low-wind speed areas.

In Germany the highest onshore wind turbine will be installed with a tower height of 300 metres. At this height the wind speeds are significantly faster resulting in a capacity factor of 55% and twice the electricity yield compared to turbines at conventional heights.

tech_0371_seabed-fixed-offshore-wind-tur.technology_status_summary_zh: 海床固定式離岸風機占目前已裝設離岸風電裝置容量的絕大多數，其能量捕捉與發電技術本質上與陸域相似，離岸風機經過海事化處理並配置為在離岸環境中最佳運作，基礎型式多樣，包括單樁、多樁、重力式基礎與吸力沉箱，這些可能搭配特定支撐結構。
tech_0371_seabed-fixed-offshore-wind-tur.technology_status_summary_en: Seabed fixed offshore wind turbines represent the overwhelming majority of the currently installed offshore wind generating capacity. The energy capture and power generation technology is fundamentally similar to that onshore. Offshore turbines are marinised and configured for optimal operation in the offshore environment. There is a diversity of foundation types including monopiles, multi-piles, gravity foundations and suction caissons. These may be associated with particular support structures including tubular towers, jackets, tripods, lattice towers and hybrids.
tech_0371_seabed-fixed-offshore-wind-tur.description: Seabed fixed offshore wind turbines represent the overwhelming majority of the currently installed offshore wind generating capacity. The energy capture and power generation technology is fundamentally similar to that onshore. Offshore turbines are marinised and configured for optimal operation in the offshore environment. There is a diversity of foundation types including monopiles, multi-piles, gravity foundations and suction caissons. These may be associated with particular support structures including tubular towers, jackets, tripods, lattice towers and hybrids.

initiatives.description: Global installed offshore wind capacity increased by 13 GW in 2022 to a total of 68 GW.

North Sea: nine countries (Belgium, Denmark, France, Germany, Ireland, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Norway, United Kingdom) set the combined target for offshore wind in the North Sea of 120 GW by 2030 and 300 GW by 2050.

tech_0372_alcohol-to-jet-biokerosene.technology_name_zh: 醇類製噴射燃油（生質航煤）
tech_0372_alcohol-to-jet-biokerosene.technology_status_summary_zh: 此製程整合多項各自成熟的步驟，將醇類（甲醇、乙醇、丁醇）轉化為即插即用型再生柴油或噴射燃油，原料醇經脫水去除水分、寡聚合以將短鏈碳氫化合物轉為長鏈、加氫（加入氫氣）以將碳氫化合物轉化為所需燃料，最後經蒸餾分離出柴油、噴射燃油與其他產物。醇類製噴射燃油（ATJ）已具一定成熟度。
tech_0372_alcohol-to-jet-biokerosene.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在淨零世界中，永續航空燃料（SAF）的需求龐大，而SAF的其他替代方案成本高昂或技術上不可行（如長程航班電氣化）。
tech_0372_alcohol-to-jet-biokerosene.technology_status_summary_en: This process integrates several individually well-known steps to convert an alcohol (methanol, ethanol, butanol) into a drop-in renewable diesel or jet fuel. The feedstock alcohol undergoes dehydration to remove water, oligomerisation to create longer chain hydrocarbons out of shorter chains, hydrogenation through the addition to hydrogen to convert the hydrocarbons into desired fuels, and finally distillation to separate the products into diesel, jet fuel and other streams. Alcohol-to-jet (ATJ) is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0372_alcohol-to-jet-biokerosene.market_dynamics_summary_en: The demand for sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) in a net-zero world is large, and other alternatives to SAFs are expensive or technically infeasible (e.g. electrifying long-haul flights)
tech_0372_alcohol-to-jet-biokerosene.description: This process integrates several individually well-known steps to convert an alcohol (methanol, ethanol, butanol) into a drop-in renewable diesel or jet fuel. The feedstock alcohol undergoes dehydration to remove water, oligomerisation to create longer chain hydrocarbons out of shorter chains, hydrogenation through the addition to hydrogen to convert the hydrocarbons into desired fuels, and finally distillation to separate the products into diesel, jet fuel and other streams. Alcohol-to-jet (ATJ) is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0372_alcohol-to-jet-biokerosene.NZErationale: The demand for sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) in a net-zero world is large, and other alternatives to SAFs are expensive or technically infeasible (e.g. electrifying long-haul flights)

initiatives.description: The ReFuelEU Aviation initiative in the EU proposes sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) blending obligations for EU fuel suppliers, starting with 2% SAF in 2025 and rising to 70% by 2050.

In 2022, the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) adopted a long-term aspirational goal (LTAG) to achieve net zero aviation sector emissions by 2050. It has previously set a goal for carbon-neutral growth from 2020 onwards, and has adopted the Carbon Offsetting and reduction for International Aviation (CORSIA) framework, which includes SAF as an option for carbon emissions reduction.

LanzaTech, a United States-based biofuels company that has achieved commercial operation of syngas fermentation to ethanol, is planning two demonstration facilities for an ATJ pathway developed by the US Department of Energy's Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL). The first demo plant will be in Georgia, United States, and a second demo plant is planned for Japan, in collaboration with Japan's All Nippon Airways (ANA) airline and the New Energy and Industrial Technology Development Organization (NEDO). In Sept 2021, LanzaTech announced a collaboration with Argonne National Laboratory to demonstrate ethanol from biogenic CO2 captured from an ethanol plant and electrolytic hydrogen from solar PV. The demo will produce about 130 litres per day of ethanol that will then be converted to biokerosene via LanzaJet's (a subsidiary of LanzaTech) alcohol-to-jet (ATJ) process. In the same month, LanzaTech announced an additional partnership with the sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) aggregator and supplier SkyNRG Americas to produce biokerosene from landfill gas (a mixture of methane [CH4], CO2 and nitrogen [N2]) using renewably-powered electrolytic hydrogen. The project is dubbed LOTUS, and has secured funding from the US DOE as part of its Sustainable Aviation Fuel Grand Challenge.

In November 2025, US company LanzaJet started production at its "Freedom Pines Fuels" plant (28 ktpa of SAF + 3ktpa renewable diesel, worldwide first ethanol-to-SAF) in Soperton (Georgia), United States.

In January 2024, Indian company Praj Industries launched a sustainable aviation fuel demonstration facility in Pune to develop alcohol-to-jet (AtJ) technology for the first time in India. This provides a pathway to use feedstocks that are more readily available in India such as sugar cane and agricultural residues, to help avoid oil imports and reduce emissions.

LanzaJet's "Speedbird" project (90 ktpa) announces siting at Teesside (UK) and receives $13 million of UK support to proceed into FEED, which was awarded to Fluor. Technology includes Technip Energies' Hummingbird® ethanol-to-ethylene process.

Summit Agricultural Group's "Summit Next Gen" project (~790 ktpa SAF in Houston Ship Channel), announced in 2023, was qualified under the Texas Jobs, Energy, Technology, and Innovation (JETI) program in 2025.
Mitsui & Co. and Taiyo Oil launch a joint study to produce SAF/RD at the Okinawa (Nansei Sekiyu) site, targeting up to 180ktpa SAF and renewable diesel in 2028.
Taiyo Oil "Okinawa Operations" receives a $12 million subsidy from METI, selects Honeywell UOP ethanol-to-SAF technology and targets production of 200 kL per year (~165 ktpa) in 2029.
Mitsui & Co. and Cosmo Oil launch a joint study to produce 220 million L per year of SAF and 20 million L of renewable diesel from 2027, using LanzaJet ATJ technology.
Cosmo Oil ATJ project is granted $21 million subsidy from METI; the project targets 150 ML/yr SAF and 17ML/yr renewable diesel at the Sakaide Distribution Terminal (Kagawa), starting in 2029.
KazMunayGas (KMG) and LanzaJet complete a joint feasibility study, and move to Front-End Engineering and Design (FEED) to produce 70 ktpa of ATJ SAF in Kazakhstan by 2030.
LanzaJet and BioD launch a feasibility study to develop an ATJ SAF plant in Colombia, targeting operation in 2029.

In 2025, India’s TruAlt Bioenergy has signed a memorandum of understanding with the Andhra Pradesh Economic Development Board to build a $270 million ethanol-to-SAF plant aiming at producing 80 ktpa SAF by 2027.

tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.technology_name: Transesterification of oil/fats to fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) (biodiesel)
tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.technology_name_zh: 油脂酯轉化製脂肪酸甲酯（FAME）（生質柴油）
tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.technology_status_summary_zh: 脂肪酸甲酯（FAME）生質柴油是透過植物油（大豆、棕櫚、油菜籽）或廢油（動物脂肪、廢食用油）與甲醇在觸媒存在下反應製成，油中三酸甘油酯的酯轉化反應產生生質柴油與甘油，生質柴油與甘油經一系列純化與分離步驟，以清潔最終產品並回收觸媒與剩餘甲醇，甘油可售出。
tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 相較脂肪酸甲酯（FAME）生質柴油的摻混上限，其他即插即用型生質燃料能更有效利用現有基礎設施，此外油籽作物原料的永續性限制及廢油原料的固有限制，也限制了整體成長潛力。
tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.technology_status_summary_en: Fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) biodiesel is produced by reacting either vegetable oil (soybean, palm, rapeseed) or waste oils (animal fats, used cooking oils) with methanol in the presence of a catalyst. The transesterification reaction of the triglycerides found within the oils produces biodiesel and glycerine. The biodiesel and glycerine undergo a series of purification and separation steps to clean the final products and to recover the catalyst and any remaining methanol. Glycerine can be sold to the pharmaceutical industry. The biodiesel can be blended up to 5-7% with fossil diesel for use in road transport, or can be blended up to 100% for use in marine diesel engines.
tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.market_dynamics_summary_en: Compared to fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) biodiesel's blending limit, there are other drop-in biofuels that can use existing infrastructure more efficiently. Additionally, there are sustainability limits to oilseed crop feedstock and inherent limitations to waste oil feedstock that limit the overall potential for growth.
tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.description: Fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) biodiesel is produced by reacting either vegetable oil (soybean, palm, rapeseed) or waste oils (animal fats, used cooking oils) with methanol in the presence of a catalyst. The transesterification reaction of the triglycerides found within the oils produces biodiesel and glycerine. The biodiesel and glycerine undergo a series of purification and separation steps to clean the final products and to recover the catalyst and any remaining methanol. Glycerine can be sold to the pharmaceutical industry. The biodiesel can be blended up to 5-7% with fossil diesel for use in road transport, or can be blended up to 100% for use in marine diesel engines.
tech_0373_transesterification-of-oil-fat.NZErationale: Compared to fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) biodiesel's blending limit, there are other drop-in biofuels that can use existing infrastructure more efficiently. Additionally, there are sustainability limits to oilseed crop feedstock and inherent limitations to waste oil feedstock that limit the overall potential for growth.

initiatives.description: FAME biodiesel is produced at a commercial scale across the world. In 2019, a total of 38.5 million tonnes of FAME were produced. Four countries dominate in FAME production, contributing to 55% of global production. This includes Indonesia (18.2%), United States (15.6%), Brazil (12.5%) and Germany (8.8%). As a region, Europe is the largest producer, accounting for 33.3% of global FAME production.

tech_0374_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_name: Gasification and fischer-tropsch integrated with CO2 capture (biodiesel, biokerosene)
tech_0374_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_name_zh: 氣化與費托合成整合二氧化碳捕捉（生質柴油、生質航煤）
tech_0374_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質基費托合成路徑（bio-FT）通常稱為生質合成油（BTL）路線，儘管此統稱也可涵蓋任何以生質原料生產液態燃料的路線，在bio-FT路線中，生質原料先氣化為合成氣，合成氣再透過費托製程轉化為碳氫化合物液體。木質纖維素含量高的生質原料（如木材、稻草、林業與農業殘餘物、都市固體廢棄物）經加熱氣化。
tech_0374_gasification-and-fischer-trops.market_dynamics_summary_zh: bio-FT路徑使用的生質原料，不像其他生質燃料那樣面臨相同的土地利用變化與糧食競爭疑慮，此外bio-FT製程產出即插即用型燃料，可完全取代運輸部門的化石燃料對應品，讓現有基礎設施得以重複利用。
tech_0374_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_status_summary_en: The biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) is typically referred to as a biomass-to-liquid (BTL) route, though this umbrella term can apply to any route which produces liquid fuel from biomass. In the bio-FT route, biomass is first gasified into syngas and the syngas is then converted into hydrocarbon liquids via the Fischer-Tropsch process. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is gasified via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and other hydrocarbons. This "syngas" is then sent to a water-gas shift (WGS) reactor to increase the H2/CO ratio required for Fischer-Tropsch (FT) synthesis. In the CCUS variant,  the CO2 is separated from the syngas prior to FT synthesis, resulting in a pure stream of CO2 that can be captured and compressed for utilisation or storage rather than vented. If stored, negative emissions are created. The resulting liquids from the FT reactor are further cleaned and separated into their hydrocarbon products (diesel, jet, naphtha, wax, etc). Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification. The biomass used to produce bio-FT are not food crops, avoiding direct competition with food and unwanted land-use change. Fuels resulting from bio-FT are "drop-in" and can therefore use existing fossil fuel infrastructure and technology without blending limits. Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification. Bio-FT kerosene (biojet) is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0374_gasification-and-fischer-trops.market_dynamics_summary_en: The biomass feedstock used in the biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bioFT) does not face the same land-use change and food competition concerns that plague other biofuels. Additionally, the bio-FT process produces a drop-in fuel that can completely replace fossil counterparts in the transport sector, allowing reuse of existing infrastructure. Adding carbon capture and storage (CCS) is relatively easy for bio-FT and provides negative emissions that can offset hard-to-abate emissions elsewhere in the energy system.
tech_0374_gasification-and-fischer-trops.description: The biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) is typically referred to as a biomass-to-liquid (BTL) route, though this umbrella term can apply to any route which produces liquid fuel from biomass. In the bio-FT route, biomass is first gasified into syngas and the syngas is then converted into hydrocarbon liquids via the Fischer-Tropsch process. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is gasified via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and other hydrocarbons. This "syngas" is then sent to a water-gas shift (WGS) reactor to increase the H2/CO ratio required for Fischer-Tropsch (FT) synthesis. In the CCUS variant,  the CO2 is separated from the syngas prior to FT synthesis, resulting in a pure stream of CO2 that can be captured and compressed for utilisation or storage rather than vented. If stored, negative emissions are created. The resulting liquids from the FT reactor are further cleaned and separated into their hydrocarbon products (diesel, jet, naphtha, wax, etc).
