Additionally, the bio-FT process produces a drop-in fuel that can completely replace fossil counterparts in the transport sector, allowing reuse of existing infrastructure. Adding carbon capture and storage (CCS) is relatively easy for bio-FT and provides negative emissions that can offset hard-to-abate emissions elsewhere in the energy system.

initiatives.description: Altalto Immingham, developed by Velocys with British Airways, is a residual-waste-to-FT-SAF project in the Humber region, targeting production of 30 million litres/y (23 ktpa) of SAF by 2030 from residual waste with mentions of carbon capture plans. Announced in 2019, the project  received UK support under the F4C, the Green Fuels, Green Skies scheme (£2.4 million in 2021) and the Advanced Fuels Fund (£30 million over 2021-2025). Technologies involve NextChem’s NX Circular gasification and Velocys’ MicroFTL.

In 2022, USA BioEnergy announced its Bon Wier (Texas, US) "Texas Renewable Fuels" project, a woody-biomass (wood fiber) gasification-to-FT facility targeting ~65 million gallons per year of fuels (including SAF) by 2030 and at least 50 million tons of captured and sequestrated CO2 over the useful life of the plant. It will rely on bp/Johnson Matthey's FT CANS and Honeywell's UOP's FT Unicracking for upgrading (2025). It is backed by 20-year feedstock (2024: 2.2 million tons of wood fiber from forest thinnings) and offtake (2023: Southwest Airlines Co.) agreements. In 2025, USA BioEnergy announced partnership with Applied Atomics to supply the plant with small nuclear power (initially 100 MW, up to potentially 1 GW).

Strategic Biofuels' Louisiana Green Fuels project was first announced in April 2021 as a carbon-negative biorefinery producing ~33 million gallons/yr (later SAF) with integrated CCS at the Port of Columbia. It was reframed in 2025 so that Phase 1 is now a 100 MW BECCS power plant sequestering >1 MtCO2/yr, with the gasification-to-FT fuels plant deferred to a potential Phase 2.

Velocys' Bayou Fuels project in Natchez (Mississippi, US) was conceived as a biomass-gasification-to-Fischer-Tropsch plant producing on the order of 25-30 million gallons/yr of negative-carbon-intensity diesel, naphtha and SAF from forestry residues with associated CO2 capture (0.3-0.5 million tonnes of CO2 per year for transport and geological storage by Oxy Low Carbon Ventures), but after completing early engineering and securing permits in the late 2010s the company has cancelled the Natchez site in 2025.

Alfanar’s Lighthouse Green Fuels project at the Teesside industrial cluster (UK) is currently conducting FEED studies, supported with close to £30 million of public funding from the UK "Green Fuels, Green Skies" programme and the "Advanced Fuels Fund" over 2021-2025. It consists of a large MSW/biogenic-waste gasification-to-Fischer–Tropsch SAF plant coupled with CO2 capture, targeting production of 180 million litres per year of SAF from 2029.

Northpointe Energy Ltd (formerly Fulcrum BioEnergy’s) NorthPoint project at Ellesmere Port (Cheshire, UK) is planned as a commercial-scale municipal solid-waste gasification-to-Fischer–Tropsch SAF plant. It was awarded £19 million of UK Advanced Fuels Fund support in 2022-2025. It plans to deliver about 135 million litres of SAF by 2031, with the oppportunity for capturing 0.5 Mtpa CO2.

tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_name: Gasification and fischer-tropsch without integrated CO2 capture (biodiesel, biokerosene)
tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_name_zh: 氣化與費托合成（不含二氧化碳捕捉）（生質柴油、生質航煤）
tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質基費托合成路徑（bio-FT）通常稱為生質合成油（BTL）路線，儘管此統稱也可涵蓋任何以生質原料生產液態燃料的路線，在bio-FT路線中，生質原料先氣化為合成氣，合成氣再透過費托製程轉化為碳氫化合物液體。木質纖維素含量高的生質原料（如木材、稻草、林業與農業殘餘物、都市固體廢棄物）經加熱氣化。
tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.market_dynamics_summary_zh: bio-FT路徑使用的生質原料，不像其他生質燃料那樣面臨相同的土地利用變化與糧食競爭疑慮，此外bio-FT製程產出即插即用型燃料，可完全取代運輸部門的化石燃料對應品，讓現有基礎設施得以重複利用。
tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_status_summary_en: The biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) is typically referred to as a biomass-to-liquid (BTL) route, though this umbrella term can apply to any route which produces liquid fuel from biomass. In the bio-FT route, biomass is first gasified into syngas and the syngas is then converted into hydrocarbon liquids via the Fischer-Tropsch process. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is gasified via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and other hydrocarbons. This "syngas" is then sent to a water-gas shift (WGS) reactor to increase the H2/CO ratio required for Fischer-Tropsch (FT) synthesis, and CO2 is separated and vented. The syngas is fed into the FT reactor, and the resulting liquid hydrocarbons are cleaned, refined and separated into diesel, jet fuel, naphtha and other products. The biomass used to produce bio-FT are not food crops, avoiding direct competition with food and unwanted land-use change. Fuels resulting from bio-FT are "drop-in" and can therefore use existing fossil fuel infrastructure and technology without blending limits. Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification. Bio-FT kerosene (biojet) is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.market_dynamics_summary_en: The biomass feedstock used in the biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) does not face the same land-use change and food competition concerns that plague other biofuels. Additionally, the bio-FT process produces a drop-in fuel that can completely replace fossil counterparts in the transport sector, allowing reuse of existing infrastructure.
tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.description: The biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) is typically referred to as a biomass-to-liquid (BTL) route, though this umbrella term can apply to any route which produces liquid fuel from biomass. In the bio-FT route, biomass is first gasified into syngas and the syngas is then converted into hydrocarbon liquids via the Fischer-Tropsch process. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is gasified via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and other hydrocarbons. This "syngas" is then sent to a water-gas shift (WGS) reactor to increase the H2/CO ratio required for Fischer-Tropsch (FT) synthesis, and CO2 is separated and vented. The syngas is fed into the FT reactor, and the resulting liquid hydrocarbons are cleaned, refined and separated into diesel, jet fuel, naphtha and other products. The biomass used to produce bio-FT are not food crops, avoiding direct competition with food and unwanted land-use change. Fuels resulting from bio-FT are "drop-in" and can therefore use existing fossil fuel infrastructure and technology without blending limits. Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification. Bio-FT kerosene (biojet) is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0375_gasification-and-fischer-trops.NZErationale: The biomass feedstock used in the biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) does not face the same land-use change and food competition concerns that plague other biofuels. Additionally, the bio-FT process produces a drop-in fuel that can completely replace fossil counterparts in the transport sector, allowing reuse of existing infrastructure.

initiatives.description: In September 2024, Fulcrum BioEnergy went bankrupt and shut its Waste-to-Jet Nevada plant amid technical challenges (nitric acid corrosion, NOx emissions).

In Nevada, United States, Fulcrum Bioenergy used municipal solid waste (MSW) as feedstock to produce a bioderived "syncrude" from bio-FT. The syncrude was sent to a nearby petroleum refinery (Marathon Petroleum) to be upgraded into diesel and other transportation fuels. A first fuel production was achieved in December 2022, but the company filed for bankrupcy protection in September 2024.

In France, the BioTfueL programme (launched in 2010 by Axens, Avril, CEA, IFPEN, thyssenkrupp Uhde, TotalEnergies) built two demonstration units - one in Venette for biomass pretreatment, and one in Dunkirk for gasification, syngas clean-up, Fischer-Tropsch synthesis and upgrading - to produce fuels from lignocellulosic feedstocks; between roughly 2015 and 2021 the programme successfully validated a complete biomass-to-liquids chain and process flexibility across forestry and agricultural residues.

SuMo Engineering’s CLEARSKIES project, based in Wednesbury (UK)emonstration plant using gasification, Fischer-Tropsch synthesis and gas reforming technology to convert refuse derived fuel and biomethane into SAF. In 2025, it received £4.2 million of public support from UK Advanced Fuels Fund.

Willis Sustainable Fuels’ Teesside Carbonshift SAF project is a small commercial-scale plant in north-east England that will use autothermal reforming coupled with Fischer–Tropsch synthesis to convert residue-derived biomethane into SAF. Following its announcement in 2023, it was supported with £2.9 million from UK Advanced Fuels Fund.

tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_name: Gasification and fischer-tropsch with hydrogen enhancement (biodiesel, biokerosene)
tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_name_zh: 氣化與費托合成搭配氫氣增強（生質柴油、生質航煤）
tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質基費托合成路徑（bio-FT）通常稱為生質合成油（BTL）路線，在搭配氫氣增強的bio-FT路線中，生質原料先氣化為合成氣（主要為氫氣、一氧化碳與二氧化碳），與一般bio-FT路線將合成氣送入水煤氣變換（WGS）反應器不同，此路線改為在合成氣中加入低碳氫氣以驅動反應。
tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 雖然不含氫氣的bio-FT對淨零仍是有價值的生質燃料，但加入氫氣可讓生質原料中的生物碳獲得更有效利用（儘管這會妨礙碳捕捉封存CCS提供負排放的可能性），然而氫氣來源必須是低排放的。
tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.technology_status_summary_en: The biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) is typically referred to as a biomass-to-liquid (BTL) route, though this umbrella term can apply to any route which produces liquid fuel from biomass. In the bio-FT route with hydrogen enhancement, biomass is first gasified into syngas (mostly hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide). Instead of sending the syngas to a water-gas shift (WGS) reactor, as is done in the usual bio-FT route, low-carbon hydrogen is added to the syngas to drive a reverse water-gas shift (rWGS) reaction, converting hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) into water and carbon monoxide (CO). Sufficient hydrogen is added to ensure a desired H2/CO ratio for Fischer-Tropsch (FT) synthesis. The liquids from the FT reactor are further cleaned and separated into their drop-in hydrocarbon products (diesel, jet, naphtha, etc). The benefit of adding hydrogen is a more efficient use of the carbon in biomass, as the carbon in CO2 is converted into hydrocarbon fuels rather than being either vented (bio-FT route) or captured and stored (bio-FT w/ CCS route). Rather than providing negative emissions, the additionally converted carbon can displace fossil carbon within the energy system. Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification. Bio-FT kerosene (biojet) is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.market_dynamics_summary_en: While bio-FT without hydrogen is still a valuable biofuel for net zero, adding hydrogen would allow for a more efficient use of biogenic carbon within the biomass (though it would prevent carbon capture and storage (CCS) from providing negative emissions). However, the source of hydrogen must be low-emission.
tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.description: The biomass-based Fischer Tropsch pathway (bio-FT) is typically referred to as a biomass-to-liquid (BTL) route, though this umbrella term can apply to any route which produces liquid fuel from biomass. In the bio-FT route with hydrogen enhancement, biomass is first gasified into syngas (mostly hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide). Instead of sending the syngas to a water-gas shift (WGS) reactor, as is done in the usual bio-FT route, low-carbon hydrogen is added to the syngas to drive a reverse water-gas shift (rWGS) reaction, converting hydrogen (H2) and carbon dioxide (CO2) into water and carbon monoxide (CO). Sufficient hydrogen is added to ensure a desired H2/CO ratio for Fischer-Tropsch (FT) synthesis. The liquids from the FT reactor are further cleaned and separated into their drop-in hydrocarbon products (diesel, jet, naphtha, etc). The benefit of adding hydrogen is a more efficient use of the carbon in biomass, as the carbon in CO2 is converted into hydrocarbon fuels rather than being either vented (bio-FT route) or captured and stored (bio-FT w/ CCS route). Rather than providing negative emissions, the additionally converted carbon can displace fossil carbon within the energy system. Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification. Bio-FT kerosene (biojet) is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0376_gasification-and-fischer-trops.NZErationale: While bio-FT without hydrogen is still a valuable biofuel for net zero, adding hydrogen would allow for a more efficient use of biogenic carbon within the biomass (though it would prevent carbon capture and storage (CCS) from providing negative emissions). However, the source of hydrogen must be low-emission.

initiatives.description: In June 2023, Elyse Energy and partners announced the BioTJet project, which will deploy the BioTfueL route at industrial scale in Lacq (France) to produce about 82 ktpa of e-bio-SAF from lignocellulosic biomass, using low-carbon/green hydrogen to increase yields. The plant, targeting start-up around 2028, will combine thyssenkrupp Uhde's PRENFLO entrained-flow gasification with Axens' GASEL Fischer-Tropsch technology and downstream Axens upgrading. The project benefited from €7.9 million (of wich €3 million repayable) of French public support in 2023. In 2025, it was awarded another public funding under the "Carb Aéro" scheme, totalling €100 million for 4 projects, to conduct FEED studies.

DG Fuels' St. James (Louisiana) project is a hydrogen-enhanced biomass-gasification-to-Fischer-Tropsch SAF plant that will use external green and blue hydrogen (with CO2 capture on the blue-H2 unit). Announced in 2021, last updates target first output around 2030 at ~600 kt/yr, which would make it the largest announced FT-SAF project to date, backed by offtakes with Delta and Air France-KLM. Core technologies include bp/Johnson Matthey's FT CANS and NextChem's NX Circular gasification, with Samsung E&A providing hydrogen and power systems.

In 2024, DG Fuels announced a hydrogen-enhanced G-FT project in Phelps County (Nebraska, US), expected to be operational before 2030 and designed to produce about 450 million litres of SAF per year from corn stover; as in Louisiana, the plant would integrate NextChem's NX Circular gasification technology with a Fischer-Tropsch / upgrading train and rely on external low-carbon hydrogen for yield enhancement.

In 2024, DG Fuels announced plans for a SAF plant in Moorhead (Minnesota, US) to produce 193 million gallons per year of low-carbon aviation fuel (SAF) using agricultural and wood waste as feedstock before 2030.

In 2024, China Energy Engineering Group's started construction of its plant in Shuangyashan (Heilongjiang) targeting 300 ktpa SAF and 200 ktpa green methanol, with a first phase aimed at 100 ktpa of SAF to be operational in 2027.

tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.technology_name: Hydrotreating of oil/fats to hydrogenated vegetable oil (HVO) or hydroprocessed esters and fatty acids (HEFA) (biodiesel, biokerosene)
tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.technology_name_zh: 油脂加氫處理製氫化植物油（HVO）或加氫處理酯與脂肪酸（HEFA）（生質柴油、生質航煤）
tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫化植物油（HVO，又稱加氫處理酯與脂肪酸HEFA）是一種再生柴油，而HEFA是一種即插即用型生質航煤，意即它是一種即插即用型燃料，理論上與化石柴油及煤油的摻混無上限，儘管目前航空應用上限為50%摻混。HVO透過煉油廠常用的成熟加氫處理製程生產，油原料（大豆、棕櫚或油菜籽等植物油，或廢油）。
tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 油籽作物原料存在永續性限制，廢油原料也有固有限制，這些限制了整體成長潛力。
tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.technology_status_summary_en: Hydrogenated vegetable oil (HVO) - also known as hydroprocessed esters and fatty acids (HEFA) -  is a type of renewable diesel while HEFA is a type of drop-in biokerosene, meaning it is a drop-in fuel and theoretically has no upper blend limit with fossil diesel and kerosene, though it is currently capped at 50% blend for use in aviation. HVO is produced via well-known hydrotreatment commonly used at petroleum refineries. An oil feedstock (vegetable oil such a soybean, palm or rapeseed, or waste oils such as animal fats and used cooking oils) is reacted with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst to remove oxygen and break the triglycerides in the oil into three separate hydrocarbon chains. When compared to FAME biodiesel, HVO/HEFA has better storage stability, cold flow properties and a higher cetane number (higher ignitibility). HVO/HEFA kerosene/jet fuel  is an American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) pathway, allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.market_dynamics_summary_en: There are sustainability limits to oilseed crop feedstock and inherent limitations to waste oil feedstock that limit the overall potential for growth.
tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.description: Hydrogenated vegetable oil (HVO) - also known as hydroprocessed esters and fatty acids (HEFA) -  is a type of renewable diesel while HEFA is a type of drop-in biokerosene, meaning it is a drop-in fuel and theoretically has no upper blend limit with fossil diesel and kerosene, though it is currently capped at 50% blend for use in aviation. HVO is produced via well-known hydrotreatment commonly used at petroleum refineries. An oil feedstock (vegetable oil such a soybean, palm or rapeseed, or waste oils such as animal fats and used cooking oils) is reacted with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst to remove oxygen and break the triglycerides in the oil into three separate hydrocarbon chains. When compared to FAME biodiesel, HVO/HEFA has better storage stability, cold flow properties and a higher cetane number (higher ignitibility). HVO/HEFA kerosene/jet fuel  is an American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) pathway, allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0377_hydrotreating-of-oil-fats-to-h.NZErationale: There are sustainability limits to oilseed crop feedstock and inherent limitations to waste oil feedstock that limit the overall potential for growth.

tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_name: Hydrothermal liquefaction and upgrading of biomass (biodiesel, biokerosene)
tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_name_zh: 生質原料水熱液化與升級（生質柴油、生質航煤）
tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_status_summary_zh: 在水熱液化（HTL，又稱觸媒水熱裂解CHJ）中，生質原料在高壓（150至350巴）與高溫（攝氏250-450度）的水中分解為氣體與生質油，通常在鹼性觸媒存在下進行，可使用多種生質原料，且不需乾燥，這是相較熱裂解與其他熱化學轉化製程的優勢，生質油與氣態及水相產物分離後，可精煉為高品質燃料。
tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在成熟度更高的替代生質燃料。
tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_status_summary_en: In hydrothermal liquefaction (HTL) (also called catalytic hydrothermolysis, CHJ) , biomass decomposes into gases and bio-oil using water under high pressure (150 to 350 bar) and high temperature (250 - 450 °C), often in the presence of an alkali catalyst. A variety of biomass can be used and the biomass does not need to be dry, an advantage over pyrolysis and other thermochemical conversion processes. The bio-oil is separated from the gaseous and aqueous products, and can then be refined into high quality fuel such as diesel using typical petroleum refining processes. It can be co-fed with fossil-based oil into refineries. As this version of bio-oil has lower oxygen content than pyrolysis oil, it can be blended into heavy fuel oil for use in the shipping industry. HTL with upgrading is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) pathway, allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.market_dynamics_summary_en: There are alternative biofuels available at higher TRL.
tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.description: In hydrothermal liquefaction (HTL) (also called catalytic hydrothermolysis, CHJ) , biomass decomposes into gases and bio-oil using water under high pressure (150 to 350 bar) and high temperature (250 - 450 °C), often in the presence of an alkali catalyst. A variety of biomass can be used and the biomass does not need to be dry, an advantage over pyrolysis and other thermochemical conversion processes. The bio-oil is separated from the gaseous and aqueous products, and can then be refined into high quality fuel such as diesel using typical petroleum refining processes. It can be co-fed with fossil-based oil into refineries. As this version of bio-oil has lower oxygen content than pyrolysis oil, it can be blended into heavy fuel oil for use in the shipping industry. HTL with upgrading is a American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) pathway, allowed to be blended up to 50%.
tech_0378_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.NZErationale: There are alternative biofuels available at higher TRL.

initiatives.description: Licella's Project Swift, a Cat-HTR™ hydrothermal-liquefaction-to-SAF biorefinery, plans to convert lignocellulosic sugarcane residues to 60 ML/yr of low-carbon fuels (40 ML/yr SAF). In 2025, ARENA granted AU$8 million to the project to complete FEED studies.

In 2025, Licella-owned Arbios Biotech completed construction of its Chuntoh Ghuna facility in Prince George (Canada) and plans for a scale-up. Announced in 2021, the plant was designed to process 25 ktpa (dry) of wood residues into 50 000 barrels of bio-oil annually, which makes it the current largest HTL plant worldwide. It relies on Licella's Cat-HTR™ technology.

Silva Green Fuel (Statkraft) completed construction of a demonstration plant at Tofte in 2022 that can produce 4 kL/day of biofuel made from hydrothermal liquefaction of forestry residues. In 2025, Statkraft initiated steps to close the demo after failing to secure a partner.

In 2025, Steeper Energy acquired Aalborg Univerity's HTL 20 kg/h feed pilot plant, which has been in operation since 2013, and relocated it to Aalborg East. Steeper announced a ten-fold capacity upgrade to accelerate the development of its Hydrofaction technology, so as to produce 50-160 litres bio-oil per day by 2026 and 0.65-1.1 kL/day in 2027.

tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_name: Hydrothermal liquefaction and upgrading of micro-algae (to be merged)
tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_name_zh: 微藻水熱液化與升級（待合併）
tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_status_summary_zh: 微藻可於開放式池塘、封閉式光生物反應器或異營式生物反應器（無需光照）中培養，培養完成後，整個藻類細胞經水熱液化（HTL）處理，製程與陸生生質原料相同，藻類生質油與其餘產物分離後，可送至加氫處理（煉油廠常見）升級為即插即用型生質柴油（再生柴油），其餘固體、水與二氧化碳經處理後回收再利用。
tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在替代生質原料，且藻類生質能原料尚未達到商業化，然而藻類生產有潛力釋放更多永續生質原料，可加速邁向淨零的路徑，及/或在淨零之後提供額外的生質原料。
tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_status_summary_en: Micro-algae can be grown in open ponds, closed photobioreactors, or in heterotrophic bioreactors (no light required). Once grown, the whole algae cell is hydrothermally liquefied (HTL) via the same process used for terrestrial biomass. The algal bio-oil is separated from the remaining products and can be sent to  hydrotreatment (common in petroleum refineries) to be upgraded to a drop-in biodiesel (renewable diesel). The remaining solids, water and carbon dioxide are treated and recycled to the cultivation step, while remaining off-gases provide heat, electricity or hydrogen. As with all algal biofuel systems, the main challenge arises from the high cost of harvesting and cultivation, as well as reducing the energy/water/nutrient/land footprint of the system. However, algae can be grown on non-arable land, avoiding competition with food. Benefits of using the HTL route with micro-algae include HTL's ability to handle wet feedstocks, and its use of all algae components (lipids, carbohydrates, proteins), removing the need to cultivate high lipid content and extraction.
tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative biomass feedstocks exist, and algal feedstocks for bioenergy have not yet reached commercialisation. However, algae production has the potential to unlock significantly more sustainable biomass that can speed up the pathway to net zero and/or provide additional biomass feedstocks beyond net zero.
tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.description: Micro-algae can be grown in open ponds, closed photobioreactors, or in heterotrophic bioreactors (no light required). Once grown, the whole algae cell is hydrothermally liquefied (HTL) via the same process used for terrestrial biomass. The algal bio-oil is separated from the remaining products and can be sent to  hydrotreatment (common in petroleum refineries) to be upgraded to a drop-in biodiesel (renewable diesel). The remaining solids, water and carbon dioxide are treated and recycled to the cultivation step, while remaining off-gases provide heat, electricity or hydrogen. As with all algal biofuel systems, the main challenge arises from the high cost of harvesting and cultivation, as well as reducing the energy/water/nutrient/land footprint of the system. However, algae can be grown on non-arable land, avoiding competition with food. Benefits of using the HTL route with micro-algae include HTL's ability to handle wet feedstocks, and its use of all algae components (lipids, carbohydrates, proteins), removing the need to cultivate high lipid content and extraction.
tech_0379_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.NZErationale: Alternative biomass feedstocks exist, and algal feedstocks for bioenergy have not yet reached commercialisation. However, algae production has the potential to unlock significantly more sustainable biomass that can speed up the pathway to net zero and/or provide additional biomass feedstocks beyond net zero.

tech_0380_hydrotreating-of-micro-algae-b.technology_name_zh: 微藻加氫處理（生質柴油、生質航煤）
tech_0380_hydrotreating-of-micro-algae-b.technology_status_summary_zh: 培養微藻以生產脂質，可於開放式池塘、封閉式光生物反應器或異營式生物反應器（無需光照）中培養，從藻類萃取脂質（油）後，製程與傳統氫化或加氫處理植物油（HVO）相似，在觸媒存在下對油原料加氫，將三酸甘油酯轉化為長鏈碳氫化合物，可視為即插即用型再生柴油，主要技術挑戰在於。
tech_0380_hydrotreating-of-micro-algae-b.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在替代生質原料，且藻類生質能原料尚未達到商業化，然而藻類生產有潛力釋放更多永續生質原料，可加速邁向淨零的路徑，及/或在淨零之後提供額外的生質原料。
tech_0380_hydrotreating-of-micro-algae-b.technology_status_summary_en: Micro-algae are grown to produce lipids. They can be grown in open ponds, closed photobioreactors, or in heterotrophic bioreactors (no light required). Once the lipids (oils) are extracted from the algae, the process is similar to traditional hydrogenated or hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO). Hydrogen is added to the oil feedstock in the presence of a catalyst to convert triglycerides into long-chained hydrocarbons that can be  considered a renewable diesel (drop-in). The main technical challenge with this technology is producing algae with high lipid content and then extracting the lipids efficiently. A major benefit of algae-based biofuels is their potential to produce biofuels without competition with food, as algae can be grown on non-arable land, though it has high land/nutrient/energy/water usage.
tech_0380_hydrotreating-of-micro-algae-b.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative biomass feedstocks exist, and algal feedstocks for bioenergy have not yet reached commercialisation. However, algae production has the potential to unlock significantly more sustainable biomass that can speed up the pathway to net zero and/or provide additional biomass feedstocks beyond net zero.

tech_0381_pyrolysis-and-upgrading-biodie.technology_name_zh: 熱裂解與升級（生質柴油）
tech_0381_pyrolysis-and-upgrading-biodie.technology_status_summary_zh: 在熱裂解中，生質原料於無氧狀態下加熱，分解為生質油與生物碳。乾燥生質原料（含水量最高10%）的快速熱裂解（約以秒計）通常用於產出以生質油為主的輸出，生質油產出後，可透過標準煉油裝置（流體觸媒裂解機、加氫裂解機）精煉為柴油等更高品質燃料，生質油可與化石原油共同送入煉油廠處理。
tech_0381_pyrolysis-and-upgrading-biodie.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在成熟度更高的替代生質燃料。
tech_0381_pyrolysis-and-upgrading-biodie.technology_status_summary_en: In pyrolysis, biomass is heated in the absence of oxygen and decomposes into bio-oil and biochar. Fast pyrolysis (on the order of seconds) of dry biomass (up to 10% moisture content) is typically used to produce an output that is mostly bio-oil. Once produced, the pyrolysis bio-oil can then be refined to higher quality fuels such as diesel via standard petroleum refining units (fluid catalytic crackers, hydrocrackers). The bio-oil can be co-fed into refineries with crude fossil-based oil.
tech_0381_pyrolysis-and-upgrading-biodie.market_dynamics_summary_en: There are alternative biofuels available at higher TRL.
tech_0381_pyrolysis-and-upgrading-biodie.description: In pyrolysis, biomass is heated in the absence of oxygen and decomposes into bio-oil and biochar. Fast pyrolysis (on the order of seconds) of dry biomass (up to 10% moisture content) is typically used to produce an output that is mostly bio-oil. Once produced, the pyrolysis bio-oil can then be refined to higher quality fuels such as diesel via standard petroleum refining units (fluid catalytic crackers, hydrocrackers). The bio-oil can be co-fed into refineries with crude fossil-based oil.
tech_0381_pyrolysis-and-upgrading-biodie.NZErationale: There are alternative biofuels available at higher TRL.

initiatives.description: The ReFuelEU Aviation initiative in the EU proposes sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) blending obligations for EU fuel suppliers, starting with 2% SAF in 2025 and rising to 70% by 2050.

Launched in 2024, the SEAFAIRER project is a Horizon Europe consortium (€10 million budget), led by DTU with Fraunhofer UMSICHT as key technology partner, that aims to scale its VINTER ("Vertical Intermediate Thermochemical Conversion") pyrolysis process to a demonstration level (at least 250 kg/h) to produce biocrude, syngas and biochar from rice husk, biowaste and agave bagasse, and deliver 6 kL of marine biofuel for a 30-day ship trial out of Valencia. Two pathways are explored: one with direct use as a marine fuel (10-30% blend); another involving upgrading by Galp (for 50-100% blends). In 2025, the demonstration unit was completed, targeting operation in 2026.

In 2024, Ensyn invested USD 9 million in the Castlerock Biofuels project to create a 20 million gallon/year renewable fuel oil production facility in Millinocket (Maine) through fast pyroysis of forest residuals, targeting industrial and institutional heating. The facility will involve the advanced thermal processing (RTP®) technology and is expected to be operational by 2027.

The five-year Horizon 2020 To-Syn-Fuel project, developed by Fraunhofer UMSICHT in Markt Hohenburg (Bavaria) ended in 2022 and demonstrated Thermo-Catalytic Reforming (TCR® intermediate/fast pyrolysis), hydrodeoxygenation (HDO) and pressure swing adsorption (PSA) to convert sewage sludge (design 500 dry kg/h) into biocrude oil, proving the technology at TRL 7.

tech_0382_synthetic-iso-paraffins.technology_status_summary_zh: 又稱「糖轉碳氫化合物」路線，此路徑將生質原料中的糖分直接轉化為類似柴油與噴射燃油的碳氫化合物，生物路線使用微生物進行轉化，觸媒路線則在高溫條件下使用觸媒，合成異構烷烴是通過美國材料試驗協會（ASTM）認證的永續航空燃料（SAF），可摻混高達10%。
tech_0382_synthetic-iso-paraffins.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在淨零世界中，永續航空燃料（SAF）的需求龐大，而SAF的其他替代方案成本高昂或技術上不可行（如長程航班電氣化）。
tech_0382_synthetic-iso-paraffins.technology_status_summary_en: Also known as the "sugars to hydrocarbons" route, this pathway converts sugars from biomass directly into hydrocarbons similar to diesel and jet fuel. Biological routes use micro-organisms to perform the conversion, while catalytic routes use catalysts under high temperature conditions. Synthetic Iso-Paraffins is an American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 10%.
tech_0382_synthetic-iso-paraffins.market_dynamics_summary_en: The demand for sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) in a net-zero world is large, and other alternatives to SAFs are expensive or technically infeasible (e.g. electrifying long-haul flights)
tech_0382_synthetic-iso-paraffins.description: Also known as the "sugars to hydrocarbons" route, this pathway converts sugars from biomass directly into hydrocarbons similar to diesel and jet fuel. Biological routes use micro-organisms to perform the conversion, while catalytic routes use catalysts under high temperature conditions. Synthetic Iso-Paraffins is an American Society for Testing and Materials (ASTM)-certified sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), allowed to be blended up to 10%.
tech_0382_synthetic-iso-paraffins.NZErationale: The demand for sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) in a net-zero world is large, and other alternatives to SAFs are expensive or technically infeasible (e.g. electrifying long-haul flights)

tech_0383_transesterification-of-micro-a.technology_name: Transesterification of micro-algae to fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) (biodiesel)
tech_0383_transesterification-of-micro-a.technology_name_zh: 微藻酯轉化製脂肪酸甲酯（FAME）（生質柴油）
tech_0383_transesterification-of-micro-a.technology_status_summary_zh: 培養微藻以生產脂質，可於開放式池塘、封閉式光生物反應器或異營式生物反應器（無需光照）中培養，從藻類萃取脂質（油）後，製程與傳統脂肪酸甲酯（FAME）生質柴油生產相似，油原料與甲醇在觸媒存在下反應產生生質柴油與甘油，微藻的主要挑戰在於培養與採收成本高昂。
tech_0383_transesterification-of-micro-a.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在替代生質原料，且藻類生質能原料尚未達到商業化，然而藻類生產有潛力釋放更多永續生質原料，可加速邁向淨零的路徑，及/或在淨零之後提供額外的生質原料。
tech_0383_transesterification-of-micro-a.technology_status_summary_en: Micro-algae are grown to produce lipids. They can be grown in open ponds, closed photobioreactors, or in heterotrophic bioreactors (no light required). Once the lipids (oils) are extracted from the algae, the process is similar to traditional fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) biodiesel production, in which the oil feedstock is reacted with methanol in the presence of a catalyst to produce biodiesel and glycerine. The main challenge with micro-algae is the high cost of cultivation and harvesting (lipid extraction) compared to terrestrial biomass; other issues arise from lipid content, reducing energy/water/nutrient/land footprint, and integrating the full process pathway at demonstration scale. On the other hand, algae can be grown on non-arable land, avoiding competition with food. It can also be grown rapidly, and exhibits high photosynthetic efficiency, potentially leading to greater biofuel per unit area yields compared to terrestrial biomass.
tech_0383_transesterification-of-micro-a.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative biomass feedstocks exist, and algal feedstocks for bioenergy have not yet reached commercialisation. However, algae production has the potential to unlock significantly more sustainable biomass that can speed up the pathway to net zero and/or provide additional biomass feedstocks beyond net zero.

tech_0384_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_name: Hydrothermal liquefaction and upgrading of micro-algae (biodiesel, biokerosene)
tech_0384_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_name_zh: 微藻水熱液化與升級（生質柴油、生質航煤）
tech_0384_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_status_summary_zh: 微藻可於開放式池塘、封閉式光生物反應器或異營式生物反應器（無需光照）中培養，培養完成後，整個藻類細胞經水熱液化（HTL）處理，製程與陸生生質原料相同，藻類生質油與其餘產物分離後，可送至加氫處理（煉油廠常見）升級為即插即用型生質柴油（再生柴油），其餘固體、水與二氧化碳經處理後回收再利用。
tech_0384_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在替代生質原料，且藻類生質能原料尚未達到商業化，然而藻類生產有潛力釋放更多永續生質原料，可加速邁向淨零的路徑，及/或在淨零之後提供額外的生質原料。
tech_0384_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.technology_status_summary_en:
Micro-algae can be grown in open ponds, closed photobioreactors, or in heterotrophic bioreactors (no light required). Once grown, the whole algae cell is hydrothermally liquefied (HTL) via the same process used for terrestrial biomass. The algal bio-oil is separated from the remaining products and can be sent to  hydrotreatment (common in petroleum refineries) to be upgraded to a drop-in biodiesel (renewable diesel). The remaining solids, water and carbon dioxide are treated and recycled to the cultivation step, while remaining off-gases provide heat, electricity or hydrogen. As with all algal biofuel systems, the main challenge arises from the high cost of harvesting and cultivation, as well as reducing the energy/water/nutrient/land footprint of the system. However, algae can be grown on non-arable land, avoiding competition with food. Benefits of using the HTL route with micro-algae include HTL's ability to handle wet feedstocks, and its use of all algae components (lipids, carbohydrates, proteins), removing the need to cultivate high lipid content and extraction.

Micro-algae are grown to produce lipids. They can be grown in open ponds, closed photobioreactors, or in heterotrophic bioreactors (no light required). Once the lipids (oils) are extracted from the algae, the process is similar to traditional hydrogenated or hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO). Hydrogen is added to the oil feedstock in the presence of a catalyst to convert triglycerides into long-chained hydrocarbons that can be  considered a renewable diesel (drop-in). The main technical challenge with this technology is producing algae with high lipid content and then extracting the lipids efficiently. A major benefit of algae-based biofuels is their potential to produce biofuels without competition with food, as algae can be grown on non-arable land, though it has high land/nutrient/energy/water usage.

Micro-algae are grown to produce lipids. They can be grown in open ponds, closed photobioreactors, or in heterotrophic bioreactors (no light required). Once the lipids (oils) are extracted from the algae, the process is similar to traditional fatty acid methyl ester (FAME) biodiesel production, in which the oil feedstock is reacted with methanol in the presence of a catalyst to produce biodiesel and glycerine. The main challenge with micro-algae is the high cost of cultivation and harvesting (lipid extraction) compared to terrestrial biomass; other issues arise from lipid content, reducing energy/water/nutrient/land footprint, and integrating the full process pathway at demonstration scale. On the other hand, algae can be grown on non-arable land, avoiding competition with food. It can also be grown rapidly, and exhibits high photosynthetic efficiency, potentially leading to greater biofuel per unit area yields compared to terrestrial biomass.

Similar to anaerobic digestion of non-algae feedstock, except that the feedstock is either the remnants of micro-algae after lipid extraction or macro-algae (seaweed). The  bacteria break down the algae without oxygen and in the process produce biogas, composed mostly of methane (50-75%) and carbon dioxide (25-45%). Biomass can be in the form of animal manure, organic portion of municipal solid waste (MSW), industrial waste such as dry distillers grain (DDG) from ethanol production, agricultural residues and energy crops. The biogas can be burned directly, without upgrading to biomethane.
tech_0384_hydrothermal-liquefaction-and-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative biomass feedstocks exist, and algal feedstocks for bioenergy have not yet reached commercialisation. However, algae production has the potential to unlock significantly more sustainable biomass that can speed up the pathway to net zero and/or provide additional biomass feedstocks beyond net zero.

initiatives.description: The US Department of Energy announced up to $10 million for algal systems research and development to expand US bioenergy feedstock.
In 2025, AECOM launched a 1-year pilot plant in Lakeland (Florida) to remove algae from Lake Bonnet, sent to Genifuel for conversion into SAF.
Euglena has developed its "Susteo" biodiesel, made of a blend of oil extracted from its "Euglena" microalgae and used cooking oil, which was tested in a 90-minute private flight.
In 2021, Honeywell announced its UOP Ecofining™ technology supported the world’s first jet flights using sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) produced from algal oil by IHI Corporation.

tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_name: Enzymatic fermentation of lignocellulosic biomass with integrated CO2 capture (bioethanol)
tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_name_zh: 木質纖維素生質原料酵素發酵整合二氧化碳捕捉（生質乙醇）
tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_status_summary_zh: 在發酵步驟中，會排放純度極高的二氧化碳流，由於其純度高，可以相對低成本進行捕捉與壓縮，由於捕捉的二氧化碳為生物源，若後續加以封存，可產生負排放，有助於抵消能源系統其他部分的二氧化碳排放，詳情請見無CCUS版本。
tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 搭配碳捕捉封存（CCS）的木質纖維素乙醇，同時提供運輸用永續液態燃料與負排放。然而其他生質燃料路線（如生質合成油BtL）可使用相同生質原料，但生產無摻混上限的即插即用型燃料，此外道路運輸中很大一部分正日益電氣化。
tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_status_summary_en: During the fermentation step, a pure stream of CO2 is emitted that can be captured and compressed at relatively low cost due to the high purity of the stream. As the captured CO2 is biogenic, it can provide negative emissions if it is subsequently stored. This can help offset CO2 emissions in other parts of the energy system. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.market_dynamics_summary_en: Lignocellulosic ethanol with carbon capture and storage (CCS) provides both sustainable liquid fuel for transports and negative emissions. However, other biofuels routes such as biomass-to-liquid (BtL) can use the same biomass feedstock but produce drop-in fuels with no upper blend limits. Additionally, a significant portion of road transport is electrified.
tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.description: During the fermentation step, a pure stream of CO2 is emitted that can be captured and compressed at relatively low cost due to the high purity of the stream. As the captured CO2 is biogenic, it can provide negative emissions if it is subsequently stored. This can help offset CO2 emissions in other parts of the energy system. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0385_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.NZErationale: Lignocellulosic ethanol with carbon capture and storage (CCS) provides both sustainable liquid fuel for transports and negative emissions. However, other biofuels routes such as biomass-to-liquid (BtL) can use the same biomass feedstock but produce drop-in fuels with no upper blend limits. Additionally, a significant portion of road transport is electrified.

tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_name: Enzymatic fermentation of sugar or starch with integrated CO2 capture (bioethanol)
tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_name_zh: 糖或澱粉酵素發酵整合二氧化碳捕捉（生質乙醇）
tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_status_summary_zh: 在發酵步驟中，會排放純度極高的二氧化碳流，由於其純度高，可以相對低成本進行捕捉與壓縮，由於捕捉的二氧化碳為生物源，若後續加以封存，可產生負排放，有助於抵消能源系統其他部分的二氧化碳排放，詳情請見無CCUS版本。
tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 搭配碳捕捉封存的傳統乙醇，在淨零世界中仍能發揮作用，協助運輸部門去碳化，同時提供負排放，然而其角色受限於使用與糧食生產競爭的農作物。
tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_status_summary_en: During the fermentation step, a pure stream of CO2 is emitted that can be captured and compressed at relatively low cost due to the high purity of the stream. As the captured CO2 is biogenic, it can provide negative emissions if it is subsequently stored. This can help offset CO2 emissions in other parts of the energy system. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.market_dynamics_summary_en: Conventional ethanol with the addition of carbon capture and storage still plays a role in a net zero world to help decarbonise transport while providing negative emissions. However, its role is limited by its use of agricultural crops that compete with food production.
tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.description: During the fermentation step, a pure stream of CO2 is emitted that can be captured and compressed at relatively low cost due to the high purity of the stream. As the captured CO2 is biogenic, it can provide negative emissions if it is subsequently stored. This can help offset CO2 emissions in other parts of the energy system. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0386_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.NZErationale: Conventional ethanol with the addition of carbon capture and storage still plays a role in a net zero world to help decarbonise transport while providing negative emissions. However, its role is limited by its use of agricultural crops that compete with food production.

initiatives.description: In October 2025, Green Plains announced the startup of its CCS system at the York corn-ethanol biorefinery.
In November 2025, ADM announced the start of operations for a new carbon capture and storage project at its Columbus, Nebraska Corn Processing Complex.

tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_name: Enzymatic fermentation of lignocellulosic biomass without integrated CO2 capture (bioethanol)
tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_name_zh: 木質纖維素生質原料酵素發酵（不含二氧化碳捕捉）（生質乙醇）
tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_status_summary_zh: 透過酵素發酵製成的木質纖維素乙醇是一種先進（第二代）生質燃料，木質纖維素生質原料經酵素水解分解為糖分，接著發酵製成乙醇的流程與傳統（第一代）乙醇生產相同，雖然成本高於傳統乙醇，但木質纖維素乙醇使用的生質原料被視為殘餘物，因此不會與糧食資源直接競爭。
tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 不含碳捕捉封存（CCS）的木質纖維素乙醇是重要的橋接生質燃料，因其可取代以不永續原料生產的糖/澱粉乙醇，同時累積學習與發展經驗，以便未來部署搭配CCS的木質纖維素乙醇，然而其他生質燃料路線（如生質合成油BtL）可使用相同生質原料。
tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.technology_status_summary_en: Lignocellulosic ethanol via enzymatic fermentation is an advanced (second generation) biofuel where lignocellulosic biomass is broken down into sugars via enzymatic hydrolysis. From there, the fermentation process to produce ethanol is the same as conventional (first generation) ethanol production. Though more expensive than conventional ethanol, lignocellulosic ethanol uses a biomass feedstock that is considered residue and therefore does not have direct competition with food resources. Like conventional ethanol, its drawbacks are ethanol blend limits with gasoline (15% for use in gasoline engines, 85% for use in flex fuel vehicles, and 95% for use in compression ignition engines).
tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.market_dynamics_summary_en: Lignocellulosic ethanol without carbon capture and storage (CCS) is an important bridging biofuel, as it displaces sugar/starch ethanol produced from unsustainable feedstock while allowing learning and development so that lignocellulosic ethanol with CCS can be deployed. However, other biofuels routes such as biomass-to-liquid (BtL) can use the same biomass feedstock but produce drop-in fuels with no upper blend limits. Additionally, a significant portion of road transport is electrified.
tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.description: Lignocellulosic ethanol via enzymatic fermentation is an advanced (second generation) biofuel where lignocellulosic biomass is broken down into sugars via enzymatic hydrolysis. From there, the fermentation process to produce ethanol is the same as conventional (first generation) ethanol production. Though more expensive than conventional ethanol, lignocellulosic ethanol uses a biomass feedstock that is considered residue and therefore does not have direct competition with food resources. Like conventional ethanol, its drawbacks are ethanol blend limits with gasoline (15% for use in gasoline engines, 85% for use in flex fuel vehicles, and 95% for use in compression ignition engines).
tech_0387_enzymatic-fermentation-of-lign.NZErationale: Lignocellulosic ethanol without carbon capture and storage (CCS) is an important bridging biofuel, as it displaces sugar/starch ethanol produced from unsustainable feedstock while allowing learning and development so that lignocellulosic ethanol with CCS can be deployed. However, other biofuels routes such as biomass-to-liquid (BtL) can use the same biomass feedstock but produce drop-in fuels with no upper blend limits. Additionally, a significant portion of road transport is electrified.

initiatives.description: Brazilian ethanol producer Raizen in May 2024 announced the inauguration of a new 82 MMly (21.66 MMgy) cellulosic ethanol plant. The R$1.2 billion ($230.63 million) facility is located at Bonfim Bioenergy Park in Guariba, Sao Paulo, Brazil. Two other similar plants (Barra Bonita and Univalem in Valparaiso) started operation in 2025, and five others are planned or under construction (in Morro Agudo, Andradina, Caarapo and Taruma).

tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_name: Enzymatic fermentation of sugar or starch without integrated CO2 capture (bioethanol)
tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_name_zh: 糖或澱粉酵素發酵（不含二氧化碳捕捉）（生質乙醇）
tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_status_summary_zh: 由糖類與澱粉作物製成的生質乙醇被視為傳統（第一代）生質燃料，碳水化合物（糖分）經酵素發酵製成乙醇，產出的液態生質燃料可與汽油以最高15%比例摻混供一般汽油引擎使用，彈性燃料車輛可達85%，專用（壓燃式）乙醇引擎則可達95%。然而除摻混上限的挑戰外，使用糧食作物生產乙醇也存在永續性疑慮。
tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 傳統乙醇對第一代生質燃料供應鏈的發展一直很重要，但若無搭配碳捕捉封存（CCS），加上其原料與糧食生產的競爭，預期長期將完全淘汰。
tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.technology_status_summary_en: Bioethanol from sugar and starch crops is considered a conventional (first generation) biofuel. Carbohydrates (sugars) are enzymatically fermented into ethanol, producing a liquid biofuel that can be blended up to 15% with gasoline for any gasoline engine, and up to 85% for flex fuel vehicles, and 95% for dedicated (compression ignition) ethanol engines. However, in addition to challenges around blend limits, there are sustainability concerns with using food crops for ethanol production, which can lead to competition with food and undesirable land use change.
tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.market_dynamics_summary_en: Conventional ethanol has been important to the development of the first biofuel supply chains, but without associated carbon capture and storage (CCS), and given its feedstock competition with food, it is expected to be completely phased out in the long term.
tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.description: Bioethanol from sugar and starch crops is considered a conventional (first generation) biofuel. Carbohydrates (sugars) are enzymatically fermented into ethanol, producing a liquid biofuel that can be blended up to 15% with gasoline for any gasoline engine, and up to 85% for flex fuel vehicles, and 95% for dedicated (compression ignition) ethanol engines. However, in addition to challenges around blend limits, there are sustainability concerns with using food crops for ethanol production, which can lead to competition with food and undesirable land use change.
tech_0388_enzymatic-fermentation-of-suga.NZErationale: Conventional ethanol has been important to the development of the first biofuel supply chains, but without associated carbon capture and storage (CCS), and given its feedstock competition with food, it is expected to be completely phased out in the long term.

tech_0389_syngas-fermentation-bioethanol.technology_status_summary_zh: 合成氣（主要由氫氣[H2]、一氧化碳[CO]與二氧化碳[CO2]組成的混合氣體）以作為生物觸媒的微生物發酵製成乙醇及其他生質燃料（如丁醇、乙酸等），合成氣可透過多種途徑產生，包括將木質纖維素含量高的生質原料（如木材、稻草、林業與農業殘餘物、都市固體廢棄物）氣化，以及在限氧環境中加熱。合成氣也可透過使用其他方式製成。
tech_0389_syngas-fermentation-bioethanol.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在成熟度更高的替代乙醇生產路線，且乙醇搭配CCS在未來可提供寶貴的負排放。
tech_0389_syngas-fermentation-bioethanol.technology_status_summary_en: Syngas (a mixture of mostly hydrogen [H2], carbon monoxide [CO], and carbon dioxide [CO2]) is fermented to ethanol and other biofuels (e.g. butanol, acetic acid, etc.) using micro-organisms that function as bio-catalysts. Syngas can be produced via multiple routes, including gasification of biomass with high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) and via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment. Syngas can also be produced using off-gases from industrial processes like iron and steel manufacturing. However, when using fossil-derived syngas, the emissions reductions potential tend to be lower than using renewable sources of syngas.
tech_0389_syngas-fermentation-bioethanol.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative routes at higher TRL exist to create ethanol, and ethanol coupled with CCS provides valuable negative emissions in the future.
tech_0389_syngas-fermentation-bioethanol.description: Syngas (a mixture of mostly hydrogen [H2], carbon monoxide [CO], and carbon dioxide [CO2]) is fermented to ethanol and other biofuels (e.g. butanol, acetic acid, etc.) using micro-organisms that function as bio-catalysts. Syngas can be produced via multiple routes, including gasification of biomass with high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) and via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment. Syngas can also be produced using off-gases from industrial processes like iron and steel manufacturing. However, when using fossil-derived syngas, the emissions reductions potential tend to be lower than using renewable sources of syngas.
tech_0389_syngas-fermentation-bioethanol.NZErationale: Alternative routes at higher TRL exist to create ethanol, and ethanol coupled with CCS provides valuable negative emissions in the future.

tech_0390_anaerobic-digestion-without-bi.technology_name_zh: 厭氧消化（不含沼氣升級）
tech_0390_anaerobic-digestion-without-bi.technology_status_summary_zh: 厭氧消化是一種生物製程，由厭氧微生物群落在無氧狀態下分解有機物，將生質原料中的有機碳轉化為甲烷（CH4）與二氧化碳（CO2），主要產物為沼氣與消化液，沼氣是一種可燃氣體混合物，主要由甲烷（50-75%）與二氧化碳（25-50%）組成，並含少量硫化氫與微量氣體，消化液是殘餘的分解物質，通常富含養分。
tech_0390_anaerobic-digestion-without-bi.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 厭氧消化產生的沼氣通常含有高比例二氧化碳，這降低了其能量密度，若不升級則壓縮與長途運輸不具經濟效益，因此通常在產地附近使用，一般僅能以低壓儲槽短期儲存，限制了其靈活性與市場潛力。
tech_0390_anaerobic-digestion-without-bi.technology_status_summary_en:
Anaerobic digestion is a biological process in which a consortium of anaerobic microorganisms breaks down organic matter in the absence of oxygen, converting organic carbon in biomass into methane (CH₄) and carbon dioxide (CO2). The main products are biogas and digestate. Biogas is a combustible gas mixture composed mainly of methane (50–75%) and CO2 (25–50%), with smaller amounts of hydrogen sulfide and trace gases. Digestate is the residual decomposed material, typically rich in nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus, and can be used as a fertiliser. A wide variety of feedstocks can be used in anaerobic digestion, including animal manure and slurry, agricultural residues, dedicated energy crops (e.g. silage), food and agro-industrial waste, municipal organic waste and sewage sludge. Woody biomass is generally unsuitable without pretreatment because most anaerobic microorganisms cannot effectively degrade lignin. Anaerobic digesters were originally developed for sewage sludge and manure, but many modern facilities use co-digestion, combining multiple feedstocks, to improve gas yields and process stability.
The design and operating conditions of the anaerobic reactor – including temperature (typically mesophilic at 20–40 °C or thermophilic at 45–65 °C), solids content, retention time, mixing intensity and reactor configuration – depend strongly on feedstock characteristics. When biogas is not upgraded to biomethane and is used directly as a fuel, it is desulfurised and burned on site for heat and/or power generation.
tech_0390_anaerobic-digestion-without-bi.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biogas from anaerobic digestion typically contains a high share of CO2, which reduces its energy density and makes compression and long-distance transport uneconomic without upgrading. It is therefore most often used close to where it is produced and can generally only be stored for short periods in low-pressure holders, limiting flexibility and making project viability dependent on nearby heat and/or electricity demand. However, it should be noted that anaerobic digestion plays an important role in the safe handling of organic waste, such as manure, sewage sludge and food waste. Where local energy demand exists, using biogas directly without upgrading can provide a lower-cost, simpler technical solution.
tech_0390_anaerobic-digestion-without-bi.description:
Anaerobic digestion is a biological process in which a consortium of anaerobic microorganisms breaks down organic matter in the absence of oxygen, converting organic carbon in biomass into methane (CH₄) and carbon dioxide (CO2). The main products are biogas and digestate. Biogas is a combustible gas mixture composed mainly of methane (50–75%) and CO2 (25–50%), with smaller amounts of hydrogen sulfide and trace gases. Digestate is the residual decomposed material, typically rich in nutrients such as nitrogen and phosphorus, and can be used as a fertiliser. Woody biomass is generally unsuitable without pretreatment because most anaerobic microorganisms cannot effectively degrade lignin. The design and operating conditions of the anaerobic reactor – including temperature (typically mesophilic at 20–40 °C or thermophilic at 45–65 °C), solids content, retention time, mixing intensity and reactor configuration – depend strongly on feedstock characteristics. When biogas is not upgraded to biomethane and is used directly as a fuel, it is desulfurised and burned on site for heat and/or power generation.

tech_0391_anaerobic-digestion-and-biolog.technology_name_zh: 厭氧消化與生物甲烷化（生質甲烷）
tech_0391_anaerobic-digestion-and-biolog.technology_status_summary_zh: 氫氣與厭氧消化產生的沼氣結合，透過生物轉化生成甲烷，微生物透過氫營養型甲烷生成作用，將原始沼氣中的二氧化碳與氫氣轉化為生質甲烷，避免了排放或捕捉沼氣中二氧化碳的需求，生物甲烷化可在厭氧消化槽內進行，也可在獨立的分離反應器中進行，生物甲烷化對進料氣體雜質的耐受性更高。
tech_0391_anaerobic-digestion-and-biolog.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 沼氣升級之所以重要，是因為從原始沼氣中去除二氧化碳可產生性質與天然氣相似的生質甲烷，大幅拓展其潛在用途。不同於原始沼氣，生質甲烷可透過現有天然氣基礎設施壓縮、儲存與運輸，可實現管網注入（即摻混，須符合部分條件）。
tech_0391_anaerobic-digestion-and-biolog.technology_status_summary_en: Hydrogen is combined with biogas from anaerobic digestion to produce methane via biological conversion. Micro-organisms convert the CO2 in raw biogas and the H2  to biomethane via hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis, avoiding the need to vent or capture the CO2 in the biogas. The biological methanation can occur either within the anaerobic digester, or in a separation reactor. Biological methanation is more resilient to feed gas impurities.
tech_0391_anaerobic-digestion-and-biolog.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biogas upgrading is important because removing CO2 from raw biogas creates biomethane that has similar properties to natural gas, which greatly increases its potential uses. Unlike raw biogas, biomethane can be compressed, stored and transported through existing natural gas infrastructure. This enables grid injection (i.e. blending, subject to some limits) and use across established gas applications. Thus, biomethane provides spatial and temporal flexibility that is lacking in biogas. Although anaerobic digestion remains important for safely handling organic waste, the extent to which it is deployed in the longer term, particularly when dedicated energy crops are used, depends on broader system trade-offs (e.g. competition for liquid fuels) and the evolution of natural gas demand. While gas demand continues, however, biomethane can serve as a direct substitute for fossil natural gas, contributing to the diversification of supply using domestic resources.
tech_0391_anaerobic-digestion-and-biolog.description: Hydrogen is combined with biogas from anaerobic digestion to produce methane via biological conversion. Micro-organisms convert the CO2 in raw biogas and the H2  to biomethane via hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis, avoiding the need to vent or capture the CO2 in the biogas. The biological methanation can occur either within the anaerobic digester, or in a separation reactor. Biological methanation is more resilient to feed gas impurities.
tech_0391_anaerobic-digestion-and-biolog.NZErationale: Biogas upgrading is important because removing CO2 from raw biogas creates biomethane that has similar properties to natural gas, which greatly increases its potential uses. Unlike raw biogas, biomethane can be compressed, stored and transported through existing natural gas infrastructure. This enables grid injection (i.e. blending, subject to some limits) and use across established gas applications. Thus, biomethane provides spatial and temporal flexibility that is lacking in biogas. Although anaerobic digestion remains important for safely handling organic waste, the extent to which it is deployed in the longer term, particularly when dedicated energy crops are used, depends on broader system trade-offs (e.g. competition for liquid fuels) and the evolution of natural gas demand. While gas demand continues, however, biomethane can serve as a direct substitute for fossil natural gas, contributing to the diversification of supply using domestic resources.

initiatives.description: In March 2025, Enosis inaugurated DENOBIO, an industrial-scale biological methanation unit, at an agricultural biogas site in Lesquielles-St-Germain (Hauts-de-France, France). Supplied by off-site electrolytic hydrogen (from Lhyfe), it recovers CO2 from a farm digester to produce methane, to be injected in the natural gas network.

Denmark strengthened its position in industrial-scale biogas plants with Glansager PtG’s facility in Sønderborg in 2023, with a methane production capacity of 35 GWh/yr using biological ex-situ methanation and producing e-methane from biogenic CO2 and renewable hydrogen.

tech_0392_anaerobic-digestion-and-cataly.technology_name_zh: 厭氧消化與觸媒甲烷化（生質甲烷）
tech_0392_anaerobic-digestion-and-cataly.technology_status_summary_zh: 與厭氧消化產製生質甲烷相似，但增加甲烷化步驟，進一步將沼氣中二氧化碳的碳含量轉化為甲烷，而非排放或捕捉二氧化碳，二氧化碳在觸媒存在下與氫氣反應，其效益是更有效利用沼氣中的生物碳，可取代化石來源的甲烷。
tech_0392_anaerobic-digestion-and-cataly.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 沼氣升級之所以重要，是因為從原始沼氣中去除二氧化碳可產生性質與天然氣相似的生質甲烷，大幅拓展其潛在用途。不同於原始沼氣，生質甲烷可透過現有天然氣基礎設施壓縮、儲存與運輸，可實現管網注入（即摻混，須符合部分條件）。
tech_0392_anaerobic-digestion-and-cataly.technology_status_summary_en: Similar to biomethane production from anaerobic digestion, with the addition of a methanation step to further convert the carbon content in the biogas CO2  to methane rather than venting or capturing the CO2. The CO2 is reacted with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst. The benefit is a more effective use of biogenic carbon present in biogas, which can displace fossil-derived methane.
tech_0392_anaerobic-digestion-and-cataly.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biogas upgrading is important because removing CO2 from raw biogas creates biomethane that has similar properties to natural gas, which greatly increases its potential uses. Unlike raw biogas, biomethane can be compressed, stored and transported through existing natural gas infrastructure. This enables grid injection (i.e. blending, subject to some limits) and use across established gas applications. Thus, biomethane provides spatial and temporal flexibility that is lacking in biogas. Although anaerobic digestion remains important for safely handling organic waste, the extent to which it is deployed in the longer term, particularly when dedicated energy crops are used, depends on broader system trade-offs (e.g. competition for liquid fuels) and the evolution of natural gas demand. While gas demand continues, however, biomethane can serve as a direct substitute for fossil natural gas, contributing to the diversification of supply using domestic resources.

The CO2 is reacted with hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst. The benefit is a more effective use of biogenic carbon present in biogas, which can displace fossil-derived methane.

initiatives.description: Germany is home to the largest commercially-operating power-to-gas plant, built and operated by Audi and using CO2 from an adjacent biogas plant. Hydrogen from water electrolysis and CO2 are fed into the catalytic methanation reactor to produce biomethane for use in road vehicles. The plant has been operating since 2013.

tech_0393_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_name: Anaerobic digestion and upgrading with CO2 capture (biomethane)
tech_0393_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_name_zh: 厭氧消化與升級搭配二氧化碳捕捉（生質甲烷）
tech_0393_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_status_summary_zh: 與厭氧消化產製生質甲烷相似，但在沼氣升級固有的二氧化碳分離過程中，增加二氧化碳捕捉與壓縮裝置，若二氧化碳經封存，可產生負排放，用以抵消能源系統其他部分難以減排的排放，較大型消化槽（逾5兆瓦）較適合進行碳捕捉封存（CCS）以合理化額外的資本支出，詳情請見無CCUS版本。
tech_0393_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 沼氣升級之所以重要，是因為從原始沼氣中去除二氧化碳可產生性質與天然氣相似的生質甲烷，大幅拓展其潛在用途。不同於原始沼氣，生質甲烷可透過現有天然氣基礎設施壓縮、儲存與運輸，可實現管網注入（即摻混，須符合部分條件）。
tech_0393_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_status_summary_en: Similar to biomethane production from anaerobic digestion, with the addition of a  CO2 capture and compression unit integrated into the CO2 separation inherent to biogas upgrading. If the CO2 is stored, negative emissions are created that can offset hard-to-abate emissions elsewhere in the energy system. Larger digestors (> 5 MW) are suitable for carbon capture and storage (CCS) to justify the additional capital expense. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0393_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biogas upgrading is important because removing CO2 from raw biogas creates biomethane that has similar properties to natural gas, which greatly increases its potential uses. Unlike raw biogas, biomethane can be compressed, stored and transported through existing natural gas infrastructure. This enables grid injection (i.e. blending, subject to some limits) and use across established gas applications. Thus, biomethane provides spatial and temporal flexibility that is lacking in biogas. Although anaerobic digestion remains important for safely handling organic waste, the extent to which it is deployed in the longer term, particularly when dedicated energy crops are used, depends on broader system trade-offs (e.g. competition for liquid fuels) and the evolution of natural gas demand. While gas demand continues, however, biomethane can serve as a direct substitute for fossil natural gas, contributing to the diversification of supply using domestic resources.
