If the CO2 is stored, negative emissions are created that can offset hard-to-abate emissions elsewhere in the energy system. Larger digestors (> 5 MW) are suitable for carbon capture and storage (CCS) to justify the additional capital expense. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0393_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.NZErationale: Biogas upgrading is important because removing CO2 from raw biogas creates biomethane that has similar properties to natural gas, which greatly increases its potential uses. Unlike raw biogas, biomethane can be compressed, stored and transported through existing natural gas infrastructure. This enables grid injection (i.e. blending, subject to some limits) and use across established gas applications. Thus, biomethane provides spatial and temporal flexibility that is lacking in biogas. Although anaerobic digestion remains important for safely handling organic waste, the extent to which it is deployed in the longer term, particularly when dedicated energy crops are used, depends on broader system trade-offs (e.g. competition for liquid fuels) and the evolution of natural gas demand. While gas demand continues, however, biomethane can serve as a direct substitute for fossil natural gas, contributing to the diversification of supply using domestic resources.

initiatives.description: The Moor Bioenergy plant (UK) began capturing CO2 as a by-product of upgrading. From startup, Moor was built with carbon capture tied to its biogas upgrader. By capturing 14 k tCO2/year, Future Biogas can sell it (via Pro Gases UK) for use in beverages and refrigeration. Future Biogas announced plans to retrofit CO2 capture at six other biogas plants and to design all seven new plants with CO2 capture.

tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_name: Anaerobic digestion and upgrading without CO2 capture (biomethane)
tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_name_zh: 厭氧消化與升級（不含二氧化碳捕捉）（生質甲烷）
tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_status_summary_zh: 在厭氧消化槽中，細菌在無氧狀態下分解生質原料，過程中產生沼氣，主要由甲烷（50-75%）與二氧化碳（25-45%）組成，生質原料可為動物糞便、都市固體廢棄物（MSW）中的有機部分、工業廢棄物（如乙醇生產的乾式蒸餾穀物DDG）、農業殘餘物與能源作物，沼氣透過去除二氧化碳及硫化氫等其他雜質進行升級，產出通常稱為生質甲烷。
tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 沼氣升級之所以重要，是因為從原始沼氣中去除二氧化碳可產生性質與天然氣相似的生質甲烷，大幅拓展其潛在用途。不同於原始沼氣，生質甲烷可透過現有天然氣基礎設施壓縮、儲存與運輸，可實現管網注入（即摻混，須符合部分條件）。
tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.technology_status_summary_en: In an anaerobic digestor,  bacteria break down to biomass without oxygen and in the process produce biogas, composed mostly of methane (50-75%) and carbon dioxide (25-45%). Biomass can be in the form of animal manure, organic portion of municipal solid waste (MSW), industrial waste such as dry distillers grain (DDG) from ethanol production, agricultural residues and energy crops. The biogas is upgraded by removing CO2 and other impurities such as hydrogen sulphide, producing what is commonly referred to as biomethane. Biomethane can be used directly or injected into the gas grid if it meets the required specifications. In some cases, biomethane needs to be mixed with LPG to increase its calorific potential before being injected.
tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.market_dynamics_summary_en: Biogas upgrading is important because removing CO2 from raw biogas creates biomethane that has similar properties to natural gas, which greatly increases its potential uses. Unlike raw biogas, biomethane can be compressed, stored and transported through existing natural gas infrastructure. This enables grid injection (i.e. blending, subject to some limits) and use across established gas applications. Thus, biomethane provides spatial and temporal flexibility that is lacking in biogas. Although anaerobic digestion remains important for safely handling organic waste, the extent to which it is deployed in the longer term, particularly when dedicated energy crops are used, depends on broader system trade-offs (e.g. competition for liquid fuels) and the evolution of natural gas demand. While gas demand continues, however, biomethane can serve as a direct substitute for fossil natural gas, contributing to the diversification of supply using domestic resources.
tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.description: In an anaerobic digestor,  bacteria break down to biomass without oxygen and in the process produce biogas, composed mostly of methane (50-75%) and carbon dioxide (25-45%). Biomass can be in the form of animal manure, organic portion of municipal solid waste (MSW), industrial waste such as dry distillers grain (DDG) from ethanol production, agricultural residues and energy crops. The biogas is upgraded by removing CO2 and other impurities such as hydrogen sulphide, producing what is commonly referred to as biomethane. Biomethane can be used directly or injected into the gas grid if it meets the required specifications. In some cases, biomethane needs to be mixed with LPG to increase its calorific potential before being injected.
tech_0394_anaerobic-digestion-and-upgrad.NZErationale: Biogas upgrading is important because removing CO2 from raw biogas creates biomethane that has similar properties to natural gas, which greatly increases its potential uses. Unlike raw biogas, biomethane can be compressed, stored and transported through existing natural gas infrastructure. This enables grid injection (i.e. blending, subject to some limits) and use across established gas applications. Thus, biomethane provides spatial and temporal flexibility that is lacking in biogas. Although anaerobic digestion remains important for safely handling organic waste, the extent to which it is deployed in the longer term, particularly when dedicated energy crops are used, depends on broader system trade-offs (e.g. competition for liquid fuels) and the evolution of natural gas demand. While gas demand continues, however, biomethane can serve as a direct substitute for fossil natural gas, contributing to the diversification of supply using domestic resources.

tech_0395_biomass-gasification-small-sca.technology_name_zh: 生質原料氣化－小規模（生質合成氣）
tech_0395_biomass-gasification-small-sca.technology_status_summary_zh: 生質原料可透過氣化熱轉化為氣態產物，木質纖維素含量高的生質原料（如木材、稻草、林業與農業殘餘物、都市固體廢棄物）在限氧環境中加熱但不燃燒，產生主要由氫氣（H2，20-30%）、一氧化碳（CO，約20%）、二氧化碳（CO2，約15%）及其他碳氫化合物組成的混合氣體，小規模氣化爐（低於200千瓦）可為偏遠村落提供燃料以產生熱與電。
tech_0395_biomass-gasification-small-sca.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 已存在為鄉村社區供電的替代能源系統，包括搭配儲能的太陽能與風能，以及連接國家電網系統。
tech_0395_biomass-gasification-small-sca.technology_status_summary_en: Biomass can be thermally converted to gaseous products via gasification. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is heated, but not combusted, in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2) (20-30%), carbon monoxide (CO) (~20%), carbon dioxide (CO2) (~15%), and other hydrocarbons. Small-scale gasifiers (< 200 kWe) can provide fuel to create heat and electricity for remote villages. It can replace burning biomass directly for cooking in the home, avoiding negative health impacts.
tech_0395_biomass-gasification-small-sca.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative energy systems exist for powering rural communities, including solar and wind coupled with storage, connection to a national grid system.
tech_0395_biomass-gasification-small-sca.description: Biomass can be thermally converted to gaseous products via gasification. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is heated, but not combusted, in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2) (20-30%), carbon monoxide (CO) (~20%), carbon dioxide (CO2) (~15%), and other hydrocarbons. Small-scale gasifiers (< 200 kWe) can provide fuel to create heat and electricity for remote villages. It can replace burning biomass directly for cooking in the home, avoiding negative health impacts.
tech_0395_biomass-gasification-small-sca.NZErationale: Alternative energy systems exist for powering rural communities, including solar and wind coupled with storage, connection to a national grid system.

tech_0396_biomass-gasification-and-biolo.technology_name_zh: 生質原料氣化與生物甲烷化（生質甲烷）
tech_0396_biomass-gasification-and-biolo.technology_status_summary_zh: 與生產生質合成天然氣（bioSNG）的觸媒甲烷化路線相似，生質原料先氣化為合成氣，再利用微生物將合成氣中的一氧化碳、二氧化碳與氫氣以生物方式轉化為生質甲烷。
tech_0396_biomass-gasification-and-biolo.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在成熟度更高的生質甲烷替代生產方法（如厭氧消化與二氧化碳分離），且未來生質甲烷需求有限。
tech_0396_biomass-gasification-and-biolo.technology_status_summary_en: Similar to the catalytic methanation route to produce bioSNG,  biomass is first gasified into syngas, and then the CO, CO2 and H2 in the syngas are biologically converted into biomethane via the use of microbes.
tech_0396_biomass-gasification-and-biolo.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative methods for biomethane production exist (e.g. anaerobic digestion and CO2 separation) at a higher TRL, and the future demand for biomethane is limited.
tech_0396_biomass-gasification-and-biolo.description: Similar to the catalytic methanation route to produce bioSNG,  biomass is first gasified into syngas, and then the CO, CO2 and H2 in the syngas are biologically converted into biomethane via the use of microbes.
tech_0396_biomass-gasification-and-biolo.NZErationale: Alternative methods for biomethane production exist (e.g. anaerobic digestion and CO2 separation) at a higher TRL, and the future demand for biomethane is limited.

tech_0397_biomass-gasification-and-catal.technology_name_zh: 生質原料氣化與觸媒甲烷化（生質甲烷）
tech_0397_biomass-gasification-and-catal.technology_status_summary_zh: 常稱為生質合成天然氣（bioSNG）路線，生質原料先氣化為合成氣，合成氣再透過甲烷化轉化為生質甲烷，木質纖維素含量高的生質原料（如木材、稻草、林業與農業殘餘物、都市固體廢棄物）在限氧環境中加熱氣化，產生主要由氫氣、一氧化碳、二氧化碳及其他碳氫化合物組成的混合氣體，此「合成氣」隨後經淨化。
tech_0397_biomass-gasification-and-catal.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在成熟度更高的生質甲烷替代生產方法（如厭氧消化與二氧化碳分離），且隨著各部門日益電氣化，未來生質甲烷需求有限。
tech_0397_biomass-gasification-and-catal.technology_status_summary_en: Often referred to as the bio-synthetic natural gas (bioSNG)  route, biomass is first gasified into syngas and the syngas is then converted into biomethane via methanation. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is gasified via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and other hydrocarbons. This "syngas" is then cleaned, CO2 is removed and vented, and the remaining syngas is dried before undergoing catalytic methanation. Prior to methanation, a partial water-gas shift (WGS) reaction may be used to adjust the H2/CO ratio. Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification.
tech_0397_biomass-gasification-and-catal.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative methods for biomethane production exist (e.g. anaerobic digestion and CO2 separation) at a higher TRL, and the future demand for biomethane is limited as sectors are increasingly electrified.
tech_0397_biomass-gasification-and-catal.description: Often referred to as the bio-synthetic natural gas (bioSNG)  route, biomass is first gasified into syngas and the syngas is then converted into biomethane via methanation. Biomass with a high lignocellulosic content (e.g. wood, straw, residues from forestry and agriculture, municipal solid waste) is gasified via heating in an oxygen-restricted environment, producing a mixture of mostly hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), and other hydrocarbons. This "syngas" is then cleaned, CO2 is removed and vented, and the remaining syngas is dried before undergoing catalytic methanation. Prior to methanation, a partial water-gas shift (WGS) reaction may be used to adjust the H2/CO ratio. Technical challenges revolve around tar buildup and removal during gasification.
tech_0397_biomass-gasification-and-catal.NZErationale: Alternative methods for biomethane production exist (e.g. anaerobic digestion and CO2 separation) at a higher TRL, and the future demand for biomethane is limited as sectors are increasingly electrified.

initiatives.description: In 2025, Engie and CMA-CGM cancelled the Salamandre project (announced in 2022), that planned to produce 11 ktpa biomethane from wood residues and solid waste through gasification and Enercat's catalytic methanation, starting in 2026.

tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.technology_name: Biomass gasification and methanation integrated with CO2 capture (biomethane)
tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.technology_name_zh: 生質原料氣化與甲烷化整合二氧化碳捕捉（生質甲烷）
tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.technology_status_summary_zh: 與生質原料氣化及觸媒甲烷化路線（即bioSNG路線）相似，但在甲烷化前的合成氣淨化過程中的二氧化碳去除步驟後，增加二氧化碳捕捉與壓縮，由於製程中固有產生純度高的二氧化碳流，加入碳捕捉封存（CCS）相對容易，將二氧化碳封存而非利用可產生負排放，可抵消能源系統其他部分難以減排的排放，詳情請見無CCUS版本。
tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 存在成熟度更高的生質甲烷替代生產方法（如厭氧消化與二氧化碳分離），且未來生質甲烷需求有限。
tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.technology_status_summary_en: Similar to the biomass gasification and catalytic methanation route (aka the bioSNG route), but with the addition of CO2 capture and compression following the CO2 removal step during syngas cleaning prior to methanation. Adding carbon capture and storage (CCS) is relatively easy given the pure stream of CO2 inherently produced in the process. Storing the CO2 rather than utilising it creates negative emissions that can offset hard-to-abate emissions elsewhere in the energy system. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.market_dynamics_summary_en: Alternative methods for biomethane production exist (e.g. anaerobic digestion and CO2 separation), and the future demand for biomethane is limited.
tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.description: Similar to the biomass gasification and catalytic methanation route (aka the bioSNG route), but with the addition of CO2 capture and compression following the CO2 removal step during syngas cleaning prior to methanation. Adding carbon capture and storage (CCS) is relatively easy given the pure stream of CO2 inherently produced in the process. Storing the CO2 rather than utilising it creates negative emissions that can offset hard-to-abate emissions elsewhere in the energy system. See non-CCUS variant for more detail.
tech_0398_biomass-gasification-and-metha.NZErationale: Alternative methods for biomethane production exist (e.g. anaerobic digestion and CO2 separation), and the future demand for biomethane is limited.

tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.technology_name: Post-combustion: chemical absorption (biomass with CO2 capture)
tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.technology_name_zh: 燃燒後捕捉：化學吸收（生質能搭配二氧化碳捕捉）
tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.technology_status_summary_zh: 在採用燃燒後化學吸收捕捉的生質能發電廠中，二氧化碳透過化學溶劑（如胺類）從燃燒煙道氣中分離，二氧化碳於高溫下釋出，溶劑再生後供繼續運作。
tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可提供所需的負排放，以抵消能源系統其他部分剩餘的正排放，進而達成淨零目標。
tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.technology_status_summary_en: At a biomass-fired power plant with post-combustion capture using chemical absorption, the CO2 is separated from the combustion flue gas by using a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at high temperature and the solvent regenerated for further operation.
tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.market_dynamics_summary_en: Technology can provide negative emissions needed to offset remaining positive emissions in other parts of the energy system in order to reach net zero.
tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.description: At a biomass-fired power plant with post-combustion capture using chemical absorption, the CO2 is separated from the combustion flue gas by using a chemical solvent (e.g. amine-based). The CO2 is released at high temperature and the solvent regenerated for further operation.
tech_0399_post-combustion-chemical-absor.NZErationale: Technology can provide negative emissions needed to offset remaining positive emissions in other parts of the energy system in order to reach net zero.

initiatives.description: Ørsted is making steady construction progress at the Avedøre Power Station, part of the Ørsted Kalundborg CO2 Hub. Key absorber, desorber, and cooler units have been installed, marking a major milestone toward completing Denmark’s first full-scale carbon capture and storage value chain. Once operational in 2026, the Avedøre facility could capture 150 000 tCO2 per year of biogenic emissions from Ørsted’s biomass-fired heat and power plants for transport and storage via Northern Lights. In April 2023, two combined bio-fired heat and power plants in Denmark were awarded a contract by the Danish Energy Agency to capture around 0.4 Mt CO2 per year as early as 2026. Construction started on the two capture units in December 2023.
SLB Capturi installed world-first large-scale (0.1 Mt CO2/yr) post-combustion chemical absorption capture unit at Twence waste incinerator.

In June 2024, the Twence's innovative CO2 capture plant In 2024, the United Kingdom’s first carbon capture pilot on a waste-to-energy plant started capturing 1 tonne of CO2 per day at Enfinium Ferrybridge site, around 50% of which is of biogenic origin.

In May 2024, Stockholm-based Exergi signed a contract with Microsoft covering 3.3 Mt of removals from its planned bioenergy with carbon capture and storage (BECCS) plant in Värtan, Stockholm. The same month, Denmark’s Orsted signed a contract with Microsoft selling a further 1 Mt of removals from its planned BECCS project at Avedore Power station. With the 2.67 Mt CO2 already contracted between the two companies in 2023, this brings the total purchase under contract to 3.67 Mt CO2, the largest amount to date contracted between two companies.

Stockholm Exergi has reached final investment decision (FID) to build one of the world’s largest bioenergy carbon capture and storage (BECCS) facilities at its Värtaverket plant in Stockholm. The SEK 13 billion project will capture and permanently store about 800 000 tCO2 per year from biomass-fired flue gases, with operations planned for 2028. The captured CO2 will be transported and stored by Northern Lights.

Hafslund Celsio is constructing a carbon capture facility at its Klemetsrud waste-to-energy plant in Oslo, with civil works underway and installation of the capture unit set to start in spring 2026. The project will capture about 350 000 tCO2 per year—half from biogenic sources—for shipment to Northern Lights for permanent storage. In June 2025, Microsoft signed a ten-year offtake agreement to purchase over 1 million t of carbon removal credits from the plant once it begins operation in 2029.

Technip Energies has been selected by Viridor to perform FEED for the carbon capture and storage project at the Runcorn Energy-from-Waste facility. The design, using the Canopy by T.EN™ solution powered by Shell CANSOLV® technology, will target capture of about 900 000 tCO2 per year, half from biogenic sources, effectively removing 450 000 tCO2 annually from the atmosphere. The captured CO2 will be transported for permanent storage through the HyNet cluster.

As of October 2025, Encyclis has begun construction of the Protos carbon capture and storage facility at the Protos Energy Recovery Plant near Ellesmere Port, Cheshire. The project, delivered by Kanadevia Inova as EPC contractor, could capture around 370 000 tCO2 per year from waste combustion flue gas. Captured CO2 will be transported via ENI’s pipeline to Liverpool Bay for permanent storage. The facility, supported by the UK Government, is expected to be operational by mid-2029.

tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.technology_name: Post-combustion: solid adsorption (biomass with CO2 capture)
tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.technology_name_zh: 燃燒後捕捉：固體吸附（生質能搭配二氧化碳捕捉）
tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.technology_status_summary_zh: 在固體吸附法中，二氧化碳透過固體吸附劑（如沸石、金屬有機骨架）從燃燒煙道氣中分離，相較胺類化學吸收，固體吸附劑再生能耗較低，吸附選擇性較高。
tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 固體吸附可作為比化學吸收成本更低的替代方案，並可避免胺類降解相關的潛在環境影響。
tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.technology_status_summary_en: In solid adsorption, the carbon dioxide is separated from the combustion flue gas by using a solid sorbent (e.g. zeolites, metal organic framework). Compared to amine-based chemical absorption, solid adsorbents can have lower regeneration energy and greater adsorption selectivity.
tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.market_dynamics_summary_en: Solid adsorption can be a lower cost alternative to chemical absorption and avoid potential environmental impact related to amine degradation.
tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.description: In solid adsorption, the carbon dioxide is separated from the combustion flue gas by using a solid sorbent (e.g. zeolites, metal organic framework). Compared to amine-based chemical absorption, solid adsorbents can have lower regeneration energy and greater adsorption selectivity.
tech_0400_post-combustion-solid-adsorpti.NZErationale: Solid adsorption can be a lower cost alternative to chemical absorption and avoid potential environmental impact related to amine degradation.

initiatives.description: Enfinium and Kanadevia Inova are deploying a carbon capture pilot at the Parc Adfer energy-from-waste facility in Deeside. The pilot will run for at least six months as part of Enfinium’s plan to invest ~£1.7 bn in CCS technology.

Nuada has completed a pilot using its MOF-VPSA carbon capture technology at the Energy Innovation Centre in Sheffield. The system captured around 1 tCO2 per day from biomass and waste-to-energy flue gas, demonstrating stable and low-energy performance with up to 90 % lower energy demand than conventional processes.

Nuada’s carbon capture solution is now operational at the Enfinium Ferrybridge-1 energy-from-waste facility in West Yorkshire. The pilot uses metal-organic framework (MOF) sorbents and vacuum swing adsorption to capture CO2 from the flue gas. The demonstration will run for at least six months in an industrial setting.

tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.technology_name: Pre-combustion: physical absorption (biomass with CO2 capture)
tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.technology_name_zh: 燃燒前捕捉：物理吸收（生質能搭配二氧化碳捕捉）
tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.technology_status_summary_zh: 在整合氣化聯合循環生質能發電廠中，生質原料被氣化為由氫氣與一氧化碳組成的合成氣，合成氣經水煤氣變換（WGS）反應轉化，產生額外氫氣並將一氧化碳轉化為二氧化碳，二氧化碳隨後透過吸附等物理分離製程從變換後合成氣中捕捉，之後剩餘氫氣於複循環燃氣渦輪機中燃燒。
tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 此技術可提供所需的負排放（前提是二氧化碳經永久封存），以抵消能源系統其他部分剩餘的正排放，進而達成淨零目標。
tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.technology_status_summary_en: In an integrated gasification combined-cycle biomass power plant, biomass is gasified into a synthesis gas, consisting of hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The synthesis gas is shifted in a water-gas-shift (WGS) reaction to produce additional hydrogen and convert the carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide. The CO2 is then captured from the shifted syngas using physical separation processes, such as adsorption, and afterward, the remaining hydrogen (H2) is combusted in a combined-cycle gas turbine that generates power.
tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.market_dynamics_summary_en: Technology can provide negative emissions (provided that the CO2 is permanently stored) needed to offset remaining positive emissions in other parts of the energy system in order to reach net zero.
tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.description: In an integrated gasification combined-cycle biomass power plant, biomass is gasified into a synthesis gas, consisting of hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The synthesis gas is shifted in a water-gas-shift (WGS) reaction to produce additional hydrogen and convert the carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide. The CO2 is then captured from the shifted syngas using physical separation processes, such as adsorption, and afterward, the remaining hydrogen (H2) is combusted in a combined-cycle gas turbine that generates power.
tech_0401_pre-combustion-physical-absorp.NZErationale: Technology can provide negative emissions (provided that the CO2 is permanently stored) needed to offset remaining positive emissions in other parts of the energy system in order to reach net zero.

tech_0402_millimeter-wave-drilling.technology_status_summary_zh: 毫米波鑽井是地熱能領域一項新興技術，利用極高頻電磁波高精準度穿透岩層，與傳統機械鑽井不同，此技術可產生鑽孔，同時降低設備磨損，並將對周圍岩石的熱或機械應力降至最低，有望提升鑽井速度並延長井壽命，其非接觸特性也讓在困難地質條件下鑽井成為可能。
tech_0402_millimeter-wave-drilling.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 透過提升地熱井建造效率並降低環境衝擊，毫米波鑽井可支持再生地熱能的擴展，有助於實現淨零能源目標。
tech_0402_millimeter-wave-drilling.technology_status_summary_en: Millimetre wave drilling is an emerging technique in geothermal energy that uses extremely high-frequency electromagnetic waves to penetrate rock formations with high precision. Unlike conventional mechanical drilling, it can create boreholes with reduced wear on equipment and minimal thermal or mechanical stress on surrounding rock, potentially increasing drilling speed and well longevity. Its non-contact nature also allows for drilling in challenging geological conditions that would be difficult for traditional methods.
tech_0402_millimeter-wave-drilling.market_dynamics_summary_en: By improving the efficiency and reducing the environmental impact of geothermal well construction, millimetre wave drilling can support the expansion of renewable geothermal energy, contributing to net zero energy goals.
tech_0402_millimeter-wave-drilling.description: Millimetre wave drilling is an emerging technique in geothermal energy that uses extremely high-frequency electromagnetic waves to penetrate rock formations with high precision. Unlike conventional mechanical drilling, it can create boreholes with reduced wear on equipment and minimal thermal or mechanical stress on surrounding rock, potentially increasing drilling speed and well longevity. Its non-contact nature also allows for drilling in challenging geological conditions that would be difficult for traditional methods.
tech_0402_millimeter-wave-drilling.NZErationale: By improving the efficiency and reducing the environmental impact of geothermal well construction, millimetre wave drilling can support the expansion of renewable geothermal energy, contributing to net zero energy goals.

initiatives.description: Quaise’s millimeter-wave drilling uses high-power electromagnetic beams to melt and vaporize rock, and has now been demonstrated in the field as a new method for rapidly drilling deep geothermal wells.

tech_0403_directional-steel-shot-drillin.technology_status_summary_zh: 定向鋼珠鑽井是一種利用高速鋼珠沿受控軌跡侵蝕岩石的技術，可精準、可操控地形成鑽孔，與傳統旋轉鑽井不同，此技術可降低鑽頭的機械磨損，並能在複雜地質構造中導航，特別適用於進入難以到達的地熱儲層，或從單一井孔開鑿多個分支。
tech_0403_directional-steel-shot-drillin.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 透過提升鑽井效率並降低設備能耗與停機時間，定向鋼珠鑽井有助於擴大地熱能生產，支持淨零排放目標。
tech_0403_directional-steel-shot-drillin.technology_status_summary_en: Directional steel-shot drilling is a technique that uses high-velocity steel pellets to erode rock along a controlled trajectory, allowing precise, steerable borehole creation. Unlike traditional rotary drilling, it reduces mechanical wear on drill bits and enables navigation through complex geological formations, making it particularly useful for accessing hard-to-reach geothermal reservoirs or creating multiple branches from a single well.
tech_0403_directional-steel-shot-drillin.market_dynamics_summary_en: By improving drilling efficiency and reducing equipment energy use and downtime, directional steel-shot drilling can help expand geothermal energy production, supporting net zero emissions targets.
tech_0403_directional-steel-shot-drillin.description: Directional steel-shot drilling is a technique that uses high-velocity steel pellets to erode rock along a controlled trajectory, allowing precise, steerable borehole creation. Unlike traditional rotary drilling, it reduces mechanical wear on drill bits and enables navigation through complex geological formations, making it particularly useful for accessing hard-to-reach geothermal reservoirs or creating multiple branches from a single well.
tech_0403_directional-steel-shot-drillin.NZErationale: By improving drilling efficiency and reducing equipment energy use and downtime, directional steel-shot drilling can help expand geothermal energy production, supporting net zero emissions targets.

initiatives.description: Steel Shot Drilling Technology: Novel drilling method tested in Netherlands for more efficient, deeper wells

tech_0404_pulse-plasma-drilling.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術使用超高溫氣體電漿炬，是一種透過熔化或汽化岩石的熱鑽井形式，GA Drilling與Quaise正致力於此技術的商業化。
tech_0404_pulse-plasma-drilling.technology_status_summary_en: Using a plasma torch of gas at super-high temperatures, this technology is a form of a thermal drill through rock which will melt or vaporize rock. GA drilling and Quaise are working to commercialize this technology.
tech_0404_pulse-plasma-drilling.description: Using a plasma torch of gas at super-high temperatures, this technology is a form of a thermal drill through rock which will melt or vaporize rock. GA drilling and Quaise are working to commercialize this technology.

initiatives.description: Plasma drilling: GA Drilling and NREL collaborate to commercialise downhole generator technology for high-energy geothermal drilling applications

tech_0405_percussive-drilling.technology_status_summary_zh: 結合傳統旋轉鑽井與衝擊動作，是另一種正在測試的增強型鑽井方法。透過在鑽頭旋轉的同時「敲擊」岩石，此設計可透過更高效的鑽井提供更快的速率，其中一項商業化嘗試為Geovolve HAMMER。
tech_0405_percussive-drilling.technology_status_summary_en: Combines traditional rotary drilling with percussive action is another augmented drilling method undergoing testing. By "hammering" into the rock as the drill rotates, this design can provide faster rates through more efficient drilling. One commercialisation effort is the Geovolve HAMMER.
tech_0405_percussive-drilling.description: Combines traditional rotary drilling with percussive action is another augmented drilling method undergoing testing. By "hammering" into the rock as the drill rotates, this design can provide faster rates through more efficient drilling. One commercialisation effort is the Geovolve HAMMER.

tech_0406_thermal-shock-drilling.technology_status_summary_zh: 此方法透過快速加熱與冷卻岩石，對其施加「熱衝擊」，溫度變化會在岩石中產生裂縫，使其更容易鑽穿，熱衝擊會削弱岩石的結構完整性，讓穿透效率更高，此技術仍處於早期階段，測試深度僅達數十公尺。
tech_0406_thermal-shock-drilling.technology_status_summary_en: This method involves giving rocks "thermal shocks" by rapidly heating and cooling them. The temperature changes create cracks in the rocks, making them easier to drill. Thermal shocks weaken the structural integrity of the rock, allowing more efficient penetration. This technology is still in its early stages with tests down to a few dozen meters
tech_0406_thermal-shock-drilling.description: This method involves giving rocks "thermal shocks" by rapidly heating and cooling them. The temperature changes create cracks in the rocks, making them easier to drill. Thermal shocks weaken the structural integrity of the rock, allowing more efficient penetration. This technology is still in its early stages with tests down to a few dozen meters

initiatives.description: development of  “thermal-shock enhanced drill bit,” through computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations

tech_0407_high-pressure-water-jet-assist.technology_status_summary_zh: 高壓水射流輔助鑽井，例如徑向噴射鑽井（RJD），可在岩石中鑽出微孔，有效刺激地熱儲層，此方法使用高壓水射流將岩石切割成特定形狀，隨後可用流體動力衝擊錘更輕易地將其擊碎，此技術目前仍處於實驗室測試階段，明年可能部署現場測試以驗證其提升鑽井速率的效果。
tech_0407_high-pressure-water-jet-assist.technology_status_summary_en: High pressure water jet assissted drilling such as Radial Jet Drilling (RJD), which creates microholes through the rock, could effectively stimulate geothermal reservoirs. This method uses high-pressure water jets to cut rocks into specific shapes, which can then be broken apart more easily by fluid-powered percussive hammers. Still in the lab testing phase, field tests may be deployed next year to test how it can be used to improve drilling rates.
tech_0407_high-pressure-water-jet-assist.description: High pressure water jet assissted drilling such as Radial Jet Drilling (RJD), which creates microholes through the rock, could effectively stimulate geothermal reservoirs. This method uses high-pressure water jets to cut rocks into specific shapes, which can then be broken apart more easily by fluid-powered percussive hammers. Still in the lab testing phase, field tests may be deployed next year to test how it can be used to improve drilling rates.

initiatives.description: Applications of Radial Jet Drilling Techniques in Geothermal Wells

tech_0408_closed-loop-geothermal-systems.technology_status_summary_zh: 封閉迴路地熱系統涵蓋一系列新型、封閉或部分封閉迴路（混合式）發電技術試驗，這些試驗主要依賴岩石（不良導體）沿長井筒的熱傳導，且通常使用超臨界二氧化碳或其他新型工作流體。CLGS可應用於攝氏約100度至180度的低溫沉積資源，讓地熱能得以從有限的傳統地熱供應擴展至沉積層。
tech_0408_closed-loop-geothermal-systems.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 可利用低溫沉積熱源，大幅擴展全球地熱資源，並轉化油氣業的創新與專業經驗，此外也有助於封閉迴路或混合封閉迴路系統的發展，透過避免降低地下複雜度來簡化系統。
tech_0408_closed-loop-geothermal-systems.technology_status_summary_en: Closed-loop geothermal systems cover a range of new, closed or partially closed-loop (hybrid) technology trials for power generation. These trials largely rely on thermal conduction in rock (a poor conductor) along long wellbores and often rely on the use of supercritical CO2 or other new working fluids. CLGS allows for applications in low-temperature sedimentary resources with from about 100 °C to 180 °C. This allows expansion of geothermal from a limited conventional geothermal supply into sedimentary basins leading to a growth in geothermal supply of several magnitudes and locations. Closed loop systems also prevent interaction with potentially toxic and corrosive subsurface fluids. Some new approaches create a ''thermos"" type of well insert which retains heat better than interacing working fluid with the subsurface directly.
tech_0408_closed-loop-geothermal-systems.market_dynamics_summary_en: Enables the use of low-temperature, sedimentary heat sources allowing for significant extension for geothermal resources globally and translation of oil and gas innovation and expertise. Additionally, aids the growth of closed-loop or hybrid closed-loop systems. This reduces the complexity of subsurface interactions by avoiding the reduction of subsurface pressure and geochemical interactions and improves heat transfer efficiencies.
tech_0408_closed-loop-geothermal-systems.description: Closed-loop geothermal systems cover a range of new, closed or partially closed-loop (hybrid) technology trials for power generation. These trials largely rely on thermal conduction in rock (a poor conductor) along long wellbores and often rely on the use of supercritical CO2 or other new working fluids. CLGS allows for applications in low-temperature sedimentary resources with from about 100 °C to 180 °C. This allows expansion of geothermal from a limited conventional geothermal supply into sedimentary basins leading to a growth in geothermal supply of several magnitudes and locations. Closed loop systems also prevent interaction with potentially toxic and corrosive subsurface fluids. Some new approaches create a ''thermos"" type of well insert which retains heat better than interacing working fluid with the subsurface directly.
tech_0408_closed-loop-geothermal-systems.NZErationale: Enables the use of low-temperature, sedimentary heat sources allowing for significant extension for geothermal resources globally and translation of oil and gas innovation and expertise. Additionally, aids the growth of closed-loop or hybrid closed-loop systems. This reduces the complexity of subsurface interactions by avoiding the reduction of subsurface pressure and geochemical interactions and improves heat transfer efficiencies.

initiatives.description: XGS completed its first pilot project in late 2024 with a 100-meter-deep well in central Texas.For 3,000 hours, or 125 days, XGS continuously ran its closed-loop system
A test to demonstrate the operational functionality of the DualVac™ technology was successfully conducted in 2024 at the Ullrigg test facility at NORCE in Stavanger, Norway

tech_0409_enhanced-geothermal-systems.technology_status_summary_zh: 增強型或工程型地熱系統旨在利用地球熱能，適用於不存在或蒸汽/熱水不足、滲透率低的地區，EGS技術核心在於在熱岩中設計並創造大型熱交換區域，此製程涉及透過打開既有裂隙及/或創造新裂隙來增強滲透率，熱能透過將傳輸介質（通常為水）泵入井孔至熱裂岩層，再將加熱後的流體抽出取得。
tech_0409_enhanced-geothermal-systems.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 開啟利用更大比例熱能的機會，但未來成本仍存在不確定性。
tech_0409_enhanced-geothermal-systems.technology_status_summary_en: Enhanced or engineered geothermal systems aim at using the heat of the Earth where no or insufficient steam or hot water exists and where permeability is low. EGS technology is centred on engineering and creating large heat exchange areas in hot rock. The process involves enhancing permeability by opening pre-existing fractures and/or creating new fractures. Heat is extracted by pumping a transfer medium, typically water, down a borehole into the hot fractured rock and then pumping the heated fluid up another borehole to a power plant, from where it is pumped back down (recirculated) to repeat the cycle. There were significant developments in 2024, predominantly by Fervo and their PPA with Google, but Sage also made some progress with securing 100% offtake with Meta. In 2025, additional PPAs have been signed and FERVO making strives with reducing costs and contracting for expansions of power plants
tech_0409_enhanced-geothermal-systems.market_dynamics_summary_en: Opens the opportunity to use a much larger fraction of thermal energy, but there is uncertainty on future costs.
tech_0409_enhanced-geothermal-systems.description: Enhanced or engineered geothermal systems aim at using the heat of the Earth where no or insufficient steam or hot water exists and where permeability is low. EGS technology is centred on engineering and creating large heat exchange areas in hot rock. The process involves enhancing permeability by opening pre-existing fractures and/or creating new fractures. Heat is extracted by pumping a transfer medium, typically water, down a borehole into the hot fractured rock and then pumping the heated fluid up another borehole to a power plant, from where it is pumped back down (recirculated) to repeat the cycle. There were significant developments in 2024, predominantly by Fervo and their PPA with Google, but Sage also made some progress with securing 100% offtake with Meta. In 2025, additional PPAs have been signed and FERVO making strives with reducing costs and contracting for expansions of power plants
tech_0409_enhanced-geothermal-systems.NZErationale: Opens the opportunity to use a much larger fraction of thermal energy, but there is uncertainty on future costs.

initiatives.description: Fervo Energy’s EGS Rapid Drilling: Cut drill times by 79%; enabling cost reduction and scalability (TLR 7->8); Baker Hughes and Turboden selected to deliver infrastructure and turbins for up to 180 MW facilities
Geothermal 3.0 Ultra-deep Drilling: Approaches hot dry rock at up to 500°C, 7-10km depth; new drilling for industrial heat

tech_0410_superhot-supercritical-geother.technology_name_zh: 超高溫/超臨界地熱系統
tech_0410_superhot-supercritical-geother.technology_status_summary_zh: 超高溫岩石地熱（SHR）系統開發深層、高能量密度的高溫地熱資源，主要議題包括熱能萃取、SHR專用井設計與建造/材料，以及場址選擇。
tech_0410_superhot-supercritical-geother.technology_status_summary_en: Superhot rock geothermal (SHR) systems tap hot, deep, energy-dense geothermal resources. Main topics include heat extraction,  SHR specific well design and construction/materials , and site selection.
tech_0410_superhot-supercritical-geother.description: Superhot rock geothermal (SHR) systems tap hot, deep, energy-dense geothermal resources. Main topics include heat extraction,  SHR specific well design and construction/materials , and site selection.

initiatives.description:
Mazama Energy in Oregon - 331 C bottomhole temperature Peak drill penetration rates of 100 feet/hour
Average 76 feet/hour across diverse rock types: granite, basalt, and granodiorite
Record-breaking bit runs up to 2,760 feet through volcanic formations
Zero downhole failures of motors or measurement tools
Well integrity and cement stability at ultra-high temperatures

tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.technology_name: Conventional dry steam geothermal power plants
tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.technology_name_zh: 傳統乾蒸汽地熱發電廠
tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.technology_status_summary_zh: 乾蒸汽電廠目前約占地熱裝置容量的四分之一，直接利用由生產井以管線輸送至電廠、再送至渦輪機的乾蒸汽。
tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 成熟技術，但部署機會取決於當地潛力。
tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.technology_status_summary_en: Dry steam plants, which make up about a quarter of geothermal capacity today, directly utilise dry steam that is piped from production wells to the plant and then to the turbine.
tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.market_dynamics_summary_en: Mature technology, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.
tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.description: Dry steam plants, which make up about a quarter of geothermal capacity today, directly utilise dry steam that is piped from production wells to the plant and then to the turbine.
tech_0411_conventional-dry-steam-geother.NZErationale: Mature technology, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.

tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.technology_name: Flash steam geothermal power plant (hot water)
tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.technology_name_zh: 閃蒸蒸汽地熱發電廠（熱水型）
tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.technology_status_summary_zh: 閃蒸蒸汽電廠目前約占地熱裝置容量的三分之二，適用於溫度高於攝氏180度、以水為主的儲層，在這些高溫儲層中，液態水成分隨壓力下降而沸騰或「閃蒸」，分離出的蒸汽經管線送至渦輪機發電，剩餘熱水可能在逐漸降低的壓力與溫度下再閃蒸兩次（雙閃蒸電廠）或三次（三閃蒸），以獲得更多蒸汽。
tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 成熟技術，但部署機會取決於當地潛力。
tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.technology_status_summary_en: Flash steam plants, making up about two-thirds of geothermal installed capacity today, are used where water-dominated reservoirs have temperatures above 180°C. In these high-temperature reservoirs, the liquid water component boils, or “flashes,” as pressure drops. Separated steam is piped to a turbine to generate electricity and the remaining hot water may be flashed again twice (double flash plant) or three times (triple flash) at progressively lower pressures and temperatures, to obtain more steam.
tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.market_dynamics_summary_en: Mature technology, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.
tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.description: Flash steam plants, making up about two-thirds of geothermal installed capacity today, are used where water-dominated reservoirs have temperatures above 180°C. In these high-temperature reservoirs, the liquid water component boils, or “flashes,” as pressure drops. Separated steam is piped to a turbine to generate electricity and the remaining hot water may be flashed again twice (double flash plant) or three times (triple flash) at progressively lower pressures and temperatures, to obtain more steam.
tech_0412_flash-steam-geothermal-power-p.NZErationale: Mature technology, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.

tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.technology_name: Kalina cycle geothermal power plant (low temperature resources)
tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.technology_name_zh: 卡林那循環地熱發電廠（低溫資源）
tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.technology_status_summary_zh: 卡林那循環是利用低溫地熱資源的一種循環方案，運作溫度通常介於攝氏低至73度到180度之間，熱能透過熱交換器從地熱流體中回收，使低沸點工作流體（氨水混合物）汽化並驅動渦輪機。
tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 可利用低溫熱源，但部署機會取決於當地潛力。
tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.technology_status_summary_en: Kalina cycle is one cycle option to use low-temperature geothermal resources, typically operating with temperatures varying from as low as 73°C to 180°C. The heat is recovered from the geothermal fluid using heat exchangers to vaporise a working fluid with a low boiling point (ammonia-water mixture) and drive a turbine.
tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.market_dynamics_summary_en: Enables the use of low-temperature heat sources, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.
tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.description: Kalina cycle is one cycle option to use low-temperature geothermal resources, typically operating with temperatures varying from as low as 73°C to 180°C. The heat is recovered from the geothermal fluid using heat exchangers to vaporise a working fluid with a low boiling point (ammonia-water mixture) and drive a turbine.
tech_0413_kalina-cycle-geothermal-power-.NZErationale: Enables the use of low-temperature heat sources, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.

tech_0414_organic-rankine-cycle-low-temp.technology_name_zh: 有機朗肯循環（低溫資源）
tech_0414_organic-rankine-cycle-low-temp.technology_status_summary_zh: 有機朗肯循環（ORC）是利用低溫地熱資源的一種循環方案，運作溫度通常介於攝氏低至73度到180度之間，熱能透過熱交換器從地熱流體中回收，使低沸點工作流體（丁烷或戊烷）汽化並驅動渦輪機。
tech_0414_organic-rankine-cycle-low-temp.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 可利用低溫熱源，但部署機會取決於當地潛力。
tech_0414_organic-rankine-cycle-low-temp.technology_status_summary_en: Organic Rankine cycle (ORC) is one cycle option to use low-temperature geothermal resources, typically operating with temperatures varying from as low as 73°C to 180°C. The heat is recovered from the geothermal fluid using heat exchangers to vaporise a working fluid with a low boiling point (butane or pentane) and drive a turbine.
tech_0414_organic-rankine-cycle-low-temp.market_dynamics_summary_en: Enables the use of low-temperature heat sources, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.
tech_0414_organic-rankine-cycle-low-temp.description: Organic Rankine cycle (ORC) is one cycle option to use low-temperature geothermal resources, typically operating with temperatures varying from as low as 73°C to 180°C. The heat is recovered from the geothermal fluid using heat exchangers to vaporise a working fluid with a low boiling point (butane or pentane) and drive a turbine.
tech_0414_organic-rankine-cycle-low-temp.NZErationale: Enables the use of low-temperature heat sources, but deployment opportunities depend on local potential.

initiatives.description: Phase II of Cape Station: Turboden will deliver three 60 MWe ORC units (180 MW total) for the 2nd generation modular plant design. Expected to be commissioned around 2028.

tech_0415_working-fluids.technology_status_summary_zh: 工作流體是在地面設備中循環、傳遞從地熱儲層萃取熱能的液體或氣體，工作流體是用於在地熱系統地面設施中萃取、輸送與利用地熱的工程流體或天然流體，這些流體可能需要以特定方式處理，及/或以合成方式開發或調配，以確保渦輪機等地面設施平順持續運作。
tech_0415_working-fluids.technology_status_summary_en: working fluids are the liquids or gases that circulate through the surface equipment to transfer thermal energy extracted from the geothermal reservoir. Working fluids are the engineered or naturally occurring fluids used to extract, transport, and utilize geothermal heat within the surface facilities of a geothermal system. These may nee dto be handled in specific ways and or developed synthetically or blending to ensure smoothe continuous operationg of the surface facilities such as turbines.
tech_0415_working-fluids.description: working fluids are the liquids or gases that circulate through the surface equipment to transfer thermal energy extracted from the geothermal reservoir. Working fluids are the engineered or naturally occurring fluids used to extract, transport, and utilize geothermal heat within the surface facilities of a geothermal system. These may nee dto be handled in specific ways and or developed synthetically or blending to ensure smoothe continuous operationg of the surface facilities such as turbines.

initiatives.description: Ionic liquids as working fluids- Ls are being explored for EGS because of their high thermal stability, tunable properties (viscosity, heat capacity), and potentially lower risk of thermal short-circuiting (“fast paths”)

Nanonfluids as working fluids -  novel unsupervised deep neural network approach to predict the temperature and velocity behaviour of electro-magneto-hydrodynamics hybrid nanofluid flow for geothermal pipelines application
carbon quantum dots (CQDs) for working fluids. A 2025 study proposed adding CQDs to fluid in geothermal + hydrocarbon co-production systems to improve heat transfer

Quasi-Isothermal Heat Engine: Lava Power (formerly Luminescent) reports its liquid‑based, quasi‑isothermal heat engine is at TRL 6–7 (prototype demonstration in a relevant environment). (IEA TRL 4->6)
Turboden secured a contract with Vulcan Energy to build a 32 MWe geothermal plant near Landau, Germany, integrated into lithium extraction - aims to use the lithium brine as geothermal fluid.

tech_0416_concentrated-pv.technology_status_summary_zh: 聚光光伏（CPV）技術使用光學聚光系統，將太陽輻射聚焦於小型高效電池上，CPV模組效率可達40%以上。
tech_0416_concentrated-pv.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 相對較新的技術，僅於2000年代才進入市場；僅限於直接法向輻照度（DNI）高的地區。
tech_0416_concentrated-pv.technology_status_summary_en: Concentrated PV (CPV) technologies use an optical concentrator system which focuses solar radiation onto a small high-efficiency cell. CPV modules can achieve efficiencies of above 40%.
tech_0416_concentrated-pv.market_dynamics_summary_en: Relatively new technology, which entered the market only in the 2000s; limited to regions with high Direct Normal Irradiation (DNI).
tech_0416_concentrated-pv.description: Concentrated PV (CPV) technologies use an optical concentrator system which focuses solar radiation onto a small high-efficiency cell. CPV modules can achieve efficiencies of above 40%.
tech_0416_concentrated-pv.NZErationale: Relatively new technology, which entered the market only in the 2000s; limited to regions with high Direct Normal Irradiation (DNI).

initiatives.description: Cumulative installations (already grid-connected) in 2016: >370 MWp. Several power plants with capacity ≥ 30 MWp: Golmud, China, built by Suncore: 60 (2012) and 80 MWp (2013); Touwsrivier, South Africa, built by Soitec: 44 MWp (2014); Alamosa, Colorado, US, built by Amonix: 30 MWp (2012).

tech_0417_crystalline-silicon-pv.technology_status_summary_zh: 目前，絕大多數光伏模組基於晶圓式結晶矽（c-Si），c-Si模組的製造通常涉及矽錠成長、將矽錠切片成晶圓以製作太陽能電池、電池間的電氣互連，以及封裝電池串以形成模組。模組目前主要使用兩種形式之一的矽：單晶（sc-Si）或多晶（mc-Si）模組，目前商用單晶模組具有更高的轉換效率。
tech_0417_crystalline-silicon-pv.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 目前主導的太陽能光伏技術，在資源條件良好的地區已具競爭力。
tech_0417_crystalline-silicon-pv.technology_status_summary_en: Today, the vast majority of PV modules are based on wafer-based crystalline silicon (c-Si). The manufacturing of c-Si modules typically involves growing ingots of silicon, slicing the ingots into wafers to make solar cells, electrically interconnecting the cells, and encapsulating the strings of cells to form a module. Modules currently use silicon in one of two main forms: single- (sc-Si) or multi- (mc-Si) crystalline modules. Current commercial single-crystalline modules have a higher conversion efficiency of around 14 to 20%. Their efficiency is expected to increase up to 25% in the longer term. Multi-crystalline silicon modules have a more disordered atomic structure leading to lower efficiencies, but they are less expensive. Their efficiency is expected to increase up to 21% in the long term.
tech_0417_crystalline-silicon-pv.market_dynamics_summary_en: Dominating  solar PV technology today, already competitive today in regions with good resource conditions.
tech_0417_crystalline-silicon-pv.description: Today, the vast majority of PV modules are based on wafer-based crystalline silicon (c-Si). The manufacturing of c-Si modules typically involves growing ingots of silicon, slicing the ingots into wafers to make solar cells, electrically interconnecting the cells, and encapsulating the strings of cells to form a module. Modules currently use silicon in one of two main forms: single- (sc-Si) or multi- (mc-Si) crystalline modules. Current commercial single-crystalline modules have a higher conversion efficiency of around 14 to 20%. Their efficiency is expected to increase up to 25% in the longer term. Multi-crystalline silicon modules have a more disordered atomic structure leading to lower efficiencies, but they are less expensive. Their efficiency is expected to increase up to 21% in the long term.
tech_0417_crystalline-silicon-pv.NZErationale: Dominating  solar PV technology today, already competitive today in regions with good resource conditions.

initiatives.description: Crystalline silicon technology is the dominating technology today, with a share of around 95% in total PV cell production in 2021.

tech_0418_floating-solar-pv.technology_status_summary_zh: 浮動光伏系統安裝於漂浮於水面的結構上，可與既有電網連接搭配，例如水壩附近的情況。
tech_0418_floating-solar-pv.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 隨著土地日益成為稀缺資源，浮動光伏有望成為湖泊、水庫甚至海洋地區的替代方案。
tech_0418_floating-solar-pv.technology_status_summary_en: Floating PV systems are mounted on a structure that floats on a water surface and can be associated with existing grid connections, for instance in the case of dam vicinity.
tech_0418_floating-solar-pv.market_dynamics_summary_en: With land becoming a scarce resource, floating PV could become an alternative for areas with lakes, water reservoirs or even for the sea.
tech_0418_floating-solar-pv.description: Floating PV systems are mounted on a structure that floats on a water surface and can be associated with existing grid connections, for instance in the case of dam vicinity.
tech_0418_floating-solar-pv.NZErationale: With land becoming a scarce resource, floating PV could become an alternative for areas with lakes, water reservoirs or even for the sea.

initiatives.description: Starting from the world's first floating PV plant of 20 kWp in 2007 in Japan, the technology has rapidly developed in past years, with the largest plant being located in China and having a capacity of 320 MW. The total installed capacity stood at 3023 MWp in 2021.
State-owned China Energy Investment Corporation (CHN Energy) completed a 1GW floating solar PV facility in the Shandong Province of China in November 2024.
Vertical floating PV plant comes online in Germany

tech_0419_multi-junction-cell-pv.technology_status_summary_zh: 多接面電池設計涉及將多個電池疊層堆疊，兩個電池的情況下會形成雙接面，也稱為疊層電池，堆疊更多電池則形成三接面或四接面，在所有情況下，上層電池必須盡可能透明，讓下層電池仍能保持活性，此方法可捕捉更廣的太陽光譜，並提升整體效率，使用第三、五族元素的多接面電池成本較高。
tech_0419_multi-junction-cell-pv.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 已商業化，但目前市場滲透率相對較低，僅限於利基應用（如太空飛行）。
tech_0419_multi-junction-cell-pv.technology_status_summary_en: Multi-junction cell design involves superposing several cells in a stack. In the case of two cells, it will form a double junction, also called a tandem cell. Stacking more cells together forms a triple or a quadruple junction. In all cases, the upper cell(s) must be as transparent as possible to enable the lower cells to still be active. This approach enables a broader spectrum of sunlight to be captured, and overall efficiency to be increased. III-V multi-junction cells using elements in the III and V columns of the periodic table, such as gallium indium phosphate (GaInP), gallium indium arsenide (GaInAs), and gallium arsenide (GaAs), are often used for concentrated PV applications.
tech_0419_multi-junction-cell-pv.market_dynamics_summary_en: Commercially available, but has relatively low market penetration today, confined to niche applications (e.g. space flight).
tech_0419_multi-junction-cell-pv.description: Multi-junction cell design involves superposing several cells in a stack. In the case of two cells, it will form a double junction, also called a tandem cell. Stacking more cells together forms a triple or a quadruple junction. In all cases, the upper cell(s) must be as transparent as possible to enable the lower cells to still be active. This approach enables a broader spectrum of sunlight to be captured, and overall efficiency to be increased. III-V multi-junction cells using elements in the III and V columns of the periodic table, such as gallium indium phosphate (GaInP), gallium indium arsenide (GaInAs), and gallium arsenide (GaAs), are often used for concentrated PV applications.
tech_0419_multi-junction-cell-pv.NZErationale: Commercially available, but has relatively low market penetration today, confined to niche applications (e.g. space flight).

initiatives.description: Multi-junction PV modules are mainly used for satellite PV and concentrating PV (CPV) systems. Some tandem and multi-junction cells are commercially available. Companies manufacturing multi-junction solar cells include Canadian Solar, Wuxi Suntech Power, juwi AG, Trina Solar, JinkoSolar Holding, SHARP CORPORATION, AZUR SPACE Solar Power, Umicore, SolAero Technologies.
R&D activities for high-efficiency multi-junction and tandem solar cells PV are driven by the United States, Europe and Japan.

In 2024, The European Solar Test Installation (ESTI) has confirmed China-based Longi’s achievement of a world record-breaking efficiency rating of 34.6% for a perovskite-silicon (Si) tandem solar cell. Perovskite-Si hybrids are emerging as high-efficiency competitors to pure Si PV because they can capture more wavelength. Longi also got the record for Si-only cells in 2023 (27.1%).
In 2024, UK-based Oxford PV announced the first shipment of commercial-scale perovskite tandem solar panels produced in Germany to their US customers. The panels have 24.5% module efficiency.

tech_0420_organic-thin-film.technology_status_summary_zh: 有機薄膜光伏（OPV）電池使用染料或有機半導體作為集光活性層，此技術近年來日益受到關注與研究，目前是進展最快的太陽能技術，儘管生產成本低廉，但市場上尚無穩定產品，然而開發與示範活動正在進行中。
tech_0420_organic-thin-film.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 進展迅速的光伏技術，但儘管生產成本低廉，市場上尚無穩定產品可供應用。
tech_0420_organic-thin-film.technology_status_summary_en: Organic thin-film PV (OPV) cells use dye or organic semiconductors as the light-harvesting active layer. This technology has created increasing interest and research over the last few years and is currently the fastest-advancing solar technology. Despite the low production costs, stable products are not yet available for the market, nevertheless development and demonstration activities are underway.
tech_0420_organic-thin-film.market_dynamics_summary_en: Fast-advancing PV technology, but despite low production costs, no stable products available for the market yet.
tech_0420_organic-thin-film.description: Organic thin-film PV (OPV) cells use dye or organic semiconductors as the light-harvesting active layer. This technology has created increasing interest and research over the last few years and is currently the fastest-advancing solar technology. Despite the low production costs, stable products are not yet available for the market, nevertheless development and demonstration activities are underway.
tech_0420_organic-thin-film.NZErationale: Fast-advancing PV technology, but despite low production costs, no stable products available for the market yet.

initiatives.description: The German company Heliatek has been able to show demonstration systems on various surfaces (steel, concrete, glass) integrated into existing architecture or technical systems. According to the homepage, the largest system, with an output of 22.5 kWp, is installed on the roof of a school in France. With an area of 500 m², standard test conditions (1000 W/m²) yield an average system efficiency of 4.5%. The manufactured cells have an efficiency of 7-8% (laboratory cells > 13%).

CSIRO in Australia developed printable solar cells using an organic light absorber, prepared in ink form and deposited by commercial printing presses, as is one of the electrodes, by using silver-based ink. Efficiencies are much lower than silicon-based PV systems, but production costs are also expected to be much lower.

Chinese researchers have demonstrated organic solar cells with novel encapsulation and a protective interfacial layer for enhanced stability and efficiency. The best performing cell based on the new approach retained 94% of its initial power conversion efficiency after standards-based damp heat testing for 1,032 hours damp heat and across 200 thermal cycles.

tech_0421_perovskite-thin-film.technology_status_summary_zh: 一種非矽基薄膜光伏技術，使用鈣鈦礦——一種非常擅長吸收光線的礦物，在實驗室中已達到25%的效率，但目前僅限於小面積電池；達成類似效率的更大面積嘗試迄今尚未成功，鈣鈦礦太陽能電池目前仍存在耐久性不足的問題。
tech_0421_perovskite-thin-film.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 大面積電池的生產製程尚未開發完成，電池也存在耐久性不足的問題。
tech_0421_perovskite-thin-film.technology_status_summary_en: A non-silicon based thin-film PV technology, which uses Perovskite, a type of mineral very good at absorbing light. In the lab, efficiencies of 25% have been reached, but so far only with small cell areas; efforts to achieve similar efficiencies have not been successful so far.  Perovskite solar cells also still suffer from short durability.
tech_0421_perovskite-thin-film.market_dynamics_summary_en: Production process for larger cell areas not yet developed and cells suffer from short durability.
tech_0421_perovskite-thin-film.description: A non-silicon based thin-film PV technology, which uses Perovskite, a type of mineral very good at absorbing light. In the lab, efficiencies of 25% have been reached, but so far only with small cell areas; efforts to achieve similar efficiencies have not been successful so far.  Perovskite solar cells also still suffer from short durability.
tech_0421_perovskite-thin-film.NZErationale: Production process for larger cell areas not yet developed and cells suffer from short durability.

initiatives.description: In China, several companies are working on commercialization. Hangzhou Microquanta Semiconductor announced that it shipped 5 000 pieces of perovskite PV modules in July 2022. Shenzhen Infinite Solar Energy Technology successfully secured financing, targeting to establish a pilot production line in 2022. GCL Optoelectronics announced it installed perovskite PV modules in the facilities used for the Winter Olympic games in 2022. In addition to these Chinese companies, Wonder Solar started to establish a 200 MW/year pilot line aiming at developing a 1 GW factory in 2022. Outside of China, Saule Technologies (Poland) completed a manufacturing facility for flexible perovskite solar cells in May 2021. Oxford PV in the UK completed a 100 MW/year production line for perovskite/c-Si tandem PV in July 2021.

Scientists from Rice University in the United States announced in 2024 that they have analysed the impact of “seasoning” a formamidinium lead iodide solution with two-dimensional (2D) perovskites, and found that the template improved the efficiency and durability of their solar cells.

In 2024, Sekisui Chemical set up a new company (Sekisui Solar Film) that plans to invest USD 2 billion to produce 100 MW per year of perovskite cells by 2027 (with 3-4 times the cost of crystalline silicon PV), having found a way to prevent moisture ingress. They aim to produce 1 GW at 1 metre width cost-competitively by 2030.

Results were published in 2024 of the first demonstration of hybrid perovskite solar cell modules, comprising serially interconnected cells, produced entirely using industrial roll-to-roll printing tools under ambient room conditions.

The commercial M6 size wafer-level silicon-perovskite tandem solar cell independently certified by the authoritative certification institutions of the Fraunhofer Institute for Solar Energy (Fraunhofer ISE) in Germany achieved photovoltaic conversion efficiencies of 30.1%.

LONGi self-developed large-area (260.9 cm²) crystalline silicon-perovskite two-terminal tandem solar cell achieved a conversion efficiency of 33%, setting a new global efficiency record for large-area tandem cells.  Certified by NREL.

Fraunhofer ISE achieves 30.6% efficiency for perovskite-silicon tandem based on industry-standard bottom TOPCon cell, demonstrating that the TOPCon2 cell design and its lean process flow are compatible with perovskite/silicon tandem integration. this marks a key step towards cost-efficient industrial tandem solar cell production [Note: most others are with heterojunction (HJT) cells]

three remarkable results that pave the way for the industrialization of Tandem technology: 30% of efficiency for a 9 cm² cell with an industrial screen printing, 27.3% for a 100 cm² cell, and less than 1% loss after 9 months outdoor for our reference tandem cells.
Oxford PV is delivering its first commercial perovskite solar modules to US customers. The 72-cell solar modules have an efficiency of 24.5%
Qcells said it achieved cell efficiency of 28.6% on a large commercial-sized cell known as an M10 using the technology - setting the stage for future mass production.

(<30%) China is leading the way in mass production of perovskite solar cells. Startups there began mass production at the
100 MW (thousand kW) scale in 2023, and there are efforts to establish GW-scale (million kW) production systems
for large-area cells by the end of 2024

(<30%) UtmoLight says it has launched the world’s first gigawatt-scale perovskite solar module production line at a facility in Wuxi, China. The plant will annually produce 1.8 million panels, with a target of achieving 20% efficiency in mass production by 2025.
multiple companies with different manufacturing methods (e.g. roll-to-roll, solution-phase printing, vacuum deposition, solution coating) with MW-scale facilities for R&D and production

During the four-year project period, which will span from November 2022 to 2026, PEPPERONI aims to identify and address barriers to speed up technology’s mass manufacturing of high-performance PV modules.

a. Perovskite solar manufacturer, Yanhe Solar, has completed a Series A financing round exceeding RMB 100 million (US$14 million), with investors including Orient Jiafu and others.
b. In August 2025, the company completed and commissioned a 100 MW fully automated perovskite solar cell production line in Changde, Hunan Province, which it described as China’s first fully automated mass production line for consumer-grade perovskite solar cells.

tech_0422_microcrystalline-silicon-pv-ce.technology_status_summary_zh: 微晶矽（μc-Si）光伏電池是一種薄膜太陽能技術，使用具有混合結構的矽，即嵌入非晶基質中的小型結晶晶粒，相較結晶矽可實現更佳的光吸收，同時使用材料少得多，μc-Si電池通常與非晶矽以疊層方式結合以提升效率，成本相對較低，但效率低於傳統結晶矽模組。
tech_0422_microcrystalline-silicon-pv-ce.technology_status_summary_en: Microcrystalline silicon (μc-Si) PV cells are a type of thin-film solar technology that uses silicon with a mixed structure of small crystalline grains embedded in an amorphous matrix. This allows better light absorption than crystalline silicon while using far less material. μc-Si cells are often combined with amorphous silicon in tandem configurations to improve efficiency. They are relatively low-cost but have lower efficiencies compared with conventional crystalline silicon modules.
tech_0422_microcrystalline-silicon-pv-ce.description: Microcrystalline silicon (μc-Si) PV cells are a type of thin-film solar technology that uses silicon with a mixed structure of small crystalline grains embedded in an amorphous matrix. This allows better light absorption than crystalline silicon while using far less material. μc-Si cells are often combined with amorphous silicon in tandem configurations to improve efficiency. They are relatively low-cost but have lower efficiencies compared with conventional crystalline silicon modules.

tech_0423_chalcogenide-thin-film.technology_status_summary_zh: 由結合硫族元素（硫、硒或碲）與金屬的半導體材料製成的太陽能電池，常見範例包括CIGS（銅銦鎵硒）與碲化鎘（CdTe），這些材料具有強大的光吸收特性，可用極薄的層實現高性能，硫族化合物薄膜提供高效率與低製造成本，但面臨材料供應、毒性疑慮（鎘）與長期穩定性等挑戰。
tech_0423_chalcogenide-thin-film.technology_status_summary_en: Solar cells made from semiconductor materials that combine a chalcogen element (sulfur, selenium, or tellurium) with metals. Common examples include CIGS (copper indium gallium selenide) and CdTe (cadmium telluride). These materials have strong light-absorption properties, enabling high performance with very thin layers. Chalcogenide thin-films offer high efficiency and low manufacturing costs but face challenges related to material availability, toxicity concerns (Cd), and long-term stability.
tech_0423_chalcogenide-thin-film.description: Solar cells made from semiconductor materials that combine a chalcogen element (sulfur, selenium, or tellurium) with metals. Common examples include CIGS (copper indium gallium selenide) and CdTe (cadmium telluride). These materials have strong light-absorption properties, enabling high performance with very thin layers. Chalcogenide thin-films offer high efficiency and low manufacturing costs but face challenges related to material availability, toxicity concerns (Cd), and long-term stability.

tech_0424_space-solar-power.technology_status_summary_zh: 此技術涉及在衛星或太空平台上部署太陽能板，於地球大氣層外收集太陽能，該處陽光更強且不間斷，能量被轉化為微波或雷射，並以無線方式傳輸至地球上的接收站，太空太陽能理論上可提供穩定、大規模的再生能源，但面臨重大技術、經濟與法規挑戰，包括發射成本、電力傳輸效率與安全考量。
tech_0424_space-solar-power.technology_status_summary_en: It involves deploying solar panels on satellites or space-based platforms to collect solar energy outside Earth’s atmosphere, where sunlight is more intense and uninterrupted. The energy is converted into microwaves or lasers and transmitted wirelessly to receiving stations on Earth. SSP can theoretically provide constant, large-scale renewable energy but faces major technological, economic, and regulatory challenges, including launch costs, power-beaming efficiency, and safety considerations.
tech_0424_space-solar-power.description: It involves deploying solar panels on satellites or space-based platforms to collect solar energy outside Earth’s atmosphere, where sunlight is more intense and uninterrupted. The energy is converted into microwaves or lasers and transmitted wirelessly to receiving stations on Earth. SSP can theoretically provide constant, large-scale renewable energy but faces major technological, economic, and regulatory challenges, including launch costs, power-beaming efficiency, and safety considerations.

initiatives.description: In 2023, Caltech's Space Solar Power Demonstrator team confirmed that their orbiting solar power prototype successfully demonstrated its ability to wirelessly transmit power in space and to beam detectable power to Earth for the first time.
Space solar startup Aetherflux raises $50M to launch first space demo in 2026

tech_0425_linear-fresnel-reflector-solar.technology_name_zh: 線性菲涅耳反射鏡（太陽能）
tech_0425_linear-fresnel-reflector-solar.technology_status_summary_zh: 線性菲涅耳反射鏡（LFR）近似槽式系統的拋物線形狀，但使用成排的平面或略彎曲鏡面，將太陽光線反射至朝下的線性固定接收器，LFR系統的主要優勢在於其可彈性彎曲鏡面與固定接收器的簡單設計，所需投資成本較低，並便於直接產生蒸汽，因而省去傳熱流體與熱交換器的需求與成本，LFR電廠目前應用有限。
tech_0425_linear-fresnel-reflector-solar.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 相較拋物槽式與太陽塔式，投資成本可能較低，但熱儲存的整合也較困難。
tech_0425_linear-fresnel-reflector-solar.technology_status_summary_en: Linear Fresnel reflectors (LFRs) approximate the parabolic shape of trough systems but by using long rows of flat or slightly curved mirrors to reflect the sun’s rays onto a downward-facing linear, fixed receiver. The main advantage of LFR systems is that their simple design of flexibly bent mirrors and fixed receivers requires lower investment costs and facilitates direct steam generation, thereby eliminating the need for – and cost of – heat transfer fluids and heat exchangers. LFR plants are, however, less efficient than troughs in converting solar energy to electricity and it is more difficult to incorporate storage capacity into their design.
tech_0425_linear-fresnel-reflector-solar.market_dynamics_summary_en: Potentially lower investment costs than parabolic troughs and solar towers, but also more difficult integration of thermal storage.
tech_0425_linear-fresnel-reflector-solar.description: Linear Fresnel reflectors (LFRs) approximate the parabolic shape of trough systems but by using long rows of flat or slightly curved mirrors to reflect the sun’s rays onto a downward-facing linear, fixed receiver. The main advantage of LFR systems is that their simple design of flexibly bent mirrors and fixed receivers requires lower investment costs and facilitates direct steam generation, thereby eliminating the need for – and cost of – heat transfer fluids and heat exchangers. LFR plants are, however, less efficient than troughs in converting solar energy to electricity and it is more difficult to incorporate storage capacity into their design.
tech_0425_linear-fresnel-reflector-solar.NZErationale: Potentially lower investment costs than parabolic troughs and solar towers, but also more difficult integration of thermal storage.

initiatives.description: US-DOE SunShot 2030 targets: Baseload CSP plant (> 12h storage): USD 50/MWh
Linear fresnel reflectors accounted in 2022, with a capacity of 270 MW, for 4% of global solar thermal electricity capacity.

tech_0426_parabolic-trough-solar.technology_status_summary_zh: 拋物槽式系統由平行排列、單向彎曲以聚焦太陽光線的鏡面（反射鏡）組成，具選擇性塗層的不鏽鋼管（吸熱管）作為集熱器，並以真空玻璃套管隔熱，反射鏡與吸熱管隨太陽移動而同步轉動，合成油將熱能從集熱管傳遞至熱交換器，產生過熱蒸汽以驅動蒸汽渦輪機發電。
tech_0426_parabolic-trough-solar.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在具良好直接法向輻照度（DNI）資源的地區效益高，因太陽能熱電（STE）電廠搭配熱儲存可作為可調度發電來源，提供慣量等系統服務，並支持太陽能光伏的整合。
tech_0426_parabolic-trough-solar.technology_status_summary_en: Parabolic trough systems consist of parallel rows of mirrors (reflectors) curved in one dimension to focus the sun’s rays. Stainless steel pipes (absorber tubes) with a selective coating serve as the heat collectors and are insulated in an evacuated glass envelope. The reflectors and the absorber tubes move in tandem with the sun as it crosses the sky. A synthetic oil transfers the heat from the collector pipes to heat exchangers, producing superheated steam to run a steam turbine and produce electricity.
tech_0426_parabolic-trough-solar.market_dynamics_summary_en: High in regions with good Direct Normal Irradiation (DNI) resources, as solar thermal electricity (STE) plants in combination with thermal storage can be a dispatchable generation source, providing system services, e.g. inertia, and supporting the integration of solar PV
tech_0426_parabolic-trough-solar.description: Parabolic trough systems consist of parallel rows of mirrors (reflectors) curved in one dimension to focus the sun’s rays. Stainless steel pipes (absorber tubes) with a selective coating serve as the heat collectors and are insulated in an evacuated glass envelope. The reflectors and the absorber tubes move in tandem with the sun as it crosses the sky. A synthetic oil transfers the heat from the collector pipes to heat exchangers, producing superheated steam to run a steam turbine and produce electricity.
tech_0426_parabolic-trough-solar.NZErationale: High in regions with good Direct Normal Irradiation (DNI) resources, as solar thermal electricity (STE) plants in combination with thermal storage can be a dispatchable generation source, providing system services, e.g. inertia, and supporting the integration of solar PV

initiatives.description: Parabolic troughs accounted in 2022, with a capacity of 5560 MW, for 76% of global solar thermal electricity capacity.

tech_0427_solar-tower.technology_status_summary_zh: 太陽能塔，又稱中央接收系統（CRS），使用數百或數千面小型反射鏡（稱為定日鏡）將太陽光線聚焦於固定塔頂的中央接收器，塔式概念的聚光能力可達到極高溫度，藉此提升熱轉電效率，並降低熱儲存成本。
tech_0427_solar-tower.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 在具良好直接法向輻照度（DNI）資源的地區效益高，因太陽能熱電（STE）電廠搭配熱儲存可在日落後提供電力，並支持太陽能光伏的整合。
tech_0427_solar-tower.technology_status_summary_en: Solar towers, also known as central receiver systems (CRS), use hundreds or thousands of small reflectors (called heliostats) to concentrate the sun’s rays on a central receiver placed atop a fixed tower. The concentrating power of the tower concept achieves very high temperatures, thereby increasing the efficiency at which heat is converted into electricity and reducing the cost of thermal storage.
tech_0427_solar-tower.market_dynamics_summary_en: High in regions with good Direct Normal Irradiation (DNI) resources, as solar thermal electricity (STE) plants in combination with thermal storage can provide electricity after sunset and support the integration of solar PV.
tech_0427_solar-tower.description: Solar towers, also known as central receiver systems (CRS), use hundreds or thousands of small reflectors (called heliostats) to concentrate the sun’s rays on a central receiver placed atop a fixed tower. The concentrating power of the tower concept achieves very high temperatures, thereby increasing the efficiency at which heat is converted into electricity and reducing the cost of thermal storage.
tech_0427_solar-tower.NZErationale: High in regions with good Direct Normal Irradiation (DNI) resources, as solar thermal electricity (STE) plants in combination with thermal storage can provide electricity after sunset and support the integration of solar PV.

initiatives.description: Solar towers accounted in 2022 with a capacity of 1472 MW for 20% of global solar thermal electricity capacity.

tech_0428_solar-thermal-district-heating.technology_status_summary_zh: 太陽能區域供熱電廠採用大面積太陽能集熱器場，為區域供熱網絡供應或提升熱能，此技術高度模組化，因此可依空間條件應用於從街區到城市規模的區域供熱網絡，太陽能集熱器場可部署於地面，也可整合於建築屋頂，此技術通常僅能提供全部熱能需求的一部分，一般約占系統需求的10-50%。
tech_0428_solar-thermal-district-heating.market_dynamics_summary_zh: 太陽能區域供熱僅是區域供熱網絡的補充技術，但尤其能在利基市場中提供效益。
tech_0428_solar-thermal-district-heating.technology_status_summary_en: Solar district heating plants employ sizeable fields of solar thermal collectors to supply or upgrade the heat in district heating networks. The technology is highly modular, and can therefore be applied - subject to space - to district heating networks from block to city sizes. The solar collector fields can be deployed on the ground, but can also be integrated into building-roofs. The technology necessary provides only a share of all heat, which typically hovers around 10-50% of system needs. A key constraint is the space required for renewable energy such as solar thermal. In order to keep costs to a minimum, they need to be installed close to the heat consumers, where land availability is the most scarce.
tech_0428_solar-thermal-district-heating.market_dynamics_summary_en: Solar district heating is only a complementary technology to district heating networks, but can provide benefits in niche markets in particular.
tech_0428_solar-thermal-district-heating.description: Solar district heating plants employ sizeable fields of solar thermal collectors to supply or upgrade the heat in district heating networks.
